21c Verify INVOICES and forms properly prepared
22 Enter notation in Remarks that firearm(s) have been unloaded/rendered safe
23 Review COMPLAINT REPORT — verify address, perpetrator info, evidence section, weapon section
26 Forward firearms requiring lab analysis to Police Laboratory

Exam Alert: Firearms requiring lab analysis must be delivered to the patrol borough office by 0100 hours daily. Delay requires exigent circumstances AND permission from precinct CO/duty captain.

Exam Alert: Firearm alarm cancellation is made ONLY by the Stolen Property Inquiry Unit — recovering command does NOT cancel.

Exam Alert: Marking order and invoice listing order are different things. Order does NOT matter when physically marking firearm evidence (a firearm can be marked item 7). However, the order on the invoice is set by procedure — the firearm must always be listed as Item 1 on the invoice, regardless of marking order. (PG 218-23, Page 2 of 11, 3rd Paragraph)

Firearms Invoiced for Safekeeping

Delivered DIRECT to Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required): - Property of retired, resigned, dismissed, suspended, sick, or hospitalized members - Lawfully possessed property of a deceased person - Obtained from licensed dealer when license revoked/suspended/expired - Lawfully possessed and removed by court order/order of protection - Obtained from permit holder during renewal/expiration/cancellation

Exam Alert: Firearms invoiced for safekeeping get a separate voucher and are sent straight to Property Clerk UNLESS something needs examination. Do NOT put safekeeping guns on the same invoice as guns going to the Lab. (PG 218-23)

Tracing Firearms

Not every gun sent to the Firearms Analysis Section (FAS) is automatically traced.

  • If the MOS does NOT want the gun traced: write in block letters DO NOT TRACE in the Remarks section of both the PCI AND the Request for Lab Exam
  • If the Commanding Officer subsequently decides it SHOULD be traced: request can be made by phone to the Bureau of ATF
  • Routine Trace: Call BATF Regional Crime Gun Center 2 weeks after receipt by FAS
  • Emergency Trace: Call a supervisor at the Joint Firearms Task Force (JFTF); if unable to reach a supervisor at JFTF, call the Detective Bureau Wheel

Exam Alert: DO NOT TRACE must appear in block letters in the Remarks section of both the PCI and the Request for Lab Exam — not just one. (PG 218-23)

See Also: PG 218-04 — Delivery of evidence to the Police Lab (which firearms go / which do not).

DNA Evidence on Firearms

  • DO NOT package DNA evidence if wet — must be air-dried first
  • DO NOT use plastic bags/envelopes for DNA evidence — use paper bags/containers
  • Affix orange Biohazard label to each paper bag/container
  • If marking would contaminate DNA evidence, mark the container instead (print name in BLOCK letters)

Exam Alert: Firearm accessories NOT affixed to the firearm AND not needed for operation (holster, gun case, sling) are invoiced on a separate INVOICE from the firearm.

P.G. 218-31: Processing Computer Evidence

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To process and safeguard computer-related evidence coming into Department custody.

Key Definitions

Term Definition
CPU Box-shaped unit housing internal hard drive, memory, and central processor
Monitor Television-type display instrument
Keyboard Input device resembling a typewriter
Mouse Pointing device connected to CPU by cable
Printer Output device (dot matrix, ink-jet, laser)
Peripherals Scanners, tape drives, modems, speakers, personal digital assistants, pocket organizers, laptops

Notifications

Time Contact
Mon–Fri, 0600–2000 Computer Crimes Squad
All other times Office of the Chief of Detectives

Marking and Packaging

Step Action
3a Affix identifiable mark to hardware (monitor, CPU, printer, etc.)
3b Label items to indicate which peripherals were connected (Monitor 1, CPU 1, Keyboard 1) and tag all wires
3c Package similar floppy disks in paper envelopes — do NOT remove disks from drives; secure with masking tape

Exam Alert: DO NOT use Plastic Security Envelopes for floppy disks — static electricity can destroy data. DO NOT write on floppy disks with ballpoint pens — use labels or felt tip markers.

Critical Prohibitions

  • Members will NOT search hard drives, floppy disks, CD-ROMs, or data storage devices — Computer Crimes Squad ONLY
  • Searching may inadvertently alter or destroy evidence
  • Do not touch keyboard or execute programs at the scene
  • If modem attached, unplug telephone cord from wall jack immediately
  • To power off: unplug from wall outlet — do NOT use on/off switch; photograph monitor screen first
  • Do NOT transport in vehicle trunks with radio transceivers
  • Do NOT expose to devices emitting magnetic fields (portable radios, car speakers)

P.G. 218-33: Processing Sexual Offense Collection Kits

Date Effective: 06/19/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 42 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To ensure sexual offense evidence collection kits and other evidence are properly forwarded to the OCME Evidence Unit or Property Clerk.

Key Definitions

Term Definition
Sexual Offense Offenses enumerated in Article 130, NYS Penal Law
Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit Kit used by trained medical personnel in SAFE Program hospitals; contains slides, swabs, test tubes, envelopes
Drug Facilitated Sexual Assault Kit Separate kit with two gray-topped blood tubes and urine container; used when drugs/alcohol suspected
Sexual Offense Evidence Items containing bloodstains, serology, body fluids, biological evidence (clothing, bedding, undergarments) for DNA profiling

Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Member Ensure victim transferred to SAFE Program hospital
2 Member Take possession of kits and evidence upon completion of medical exam (with victim consent)
3 Member Ensure physician’s and complainant’s names entered on kit
4 Member Remove evidence to precinct of occurrence; prepare WORKSHEET
5–8 Desk Officer Direct preparation of INVOICE, REQUEST FOR LAB EXAM, COMPLAINT REPORT
9 Desk Officer Direct delivery of kit and COMPLAINT REPORT copy to Police Laboratory
10 Desk Officer Direct delivery of OTHER sexual offense evidence to Property Clerk (if kit was used at hospital)
11 Desk Officer If NO kit used at hospital, deliver other evidence to Police Laboratory
12 Desk Officer Comply with P.G. 218-49 for high priority cases
14 Desk Officer Immediately notify NYPD OCME Liaison Unit of all unfounded cases

Exam Alert: THE SEAL on a Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit SHALL NOT BE BROKEN by any member of the Department EXCEPT authorized personnel.

Exam Alert: Separate INVOICES required: one for the Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit, one for the Drug Facilitated Sexual Assault Kit, and one for other sexual offense evidence.

Exam Alert: A member of the service (male or female) is NOT required to be present during the medical examination. Chain of custody during examination is the sole responsibility of attending medical personnel.

Exam Alert: Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit should be used when the offense occurred within 120 hours or at the discretion of medical personnel.

P.G. 218-49: Immediate Delivery of DNA Evidence to Police Laboratory and OCME

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To provide a standard procedure for the immediate delivery of DNA evidence from the invoicing command direct to the Police Laboratory, and subsequently, immediately and directly to the OCME.

Delivery Chain

Command → Police Laboratory → OCME Evidence Unit

Detective Supervisor Responsibilities

Step Action
1 Determine if circumstances require immediate delivery based on: conferral with assigned investigator, CSU/ECT personnel, type of crime, nature of perpetrator, results of investigation
2 Confer with FIU OCME Liaison Unit personnel and Detective Borough DNA Coordinator
3 Confer with desk officer; request uniformed member for immediate delivery
4 Follow up to ensure DNA evidence was immediately delivered through the full chain

Police Laboratory Evidence Control Supervisor

Step Action
11 Inspect DNA evidence — verify proper packaging, sealing, INVOICE listing, forms
11f Confirm DNA evidence is probative and requires immediate delivery to OCME
12 Assign Police Laboratory Number to each INVOICE

OCME Evidence Unit

Step Action
21 Perform intake using FORMS Property
22 Confirm acceptance of PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT
23 Print five copies of PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT ACCEPTANCE DOCUMENT — three to messenger, two retained

Exam Alert: The delivering member must telephone the Police Laboratory Evidence Control Section after OCME acceptance and provide the OCME Evidence Control Number for each INVOICE.

Sergeant Focus: Detective supervisors must ensure ECMS entry documenting date/time of delivery, Police Laboratory Number, and OCME Evidence Control Number for all DNA evidence.

P.G. 218-51: Suspect Evidence Collection Kits

Date Effective: 06/19/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 42 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To ensure Suspect Evidence Collection Kits are properly collected and processed.

Definition

SUSPECT EVIDENCE COLLECTION KIT — A kit used by medical personnel in hospitals to gather probative forensic evidence from a suspect or arrestee.

Key Procedure Steps

Step Role Action
1 Assigned Investigator Determine if suspect has probative forensic evidence on person/clothing
2 Assigned Investigator Take measures to protect forensic evidence from alteration, loss, contamination
3 Assigned Investigator Obtain voluntary consent (CONSENT TO SUSPECT EVIDENCE COLLECTION KIT, PD321-031)
3c Assigned Investigator If no consent, contact Legal Bureau for possible court order through DA’s Office
4 Assigned Investigator Escort suspect to a designated hospital — DO NOT use same hospital as victim
5 Assigned Investigator Alert HHC hospital and SART before bringing suspect
7 Assigned Investigator Ensure investigator of same sex as suspect remains during examination
9 Assigned Investigator DO NOT collect separate DNA Exemplar Kit when exemplar already collected as part of Suspect Evidence Collection Kit
14 Assigned Investigator Prepare SEPARATE INVOICE for each Suspect Evidence Collection Kit — do NOT invoice other evidence on same INVOICE

Exam Alert: Neither consent nor a court order is necessary to invoice a suspect’s clothing (including underwear) or document/photograph a suspect’s physical features (bite marks, tattoos, etc.).

Sergeant Focus: Detective supervisors must ensure Biohazard Labels are properly utilized, review each INVOICE and REQUEST, and ensure ECMS entries documenting delivery are prepared.

P.G. 218-53: Processing Fireworks Evidence

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 5

Purpose

To establish a standard procedure to invoice fireworks coming into custody.

Key Definitions

Term Definition
Fireworks Evidence Bags, boxes, and/or loose items of fireworks
Heavy Duty Bag (For Fireworks) Specific Department-issued clear vinyl bag for collection/storage of loose fireworks

Invoicing Procedure

Step Action
1 Take two sets of photographs (one for ADA, one for arrest evidence)
3 Create INVOICE for photographs as arrest evidence
4 Create separate INVOICE for fireworks evidence
7 Place all loose fireworks into Department-issued Heavy Duty Bags; close with specific seals
8 Ensure bag is not overfilled
9 Separate party snaps, cracker balls, torpedoes from other fireworks (chain reaction risk)
10 Do NOT reopen bags once sealed
11–13 Enter quantity of sealed bags/boxes — do NOT individually count fireworks
14 Attach photocopy of INVOICE to each sealed bag/box (minimum 10 for large seizures)

Exam Alert: Impact exploders (snaps, cracker balls, etc.) must be separated from other fireworks because they could cause a chain reaction detonation. (PG 218-53)

Exam Alert: Two separate PCIs are always prepared for a fireworks arrest — one for the photographs (arrest evidence) and one for the fireworks. Both vouchers include the 61#, OLBS#, and charge/offense. (PG 218-53)

Exam Alert: For a large seizure of sealed boxes, provide overall photographs of the boxed fireworks, open one box, remove and display contents and photograph, then repack and seal with Evidence Tape. (PG 218-53)

Desk Officer — Delivery to Bomb Squad

Step Action
19 Contact Bomb Squad Range (Rodman’s Neck) Mon–Fri, 0700–1500
19a Contact Bomb Squad Office at all other times
22 Create PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT using FORMS Property
23 Make Command Log entry (INVOICE numbers, delivering member, vehicle numbers)

Exam Alert: Call Bomb Squad from the scene in three situations: (1) Large Seizure, (2) Unusual Problem, (3) Question regarding Safety. (PG 218-53)

Exam Alert: The Command Log entry for fireworks delivery includes three numbers: PCI #s, Name & shield # of MOS delivering, and RMP #. (PG 218-53)

Exam Alert: The MOS delivering fireworks to the Bomb Squad returns to command with two items: (1) the PTR Acceptance Document with assigned Bomb Squad case # on it, AND (2) a copy of the Bomb Squad Fireworks Supporting Deposition. The D.O. files the deposition with the PCI in the A/Os Arrest Folder. (PG 218-53)

Transportation Safety Rules

Rule Details
No smoking Within 50 feet of any fireworks
RMP transport Maximum 4 bags in rear seat
Passenger van Maximum 4 bags per seat on rear bench
Cargo van Maximum 25 sealed bags/boxes
Larger seizures (>25) Closed-body truck with separate cab and wood cargo floor; secure load
Windows Open minimum 2 inches to avoid toxic fumes
Trunk storage Prohibited
After dark No unloading at Bomb Squad range
Significant amount Notify FDNY through 311 for fire protection escort

Exam Alert: ONLY Department-issued Heavy Duty Bags (For Fireworks) may be used — peddler, garbage, or recycling bags are strictly prohibited. (PG 218-53)

Exam Alert: Large seizure = more than 25 bags or boxes — these must be transported in a closed-body truck with a separate cab and wood cargo floor. Load must be secured to prevent shifting (no open rack or barrier trucks). Large seizures are NOT unloaded at the Range after dark. The final decision to unload or not rests with the Bomb Squad. (PG 218-53)

Exam Alert: Large amounts of fireworks are NOT stored in a dept. facility — transport to range ASAP or as directed. Small amounts can be safely stored prior to transport but must be kept away from radiators/heaters. (PG 218-53)

See Also: PG 214-33 — Transport of animals; fireworks are transported in rear seat of RMP (NOT trunk), similar rule to transporting a dog in a patrol vehicle.

P.G. 218-54: Preparation of Request for Laboratory Examination Report (PD521-168)

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To provide guidelines for proper preparation of the REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT using FORMS Property.

Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Invoicing Officer Select YES to Is a Request for Laboratory Examination required? when preparing INVOICE
2 Invoicing Officer Select evidence type: DNA, Firearms/Firearms Related, Criminalistics, or Other
3 Invoicing Officer Ensure DNA ELIMINATION SAMPLES section is completed
4 Invoicing Officer Select and add all Analysis Exam Numbers for each item on INVOICE
5 Invoicing Officer Digitally sign REQUEST verifying accuracy and completeness
6 Desk Officer Digitally sign REQUEST verifying accuracy and completeness

Exam Alert: A REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT must be prepared for each INVOICE that lists evidence submitted to the laboratory for analysis.

Section 218 — Found Property, Decedent’s Property, and Currency

P.G. 218-26: Processing Found Property

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To record and process found property.

Scope

Found property valued at $10 or more must be reported to and deposited with police.

Special Locations

Location Rule
NYC Subway System Deliver to nearest NYC Transit Station Agent for forwarding to NYC Transit Lost Property Office (except U.S. currency, contraband, or property requiring investigation)
Yellow Medallion Taxis Delivered by taxi driver to one of 8 designated commands (one per patrol borough)

Designated Commands for Taxi Found Property

Borough Command
PBMS 017 Precinct
PBMN Central Park Precinct
PBBX 043 Precinct
PBBS 076 Precinct
PBBN 094 Precinct
PBQS 107 Precinct
PBQN 115 Precinct
PBSI 120 Precinct

Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Member Issue receipt to person delivering property (INVOICE copy at command; ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF FOUND PROPERTY, PD542-012, on patrol)
2 Member Deliver subway found property to nearest NYC Transit Station Agent
5 Member Prepare WORKSHEET
9 Desk Officer Digitally sign INVOICE
11 Desk Officer Have REPORT OF UNCLAIMED PROPERTY (PD542-122) prepared and forwarded
12–17 Desk Officer If returning to owner: require valid ID, check against INVOICE, complete FORMS release

Exam Alert: Destroy all copies of REPORT OF UNCLAIMED PROPERTY if property is claimed before distribution. If claimed after distribution, forward first copy with disposition to Stolen Property Inquiry Unit.

P.G. 218-27: Found Property Retained by Interstate Transportation Company, Airline, Bank or Safe Deposit Company

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Key Points

  • Desk Officer: direct preparation of two copies of REPORT OF UNCLAIMED PROPERTY (PD542-1272)
  • Forward original to Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
  • CO: direct investigation if notice of return not received within 60 days

P.G. 218-28: Safeguarding Property of Deceased Person

Date Effective: 02/03/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 8 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To safeguard property of a deceased person who lived alone, with other than a relative/competent person, or who dies at other than their residence.

Co-Occupant Verification

A member determines if someone is a co-occupant (not visitor/guest) by: 1. Examining documents (licenses, credit cards, mail) reflecting permanent residence 2. Questioning other residents/neighbors 3. Examining closets/bureaus for clothing and possessions 4. Examining rent receipts or similar documentation 5. Considering any other relevant factors

Co-Occupant vs. Visitor/Guest Determination

Examine documents reflecting address (license, mail, credit cards, rent receipts), question neighbors, check closets for clothes, consider other relevant factors.

Patrol Supervisor Responsibilities

Step Action
2 Confer with Medical Examiner BEFORE directing body search
3 Obtain at least one responsible civilian witness when possible
5 Supervise complete search of body
6 Have all property removed from body EXCEPT clothing actually worn
7 Have clothing/evidence processed on separate INVOICE
8 Direct search of residence — take custody of: cash, jewelry, valuables, IDs, wills, insurance policies, bank books, safe deposit keys, prescription medication (if directed to OCME)
9 Assign member to safeguard body until removed by OCME

Exam Alert: Any uniformed member of the service may search a dead human body. However, the Patrol Supervisor must confer with the ME or assistant ME PRIOR to directing a member to search the body. (PG 218-28)

Exam Alert: DO NOT seal door of premises if a co-occupant resided with the deceased. (PG 218-28)

Exam Alert: If multiple dead human bodies as a result of a mass casualty attack (e.g., active shooter), do NOT search bodies for identification. (PG 218-28)

Prescription Medication — Critical Rules

  • If the ME is taking the body: take possession of prescription meds in the immediate vicinity
  • Separate voucher for prescription meds (whether evidence or not) from other property
  • If the deceased lived alone: invoice meds NOT in a prescription container (e.g., daily pill box)
  • If the deceased did not live alone: take meds not in a prescription container that the co-occupant cannot prove are theirs

Exam Alert: Prescription meds always get a separate voucher from other property. If the person lived alone, you invoice meds found in a daily pill box or other non-prescription container. If they did NOT live alone, you only invoice meds in non-prescription containers that the co-occupant cannot prove belong to them. (PG 218-28)

Navigable Waters

Harbor searches the body removed from navigable waters and gives the property to a precinct MOS.

Police Lock

If a police lock is used to secure the premises, include the key on the PCI worksheet.

Securing Premises After DOA

The PG states the Desk Officer will have the SEAL FOR DOOR OF DOA PREMISES prepared — it does NOT say if appropriate (unlike what some may expect). After the body is removed (if DOA lived alone), have the seal pasted across door and doorjamb.

  • If someone needs to enter sealed premises for burial clothes: allow entry accompanied by a UMOS; reseal with a NEW seal afterward.

Public Administrator

  • If no next of kin OR next of kin cannot be notified: request Operations to notify Public Administrator
  • When Public Administrator’s representative arrives and property is NOT yet sent to Property Clerk: ask for identification and photocopy their ID
  • Prior to breaking the seal or opening envelope: have the Public Administrator examine the lead seal/PSE and check the number against the PCI

Surrogate Court Referral

Deceased’s Status Referral
NY State resident Surrogate Court of deceased’s resident county
Non-NY State resident Surrogate Court of borough where property is located

Key Steps — Desk Officer

Step Action
19 Request Operations Unit to notify Public Administrator if no known next of kin
20 Have SEAL FOR DOOR OF D.O.A. PREMISES (PD517-120) prepared
26 Permit entry to obtain clothing for burial — direct member to accompany and replace seal
34 Refer person seeking release to Surrogate’s Court of county where deceased lived

P.G. 218-29: Invoicing Property Removed from Parolee

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To invoice property removed from a parolee by a DOCCS officer in non-arrest situations.

Key Points

  • Desk officer: ascertain if property obtained within precinct confines (otherwise direct to correct precinct)
  • Have parole officer prepare the WORKSHEET
  • Member of service enters into FORMS Property to generate INVOICE
  • Give Invoicing Officer copy to parole officer

P.G. 218-30: Invoicing Property Taken from a Person’s Possession

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To ensure persons from whom property has been taken are afforded notice as to reclaiming steps.

Definition

CONTRABAND — Property whose mere possession is prohibited by law. Property is NOT contraband merely because it is suspected to be unlawfully obtained, stolen, or proceeds/instrumentality of a crime.

Key Procedure Steps

Step Action
1 Prepare WORKSHEET — DO NOT enter complainant information initially
2 Allow person to examine WORKSHEET for accuracy
3 THEN enter complainant information
4 Inform person they are entitled to a copy of INVOICE; ask if they will accept
5 Inform person they may note additional/erroneous items in Remarks
7 Give Prisoner/Finder/Owner copy REGARDLESS of whether property is denominated Arrest Evidence
8 Provide NOTICE TO PERSONS FROM WHOM PROPERTY HAS BEEN REMOVED (PD521-124)
9 Provide VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM if vehicle involved

Exam Alert: The Prisoner/Finder/Owner copy MUST be given to the arrestee prior to transporting to court section.

Exam Alert: Noncompliance with this procedure could place the Department in contempt of court. These provisions result from statute and court order.

P.G. 218-34: Property of the Division of the Lottery

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 1

Key Points

  • Lottery property includes: tickets, deposit boxes, licenses, lottery proceeds
  • Invoice for Safekeeping
  • Notify NYS Tax Office (at 0900 next business day if off-hours)
  • DO NOT forward to Property Clerk Division — safeguard at command until picked up by Lottery representative

P.G. 218-35: Processing Currency Required as Evidence

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To record, process and safeguard currency required as evidence.

Definition

NUMISMATIC/SENTIMENTAL VALUE — All gold coins, all U.S. silver coins dated 1964 or prior, and extremely old bills. If doubt exists, currency will be deemed to have numismatic/sentimental value. Currency with numismatic/sentimental value will NOT be deposited in a bank.

Evidence Stamp

Currency is stamped Evidence Notify N.Y.C. Police Department Property Clerk Division EXCEPT: - Currency with numismatic/sentimental value - Currency that must remain unaltered (treated with dye, fluorescent powder, or marked) - Foreign currency - Blood-stained currency

WORKSHEET Listing

Type How Listed
Stamped bills Each denomination as separate item with quantity
Unstamped bills Each denomination AND serial number as separate item

Exam Alert: Currency will be perforated ONLY by Property Clerk personnel at Property Clerk facilities.

Exam Alert: Stamped or perforated currency will NEVER be returned to a claimant or deposited in a bank. Forward to Property Clerk for claimant reimbursement.

Exam Alert: Mutilated currency (fluorescent powder, dye, blood) placed in Plastic Security Envelope marked MUTILATED CURRENCY — CONTAINS STAINED MATERIAL — USE CARE IN HANDLING.

P.G. 218-36: Processing Non-Evidence Currency

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To process currency not required as evidence (e.g., DOA property, found property).

Key Procedure — Bank Deposit

Step Role Action
6 Desk Officer Prepare and sign deposit slip
7 Desk Officer Direct officer to place currency in plastic deposit bag
8 Desk Officer Seal bag (remove protective strip, expose adhesive)
14 Desk Officer Direct command messenger + another member to deliver to bank’s night deposit vault at all times
16 Desk Officer Assign second platoon member to retrieve deposit slip next business day
22 Desk Officer Conduct Deposit Slip Confirmation using FORMS Property
23 Desk Officer Notify IAB Command Center if discrepancy is due to possible misconduct

Numismatic/Sentimental Value Currency

  • DO NOT enter monetary value in Cash Value column
  • Itemize coins by quantity
  • Place in Plastic Security Envelope
  • Deliver to Property Clerk — DO NOT deposit in bank

Exam Alert: Seizures of more than 2,000 bills require notification to Property Clerk Division for special counting and deposit procedures.

Exam Alert: All deposits are made on a subject to count basis. Bank discrepancies trigger notification from Property Clerk Division to IAB.

P.G. 218-37: Handling of Red Dye Marked Currency and Intact Exploding Currency Packets

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Intact Exploding Packet Found

Step Action
1 DO NOT handle the packet
2 Evacuate the immediate area
3 Request patrol supervisor, ESU, and Bomb Squad

Handling Red Dye Currency

Step Action
4 Wear disposable gloves
5 Work in a ventilated area
6 Wash hands thoroughly after handling
7 Indicate on INVOICE and Plastic Security Envelope: may be contaminated with CS Tear Gas

Exam Alert: Do NOT open envelopes containing contaminated currency unless absolutely necessary — sealed plastic envelopes prevent dissipation of contaminated residue. Precautions apply even after long periods of time.

P.G. 218-38: Processing Currency/Negotiable Instruments for Forfeiture

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 5

Purpose

To expeditiously process seized U.S. currency and/or negotiable instruments valued at $1,000 or more which may be forfeitable.

Key Definitions

Term Definition
Forfeitable Property used to facilitate a crime OR represents proceeds/substituted proceeds of a crime
Negotiable Instrument Cash equivalents: money orders, traveler’s checks, bank checks
Substituted Proceeds Items received in exchange for actual proceeds of a crime

Key Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Member Notify Asset Forfeiture Unit 24/7; request Forfeiture Log Number
2 Member Prepare SEPARATE WORKSHEET for forfeiture currency/instruments only
3 Member Select FORFEITURE as property category
7 Member DO NOT Evidence stamp forfeiture currency
11 Desk Officer Take digital photograph of currency seized per search warrant before bagging
14 Desk Officer Direct deposit in bank’s night deposit vault (two members)

Asset Forfeiture Unit Responsibilities

  • Determine if seizure is appropriate for forfeiture
  • Confer with DA’s Office, Civil Enforcement Unit
  • In federal cases: request adoption within 30 days
  • Property Clerk: issue check within 3 business days upon written request for federal/state forfeiture

Exam Alert: Seizures under $1,000 do NOT require Asset Forfeiture Unit notification — no Forfeiture Log Number issued.

Exam Alert: Assets will NEVER be transferred to any NYPD unit or outside agency for forfeiture processing without written permission of the CO, Asset Forfeiture Unit.

Section 218 — Invoicing Property: General Procedures

P.G. 218-01: Invoicing Property — General Procedure

Date Effective: 06/26/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 53 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To record and process property coming into police custody.

Key Definitions

Term Definition
Arrest Evidence Property seized for court presentation
DNA Arrest Evidence Property seized for court presentation containing potentially probative DNA evidence
Investigatory Property seized for investigation where a COMPLAINT REPORT and no ONLINE BOOKING SYSTEM ARREST WORKSHEET has been prepared
DNA Investigatory Investigatory property containing potentially probative DNA evidence
Forfeiture Arrest/investigatory evidence seized when probable cause existed to believe property was used to facilitate a crime or represents proceeds/substituted proceeds of a crime
Decedent’s Property Personal property of decedent
Found Property Non-contraband property found by a member of the service or civilian
Peddler’s Property Items (e.g., stands, carts) removed from vendors
Amnesty Firearm surrendered via Cash for Guns Program
Safekeeping Property that is to be returned to owner
Determine True Owner Property taken into custody where true owner cannot be determined and immediate arrest is not warranted
Photo Release Recovered stolen vehicles where arrest was made and vehicle not used in any other crime
Rotation Tow Non-evidence stolen or apparently abandoned vehicles including motorcycles recovered within NYC

Property Types

Firearms, Controlled Substances, Currency, Jewelry, Vehicles/Boats, General Property, Evidence Collection Kits/Swabs, Explosives.

Procedure

Uniformed Member of the Service (Steps 1-8)

Step Action
1 Enter circumstances and description of property in digital Activity Log
2 Notify desk officer
2a If vehicle unable to be driven, inform desk officer to request authorized tow/rotation tow
3 Prepare PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET (PD521-141A)
3a Comply with FORMS Property instructions
4 Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit to cancel or transmit alarm
5 Notify owner, if known
6 Cancel alarm for stolen vehicle ONLY after owner takes possession or vehicle removed to Property Clerk/rotation tow facility
7 Enter notifications and inquiry results on WORKSHEET
8 Deliver property and completed WORKSHEET to desk officer of command of record

Desk Officer (Steps 9-10)

Step Action
9 Review WORKSHEET for accuracy
10 Submit approved WORKSHEET to command clerk for entry into FORMS Property

Command Clerk (Steps 11-12)

Step Action
11 Enter WORKSHEET into FORMS Property to generate PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE (PD521-141)
12 Record time and name of FINEST operator who transmitted alarm cancellation on appropriate INVOICE

Uniformed Member (Steps 13-16)

Step Action
13 Ensure all property is properly tagged, packaged or sealed, and clearly identified by INVOICE number and barcode
14 Ensure every bag/security envelope or item has a FORMS Property bar-coded label affixed to upper right hand corner
14a Affix bar-coded label to upper right hand corner of windshield for automobiles/boats
15 Digitally sign INVOICE verifying accuracy and completeness
16 Complete VEHICLE REPORT in FORMS if vehicle, bicycle, moped, scooter or e-bike is seized as result of vehicle stop

Desk Officer (Steps 17-21)

Step Action
17 Review and digitally sign INVOICE
18 Photocopy INVOICE and forward to CO, Firearms and Tactics Section if item is a bullet resistant garment
19 Ensure property is properly stored and location entered into FORMS Property
20 Safeguard property until properly disposed of
21 Telephone NYS Tax Office for pickup of property owned by Division of Lottery — DO NOT send to Property Clerk

Additional Data — Separate Invoices Required

A separate PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE will be prepared for each of:

  • Different property types
  • Different property categories
  • Different owners
  • Property delivered to different locations (Police Laboratory, Property Clerk, Bomb Squad, OCME, etc.)

Exam Alert: When items will be sent to Police Laboratory, the INVOICE will not exceed twenty line items.

Exam Alert: Do NOT use terms pairs or sets when invoicing — enter the actual number of items in the quantity box.

Exam Alert: When a vehicle has obvious fire damage, notify FDNY borough dispatcher but do NOT prepare a COMPLAINT REPORT — a Fire Marshal will determine if arson occurred.

P.G. 218-02: Return of Property/Vehicles at Command and Processing Voided Property Invoices

Date Effective: 06/02/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 37 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To return all property listed on an invoice to legal claimant at a command and process voided PROPERTY CLERK INVOICES.

Procedure — Return of Property

Desk Officer (Steps 1-7)

Step Action
1 Require claimant to produce valid identification, preferably photo ID
1a Upload copy of claimant’s ID digitally to INVOICE using Add Remarks/Document function
2 Check property/vehicle against INVOICE
3 Request claimant examine security seal and check against number on INVOICE prior to breaking seal
4 Complete Release/Disposition — Return To Owner function using FORMS Property
4a Have claimant sign using digital signature capture device
5 Ensure alarm has been canceled
6 Approve delivery of property utilizing username and password
7 Release property/vehicle to claimant

Exam Alert: When no physical photo ID is available, a photograph of the claimant will be taken with a Department smartphone and digitally attached to the INVOICE.

Investigatory Evidence Return

Step Action
9 Conduct investigation to determine if property must be retained
9a Confer with appropriate investigative unit
9b Authorize release no more than ten days after receipt of request, if no longer required as evidence
10 Inform claimant property may not be released if still required for specific criminal proceeding or active investigation
11 Designate member to prepare OFFICIAL LETTERHEAD with INVOICE number(s) and reasoning for denial

Exam Alert: Investigatory evidence: desk officer must authorize release or denial within 10 days. If denied, claimant may request CO/designee of investigative unit to review within 5 days.

Voiding Invoices

Step Action
14 Use Void Invoice function if INVOICE has NOT been approved by a supervisor and cannot be corrected
15–16 If INVOICE has been approved by supervisor: create NEW invoice, then void the old one with cross-references

Exam Alert: Once approved by a supervisor, an INVOICE can only be voided by a supervisor assigned to the command where property is physically located.

Exam Alert: Filed INVOICES maintained for six years — each July, previous year’s invoices boxed and forwarded to Property Clerk warehouse.

P.G. 218-03: Delivery of Property Other Than Vehicle/Boat to Property Clerk

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To forward invoiced property to Property Clerk.

Key Points

  • Desk officer creates PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT (PD521-1412) using FORMS Property
  • Command messenger compares INVOICES against property for completeness
  • If discrepancies exist: immediate investigation, notify CO/duty captain and Internal Affairs Bureau (IAB) Command Center
  • IAB Log Number entered in Remarks section of INVOICE
  • Property Clerk member confirms acceptance in FORMS Property
  • Integrity Control Officer verifies all property not forwarded is accounted for and secured

Sergeant Focus: Any discrepancy in property against INVOICE requires immediate IAB notification — this is a critical integrity safeguard.

P.G. 218-05: Recording of Arrest and Property Clerk Invoice Numbers on Arrest Related Reports

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To determine disposition of prisoners and allow processing/disposing of property invoiced as arrest evidence.

Key Points

  • Invoicing member must enter INVOICE number on ON-LINE BOOKING SYSTEM ARREST WORKSHEET (PD244-159)
  • Prepare REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT (PD521-168) if necessary
  • All defendants’ names must be listed on the REQUEST — if not, evidence could be destroyed before accomplices/co-defendants are tried, resulting in dismissed charges

Exam Alert: Failure to list all defendant information on the REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT could lead to premature destruction of evidence and dismissal of criminal charges against co-defendants.

P.G. 218-06: Temporary Removal of Invoiced Property from the Command

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To account for invoiced property temporarily removed from the command to court or other authorized agency.

Key Points

  • Desk officer verifies identity of requesting member via IDENTIFICATION CARD (PD416-091) if from another command
  • PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT prepared and digitally signed by both requesting officer and desk officer
  • Member obtains receipt if property retained by court or other agency
  • Receipt forwarded to Property Clerk Borough Office for FORMS Property entry
  • All official requests for property should be accompanied by a subpoena from the requesting agency (except ADAs and assistant corporation counsels)

Sergeant Focus: The desk officer must verify identity, ensure proper documentation, and make Command Log entries for every temporary property removal.

Key Forms

Form Number
PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE PD521-141
PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET PD521-141A
PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT PD521-1412
ON-LINE BOOKING SYSTEM ARREST WORKSHEET PD244-159
REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT PD521-168
OFFICIAL LETTERHEAD PD158-151
IDENTIFICATION CARD PD416-091

Section 218 — Licenses and Administrative Procedures

P.G. 218-45: Handgun License Suspension, Revocation or Cancellation

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To facilitate the surrender of Handgun Licenses and handguns when a license is suspended, revoked, or cancelled.

Definition

HANDGUN — Any pistol or revolver listed on a Handgun License.

Key Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Operations Coordinator Record receipt of HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE (PD641-123) in Precinct Communication Log; deliver to CO
2 CO Assign member (preferably a supervisor) for prompt investigation
3 CO Ensure investigation completed and forms forwarded to License Division within 30 days
4 Assigned Member Respond to licensee’s address; request surrender of license and handgun(s)
4a Assigned Member Attempt visits at times other than second platoon
4b Assigned Member Contact License Division if licensee refuses AND offers no evidence of legal disposal
6 Assigned Member Complete HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE (3 copies: Original–precinct, Copy 1–License Division, Copy 2–licensee)
7 Assigned Member Prepare INVOICE; process per P.G. 218-23
7a Assigned Member Include in Remarks: HANDGUNS SHALL NOT BE RELEASED WITHOUT WRITTEN AUTHORIZATION OF THE COMMANDING OFFICER, LICENSE DIVISION
8 Assigned Member Attempt to obtain new address if licensee relocated
9 Assigned Member Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT and refer to detective squad when: handgun lost/stolen, licensee avoiding service, or handguns may have been used in crime

Legal Disposal

A licensee may legally dispose of a properly registered handgun by: 1. Sale to a licensed NYS gun dealer 2. Sale to a person authorized to possess handguns 3. Surrendering for safekeeping to a police agency

Exam Alert: Investigation must be completed within 30 days of receipt of HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE.

Sergeant Focus: CO should assign a supervisor to conduct the investigation.

P.G. 218-55: Retention of Invoiced Property

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To properly record retention requests for invoiced property.

Retention Deadlines

Property Type Deadline Action If Missed
Property (non-vehicle/boat) 1 year from date of invoice Property Clerk disposes
Motor vehicle/boat 15 days from date of invoice Property Clerk disposes

Exceptions to Automatic Disposal

Property involved in: - Homicide investigations - Sexual offenses - Arson/explosion investigations - Member of service involved cases - IAB cases

Procedure (FORMS Property)

Step Role Action
1 Assigned Investigator/Invoicing Officer Prepare REQUEST FOR RETENTION (PD521-169) using FORMS Property
2 Same Prepare separate REQUEST for each associated INVOICE
3 Property Clerk Facility Review; if approved, digitally sign and retain; if disapproved, provide explanation

Exam Alert: Each retention request retains property for one year only. A new REQUEST FOR RETENTION must be submitted for each additional one-year period.

Exam Alert: Failure to submit retention request within 1 year (property) or 15 days (vehicle/boat) = Property Clerk disposes of the property (except homicide, sex offense, arson, MOS/IAB cases).

P.G. 218-56: Change of Category for Invoiced Property

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To properly record changes to the category of invoiced property.

When Category Change Is Required

Circumstance Category Change
Case Closed / Arrest Investigatory → Arrest Evidence (or DNA Investigatory → DNA Arrest Evidence) — required for Property Clerk to retain
Case Closed / No Arrest Investigatory → Safekeeping
Other instances As applicable

Procedure (FORMS Property)

Step Role Action
1 Assigned Investigator/Invoicing Officer Use Change of Category function; complete REQUEST FOR INVOICE CHANGE OF CATEGORY (PD521-167)
2 Same Prepare separate REQUEST for each associated INVOICE
3 Desk Officer/Supervisor at Property Clerk Facility Review and digitally sign — approve or reject

Exam Alert: If property still at command (snap-out INVOICE), the original INVOICE is voided and property re-invoiced in the correct category using FORMS Property.

Exam Alert: After category change, Property Clerk determines if property is now eligible for disposal.

P.G. 218-57: Invoicing Property Due to FORMS Application Failure

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To set guidelines for invoicing property when FORMS Property becomes inoperable.

Procedure by Outage Duration

Situation Action
6 hours or less Prepare WORKSHEET; enter into FORMS when it becomes operational
More than 6 hours, command-only problem Remove property to adjoining command and invoice there
Citywide outage, more than 6 hours Contact ITB Service Desk supervisor; follow supervisor’s instructions

Notifications

Step Action
1 Immediately notify Information Technology Bureau (ITB) Service Desk
2 Ascertain: when correctable, command-only or borough/citywide, anticipated duration
3 Command Log entry: ITB member notified, Service Desk ticket number, anticipated duration

Adjoining Command Invoicing

  • Select command where incident occurred as invoicing command (not adjoining command)
  • During Putaway function, select the invoicing command as storage location

Exam Alert: Pre-printed serialized INVOICES stored at commands are NOT to be used until instructed by the CO, Information Technology Systems Division.

Sergeant Focus: COs must ensure adequate supply of pre-printed WORKSHEETS, INVOICES, and associated forms are stored in the property room for use during FORMS outages.

Section 218 — Vehicles in Police Custody

P.G. 218-12: Safeguarding Vehicles in Police Custody

Date Effective: 07/25/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 65 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To safeguard vehicles in police custody at the stationhouse.

Immobilization Methods

The recovering member must immobilize the vehicle without causing damage by:

Method Description
a Remove rotor or coil wire and attach to printout of INVOICE
b Physically block vehicle with another vehicle or stationary structures
c Utilize wheel boots
d Locate starter relay/fuse or fuel pump relay/fuse; photograph location, remove, and attach to INVOICE

Exam Alert: Members should NOT disconnect the battery due to potential risk of damaging some vehicles. Contact Auto Crime Unit (0800–2300) with questions.

  • Prepare WARNING NOTICE — VEHICLE IN POLICE CUSTODY (PD571-1211) and attach to windshield only after fingerprint processing is completed
  • Ensure vehicle is locked
  • Inform relieving desk officer of vehicle location

Exam Alert: A vehicle may be safeguarded at the command for 48 hours when not required as evidence or forfeiture. After 48 hours → removed to Property Clerk storage facility.

P.G. 218-13: Inventory Searches of Automobiles and Other Property

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To protect property, ensure against unwarranted claims of theft, and protect members and others against dangerous instrumentalities.

Automobile Inventory Search Areas

Search includes: glove compartment, console, map pockets, under seats, under floor mats, under/behind dashboard, ashtrays, air vents, under hood, and trunk.

Forcing Open Locked Items

Force open trunk/glove compartment only if it can be done with minimal damage, UNLESS:

Exception Condition
a Reasonably suspect weapons, explosives, hazardous materials or contraband
b Contents in plain view
c Contents can be inferred from outward appearance (gun cases, toolboxes)
d Consent of owner to break open

Consent Requirements (Step 2d)

  1. Ask for consent in a manner that elicits a clear yes or no
  2. Follow up: I can only conduct this inventory if you consent
  3. Inform owner significant damage may occur
  4. Ask: Do you understand?
  5. If conducted, offer RIGHT TO KNOW BUSINESS CARD (PD142-012 or PD142-013)
  6. If owner does NOT consent, cannot inventory unless search warrant obtained

Exam Alert: No consent = no inventory of locked items (absent exigent circumstances). A search warrant is required.

  • Remove all valuables from vehicle and invoice on a separate PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE

P.G. 218-14: Vehicle Stolen and Recovered Within New York City

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To process and dispose of vehicles stolen and recovered within NYC.

Key Steps

Role Key Actions
Recovering Member Obtain NYSPIN info, prepare WORKSHEET, request patrol supervisor
Patrol Supervisor Verify accuracy and completeness of WORKSHEET
Recovering Member Notify registered owner ASAP; at least one telephone attempt
Recovering Member Prepare ROTATION TOW-OWNER NOTIFICATION (PD571-1210) regardless of phone notification
Desk Officer Release vehicle to owner AFTER cancellation of alarm
Desk Officer Determine if patterns of theft/dumping exist; initiate appropriate action
Commanding Officer Review to ensure alarm cancelled and owner notified

Exam Alert: If final insurance settlement was received, enter name of insurance company on INVOICE under Lien Holder or True Owner and notify the insurance company.

P.G. 218-15: Vehicle Stolen Outside NYC and Recovered Within NYC

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Pages: 1

Key Differences from 218-14

  • Notify Inter-City Correspondence Unit for notification to originating police agency
  • An Omniform Complaint Revision will NOT be generated
  • Forward photocopy of INVOICE to Patrol Borough Pattern Identification Module by next business day

P.G. 218-16: Vehicle Stolen Within NYC and Recovered Outside NYC or by Port Authority Police

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Pages: 2

Key Steps

  • Stolen Property Inquiry Unit transmits message through FINEST to precinct of record
  • Desk Officer, Precinct of Record: cancel alarm, notify registered owner, ensure Omniform Complaint Revision prepared
  • Stolen Property Inquiry Unit: forward cancellation acknowledgement to recovering agency and precinct of record

P.G. 218-18: Photographing Stolen Evidence Vehicles When an Arrest Is Made

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To expeditiously release stolen recovered vehicles where arrest is made and vehicle was NOT used in conjunction with any other crime.

Key Rule

THE VEHICLE MAY BE RELEASED TO THE OWNER/REPRESENTATIVE AT THE STATIONHOUSE WITHOUT A RELEASE FROM THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY/CORPORATION COUNSEL — provided evidence photographs are taken.

Required Photographs

VIN, registration sticker, license plates, each side of vehicle (including vent windows, door locks, handles), front/back, interior (ignition lock, console, radio, dashboard), motor, and all damage.

Exam Alert: Operable vehicles remain at command for 48 hours pending owner arrival. If unclaimed → Rotation Tow Program → tow facility for 30 days → pound.

P.G. 218-19: Invoicing Vehicles/Property as Arrest/Investigatory Evidence or for Forfeiture Proceedings or to Determine True Owner

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Pages: 4

Vehicles MUST Be Seized as Arrest Evidence When Arrests Made For:

Charge Details
Larceny of vehicle Grand Larceny Auto (unless value ≤$100 for auto or ≤$1,000 for motorcycle)
Unauthorized use of vehicle —
Criminal possession of stolen property (vehicle) —
Possession of vehicle with altered VIN —
Leaving scene of collision — serious/critical injury —
Assault or homicide — vehicle used as weapon —
Illegal Registration — VTL Section 401(1)(a) Operating an Unregistered Vehicle

Vehicles MAY Be Seized for Forfeiture When Used to Transport:

  • Controlled Substances (Article 220, Penal Law)
  • Gambling Records (Sections 225.20 and 415, PL)
  • Untaxed Cigarettes (State Tax Law/Admin Code)
  • Equipment used in promoting pornography (Article 410, PL)
  • Equipment used in unauthorized recording of sound (Section 420.05, PL)
  • Illegally possessed firearms/rifles/shotguns (Section 265, PL)

Exam Alert: In ALL cases of vehicle seizure (arrest evidence or forfeiture), a VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM (PD571-1218) must be completed and served on the arrestee per P.G. 218-48.

P.G. 218-20: Delivery of Vehicle to Property Clerk

Date Effective: 07/25/25 | Pages: 2

Key Points

  • Traffic Safety Sergeant coordinates with Property Clerk Auto Pounds to ensure delivery within 48 hours of seizure
  • Assigned member conducts vehicle inspection using FORMS Vehicle Inspection function
  • Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET for missing equipment not on original INVOICE
  • Vehicle unable to be driven: notify Fleet Services Division, Department Tow

Sergeant Focus: The Traffic Safety Sergeant has direct responsibility for coordinating timely vehicle delivery to Property Clerk Auto Pounds.

P.G. 218-21: Rotation Tow

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 9

Purpose

To process non-evidence stolen or apparently abandoned vehicles, including motorcycles, recovered within NYC.

Key Definition

APPARENTLY ABANDONED VEHICLE — Unoccupied vehicle abandoned per VTL Section 1224 OR observed under circumstances indicating it may have been stolen but no alarm transmitted.

DOUBLE TOW — Additional tow truck needed for unusual recovery (embankment, overturned, wedged). NOT authorized solely because vehicle has no wheels.

Exam Alert: Double Tow is approved by selecting Yes in the Double Tow caption in FORMS-P when preparing the PCI, and entering the TAX # of the authorizing officer. A vehicle with no wheels does NOT automatically qualify for a double tow — a tow should handle that with one truck. (PG 218-21)

VTL 1224 — Abandoned Vehicle Time Chart

Time Left Unattended Condition
More than 6 hours No plates
More than 24 hours Illegally parked
More than 48 hours Legally parked initially, then becomes illegally parked (e.g., alternate side); 48 hours later it is considered abandoned
More than 96 hours On private property without permission of owner

Exam Alert: Know these VTL 1224 time thresholds cold — they appear frequently in test scenarios involving whether a vehicle qualifies for Ro-Tow. (PG 218-21)

What is NOT Ro-Tow (Memory Aid)

Memory Aid — CHASED FIT DOM: The situations that disqualify a vehicle from Ro-Tow are remembered as: Crime (Dept. Tow), Highway (Authorized or Dept. Tow), Arrest made (except photo release), Safeguard (Aided/Collision), Evidence (Dept. Tow), Determine True Owner — Forfeiture, Investigation, Truck (large) — Derelict (Sanitation), Owner on scene AND recovered prior to police, Moped (limited use motorcycle). (PG 218-21)

Vehicle Is NOT Qualified for Rotation Tow If:

  1. Arrest made in conjunction with recovery (except photo-release cases)
  2. Required as evidence or held for investigation
  3. Used in commission of a crime
  4. Subject to forfeiture proceedings
  5. Meets derelict guidelines
  6. Large truck or limited-use motorcycle (moped)
  7. Owner on scene and recovers own vehicle
  8. Must be safeguarded due to owner inability to remove (prisoner, aided, collision)

Exam Alert: A DSNY (Sanitation Department) sticker or notification does NOT automatically make a vehicle eligible for Ro-Tow. The final determination is made by the UMOS based on a visual inspection using criteria on the rear inside cover of the Ro-Tow Log. (PG 218-21)

Desk Officer Responsibilities

  • Notify next participating tow operator in rotation IMMEDIATELY
  • Determine availability PRIOR TO furnishing vehicle information
  • Tow operators DO NOT have option of deciding availability AFTER being told vehicle type/condition
  • Prepare REPORT OF VIOLATION if tow operator refuses AFTER being informed of vehicle
  • On limited access highways: ONLY franchise tow operator may tow

Exam Alert: If a tow operator refuses an assignment AFTER being informed of vehicle type and condition, prepare a Report of Violation (LD 6) AND forward to the Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP). The next tow operator in sequence is used whenever a tow operator is not available for any reason. (PG 218-21)

Exam Alert: Do NOT cancel the alarm or notify the owner until the tow operator advises that the vehicle has been removed to the storage facility. (PG 218-21)

Commanding Officer/Designee Responsibilities

  • Daily inspection of Ro-Tow Log for previous 24 hours (sign in remarks column with corrective action)
  • Daily inspection of Ro-Tow 30-day file to ensure no vehicle is held more than 30 days
  • Complaints involving license violations/overcharges by tow operators: LD6 forwarded to DCWP
  • Complaints of theft/other unlawful acts by tow company: Complaint Report (61) forwarded to DCWP (for information only)

Exam Alert: Overcharges by tow operators = LD6 to DCWP. Theft or other unlawful acts by tow company = 61 to DCWP (duplicate, informational only). Know which form applies to which complaint type. (PG 218-21)

Tow Operator Rights

Participating tow operators are permitted to view the Rotation Towing Logs of precincts within their assigned zone to verify their rotational sequence.

See Also: PG 218-20 — Delivery of vehicle to Property Clerk (48-hour deadline, Traffic Safety Sergeant responsibility).

Tow Operator Timelines

Timeline Action
Day 1 Vehicle towed to operator’s facility
Day 8–30 Vehicle may be delivered to Property Clerk Auto Pound (weekdays)
Day 30 Vehicles remaining MUST BE DELIVERED to Auto Pound by tow operator
After 15 days at Auto Pound Vehicle/motorcycle disposed of according to law

Exam Alert: An owner WILL NOT be notified nor alarm cancelled until tow operator advises vehicle has been removed to storage facility.

Exam Alert: Final determination of Rotation Tow eligibility must be made by a uniformed member based on visual inspection — Sanitation Department notification does NOT confer automatic eligibility.

Other Agencies Authorized to Use Rotation Tow

  • NYC Parks Department
  • Seagate PD (60th Precinct)

P.G. 218-22: Processing Boats in Police Custody

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Pages: 2

Key Points

  • Notify owner (if known) and Harbor Unit for removal to storage facility
  • Separate INVOICE for evidence/property not part of boat equipment
  • Alarm NOT cancelled until boat is at storage facility or receipted by owner
  • Boat found on a street: contact Department tow — NOT Rotation Tow

P.G. 218-48: Vehicle Seizure at Time of Arrest

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To ensure opportunity for a post-seizure retention hearing for individuals from whom a vehicle is seized.

Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Arresting Officer Prepare INVOICE per P.G. 218-19
2 Arresting Officer Complete VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM (PD571-1218) for Forfeiture or Arrest Evidence
3 Arresting Officer Insert precinct vehicle seizure number from COMMAND VEHICLE SEIZURE INDEX (PD571-091)
5 Arresting Officer Activate Body-Worn Camera when serving VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM
6 Arresting Officer Have defendant/operator sign form (note refusal if applicable)
10 Desk Officer Ensure four photocopies distributed: defendant, ADA, invoicing officer, arresting officer

Exam Alert: Failure to serve VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM may result in the inability to prosecute forfeiture cases.

Exam Alert: Vehicles registered to third parties are generally NOT seized for forfeiture unless beneficial ownership or specific knowledge by the third party can be proven.

Sergeant Focus: Commanding officers must ensure compliance is part of the command’s monthly self-inspection program monitored by Quality Assurance Section.

Section 218 — Vendor and Peddler Property

P.G. 218-39: Seizure, Removal, and Disposition of Property from General and Food Vendors

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To remove property from unlicensed food/general vendors or licensed general vendors peddling in violation of law.

Key Definitions

Term Definition
General Vendor Person who peddles/sells/leases goods or services, other than food, at retail in a public area
Food Vendor Person who peddles/sells food at retail in any public place
Pushcart Wheeled vehicle used by vendor, movable with or without motor, no DMV registration required
Stand Movable/portable/collapsible structure used to display or store merchandise
Seizure Removal pending forfeiture by City’s Law Department — NOT returned without written authorization
Removal Confiscation — may be returned under certain circumstances even if OATH summons not adjudicated
Property Clerk Division Seal Non-releasable, barcoded, pre-numbered security seal for vendor property bags

Procedure

Step Role Action
1a Member Serve Civil Summons Returnable to OATH
1b Member Place seized property in large plastic bags; twist top closed
1c Member Affix Property Clerk Division seal with prongs facing inward
4 Member Deliver pushcart, stand, goods to command of occurrence
5 Member Do NOT permit vendor to assist in removal or ride in Department vehicle (except arrest)
6 Member Return vendor’s license after issuing summons
7 Member Prepare WORKSHEET marked Removed/Safeguard
9 Member Notify food rescue organization when DOH employee determines food meets sanitary requirements

Desk Officer Responsibilities

Step Action
12 Examine sealed bag — verify seal number matches WORKSHEET and summons
16 Notify Property Clerk Division if large amount of property
17 Notify Department of Sanitation for expeditious disposal of spoiled food
18 Have OFFICIAL LETTERHEAD report prepared within 24 hours after perishable food disposed
19 Mail copy of report to vendor via U.S. Mail

Exigent Circumstances

Step Action
23 Notify vendor to stop selling and begin to move within one minute
24 Inform vendor not to return for at least two hours (vendor must be notified of time period)

Exam Alert: Supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement: one supervisor for five police officers. Dedicated supervisors present at all property seizures when practical.

Exam Alert: If a sealed bag breaks, a supervisor will re-bag using the FORMS repack function. Do NOT remove property or the original seal — place entire bag with seal into new bag with new seal.

Sergeant Focus: CO must designate sufficient supervisors to frequently inspect perishable foods so items can be condemned and disposed of when necessary.

P.G. 218-40: General Vendor Distributor Forfeiture Procedure

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To confiscate vehicles of unlicensed general vendor distributors and institute forfeiture proceedings.

Definitions

Term Definition
Distributor Person/organization engaged in sale, consignment, or distribution of goods for sale/resale by a general vendor (excluding owners who personally transport and sell their own goods in compliance with law)
Public Space All publicly-owned property between property lines on a street, including parks, plazas, roadways, sidewalks, piers, stadiums, terminals

Key Steps

Step Role Action
1 Member Serve Civil Summons Returnable to OATH for NYC Admin Code 20-474.1
2 Member Confiscate vehicle and contents for forfeiture
3 Member Remove vehicle to precinct; comply with P.G. 218-12
4 Member Prepare WORKSHEET — select FORFEITURE
6 Desk Officer Have vehicle removed to Property Clerk storage facility
9 Desk Officer Prepare PEDDLER FORFEITURE QUESTIONNAIRE (PD260-155) when claimant arrives
10 Desk Officer Have claimant sign Peddler Property — Acknowledgment of Demand tear-off portion
11 Desk Officer Forward documents within one day of demand to NYC Law Department

Required Vehicle Signage

Distributor vehicles must display a sign with: - Licensee’s name and address - Business telephone numbers - Words General Vending Distributor - Department of Consumer and Worker Protection telephone complaint number

Letters/numerals must be at least 1.5 inches in height, 0.25 inches in width, black and white.

P.G. 218-41: Unlicensed Peddler Forfeiture Program

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To institute forfeiture proceedings against property seized from unlicensed street vendors.

Scope

ONLY property of: - An unlicensed food or general vendor, OR - A licensed general vendor violating restricted streets provision (Admin Code 20-465, subd. 1)

is subject to forfeiture. Confiscate only property on display and any cart, table, or vehicle used to display it.

Key Steps

Step Role Action
1 Member Comply with P.G. 218-39
2 Member Select FORFEITURE as Property Category
4 Desk Officer Prepare PEDDLER FORFEITURE QUESTIONNAIRE upon claimant arrival
6 Desk Officer Forward documents within one day to NYC Law Department
9 Desk Officer Comply with steps 4–8 for EACH SUBSEQUENT claimant

Exam Alert: Corporation Counsel is responsible for serving the claimant with the summons and complaint — but documents must be forwarded within one day of claimant’s appearance.

P.G. 218-42: Obtaining and Returning Property Clerk Division Seals

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To record distribution of Property Clerk Division seals and ensure integrity of returned seals.

Key Points

  • Members below rank of captain on peddler enforcement must carry sufficient seals
  • Seals are issued in sets of 20, consecutively numbered
  • Member signs PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEAL DISTRIBUTION CARD (PD521-1411)
  • Completed CERTIFICATION OF PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEALS (PD521-061) delivered to immediate supervisor
  • Unused/damaged seals are destroyed — NOT re-issued
  • Integrity Control Officer makes random inspections

Exam Alert: Member must notify CO without delay when a Property Clerk Division seal is damaged or missing.

P.G. 218-43: Removal and Storage Fees for Licensed Food Vendors

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To collect removal and storage fees for licensed food vendor property.

Fee Schedule

Item Fee
Vehicle, cart, stand removed by Department vehicle $65.00
Vehicle, cart, stand NOT removed by Department vehicle $20.00
Goods seized with vendor’s vehicle/cart/stand $10.00
Goods only seized $20.00
Storage fee $5.00 per day (or part of day)

Exam Alert: No storage fee for the day property is initially delivered to the command.

Exam Alert: Goods/foods and vehicle/cart/stand stored separately are charged separate storage fees.

Special Rules

  • Licensed vendor with valid license: property returned upon demand without payment of any fee
  • Unlicensed peddler property: NOT returned upon payment — subject to forfeiture per P.G. 218-41
  • DO NOT release to owner who alleges property was stolen and refuses to pay
  • Fees forwarded to Audits and Accounts Unit on next business day

P.G. 218-44: Removal and Storage Charges (Vehicles/Boats)

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To collect and forward fees for vehicles or boats in police custody (abandoned, collision, unoccupied boat found adrift — NOT evidence/impounded vehicles).

Fee Schedule

Item Fee
Towing fee (Department tow/launch) $25.00
Storage fee (abandoned or collision vehicle/boat) $5.00 per day (no fee for delivery day)
Storage fee (stolen vehicle/boat or boat found adrift) $5.00 per day commencing 3 days after notice to owner by registered mail
  • Prepare FEE RECEIPT (PD122-017) in numerical sequence
  • Forward fees to Audits and Accounts Unit each day (excluding weekends/holidays)

Exam Alert: DO NOT release vehicle/boat to an owner who alleges it was stolen and refuses to pay charges due.

Section 218 — Key Terms, Timeframes, Fees, and Forms

Property Categories

Category Definition
Arrest Evidence Property seized for court presentation
DNA Arrest Evidence Property seized for court presentation containing potentially probative DNA evidence
Investigatory Property seized for investigation with COMPLAINT REPORT and no ARREST WORKSHEET
DNA Investigatory Investigatory property containing potentially probative DNA evidence
Forfeiture Property used to facilitate a crime or representing proceeds/substituted proceeds
Decedent’s Property Personal property of a deceased person
Found Property Non-contraband property found by a member or civilian
Peddler’s Property Items removed from vendors (stands, carts)
Amnesty Firearms surrendered via Cash for Guns Program
Safekeeping Property to be returned to owner
Determine True Owner Property where true owner cannot be determined and no immediate arrest warranted
Photo Release Recovered stolen vehicles where arrest made and vehicle not used in other crime
Rotation Tow Non-evidence stolen or apparently abandoned vehicles/motorcycles recovered in NYC

Firearms Definitions

Term Definition
Firearm Includes rifles, shotguns, pistols, revolvers, starter’s pistols, zip guns, air/CO2 guns, homemade/modified guns, simulated/toy firearms, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms, stabilizing brace firearms
Cartridge Live ammunition — bullet + shell casing as one unit
Fired Bullet Part of cartridge fired through barrel
Shell Casing Metal casing remaining in/ejecting from firearm after bullet leaves

Vehicle Definitions

Term Definition
Apparently Abandoned Vehicle Unoccupied vehicle abandoned per VTL 1224 OR observed under circumstances indicating it may be stolen but no alarm transmitted
Double Tow Additional tow truck for unusual recovery (embankment, overturned, wedged) — NOT authorized solely because vehicle has no wheels

Other Key Definitions

Term Definition
Regulated Waste Blood/infectious materials in liquid/semi-liquid state; contaminated sharps; pathological wastes
Contraband Property whose mere possession is prohibited by law (not property merely suspected stolen)
Numismatic/Sentimental Value All gold coins, all U.S. silver coins dated 1964 or prior, extremely old bills
Negotiable Instrument Cash equivalents: money orders, traveler’s checks, bank checks
Co-Occupant Person residing on a permanent basis in same premises with deceased
General Vendor Person selling goods other than food at retail in a public area
Food Vendor Person selling food at retail in any public place
Distributor Person/organization engaged in sale/distribution of goods for resale by a general vendor
Handgun Any pistol or revolver listed on a Handgun License

Critical Timeframes

Timeframe Rule Source
48 hours Maximum time vehicle may be safeguarded at command (non-evidence) 218-12
48 hours Maximum time for delivery of seized vehicle to Property Clerk Auto Pound 218-20
30 days Tow operator MUST deliver vehicle to Auto Pound 218-21
15 days After 15 days at Auto Pound, vehicle disposed of according to law 218-21
15 days Retention deadline for investigatory motor vehicles/boats 218-55
1 year Retention deadline for investigatory property (non-vehicle) 218-55
6 years Filed INVOICES maintained (each July, previous year boxed to Property Clerk warehouse) 218-02
10 days Desk officer must authorize release or denial of investigatory evidence 218-02
5 days Claimant may request CO/designee review after denial of investigatory evidence 218-02
30 days Handgun license surrender investigation must be completed 218-45
60 days Investigation if found property not returned by interstate company/airline/bank 218-27
24 hours Report prepared after disposal of perishable vendor food 218-39
120 hours Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit used within this period (or medical discretion) 218-33
0100 hours Daily deadline for firearms requiring lab analysis to be delivered to patrol borough office 218-23
1 minute Time for vendor to begin moving in exigent circumstances 218-39
2 hours Minimum time vendor must stay away from location in exigent circumstances 218-39
3 business days Property Clerk issues check for federal/state forfeiture upon written request 218-38
6 hours FORMS outage threshold — under 6 hours: prepare WORKSHEET and wait 218-57

Fee Schedule

Fee Type Amount Source
Vendor removal — by Department vehicle $65.00 218-43
Vendor removal — not by Department vehicle $20.00 218-43
Vendor goods seized with vehicle/cart/stand $10.00 218-43
Vendor goods only seized $20.00 218-43
Vendor storage (per day or part) $5.00 218-43
Vehicle/boat towing (Department tow) $25.00 218-44
Vehicle/boat storage (per day or part) $5.00 218-44

Key Forms

Form Number Used For
PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE PD521-141 All invoiced property
PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET PD521-141A Draft preparation of INVOICE
PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT PD521-1412 Transferring property between locations
PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT ACCEPTANCE DOCUMENT PD521-172 Receipt upon delivery of transferred property
REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT PD521-168 Requesting lab analysis of evidence
ON-LINE BOOKING SYSTEM ARREST WORKSHEET PD244-159 Arrest processing
ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF FOUND PROPERTY PD542-012 Receipt for found property delivered on patrol
REPORT OF UNCLAIMED PROPERTY PD542-122 Reporting found property not claimed
WARNING NOTICE — VEHICLE IN POLICE CUSTODY PD571-1211 Affixed to windshield of vehicles in custody
ROTATION TOW — OWNER NOTIFICATION PD571-1210 Notifying owner of rotation tow
VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM PD571-1218 Served on arrestee when vehicle seized
COMMAND VEHICLE SEIZURE INDEX PD571-091 Tracking vehicle seizure numbers at command
FEE RECEIPT PD122-017 Collecting removal/storage fees
SEAL FOR DOOR OF D.O.A. PREMISES PD517-120 Sealing deceased person’s premises
IDENTIFICATION TAG PD317-091 Receipt for body removed by morgue/undertaker
NOTICE TO PERSONS FROM WHOM PROPERTY HAS BEEN REMOVED PD521-124 Notice of rights to reclaim property
HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE PD641-123 Directing surrender of handgun license
HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE RESPONSE PD641-1234A Documenting investigation results
PEDDLER FORFEITURE QUESTIONNAIRE PD260-155 Documenting claimant information for forfeiture
CONSENT TO SUSPECT EVIDENCE COLLECTION KIT PD321-031 Suspect consent for forensic evidence exam
CERTIFICATION OF PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEALS PD521-061 Tracking peddler enforcement seals
PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEAL DISTRIBUTION CARD PD521-1411 Signing for issued seals
REQUEST FOR RETENTION PD521-169 Requesting retention of invoiced property
REQUEST FOR INVOICE CHANGE OF CATEGORY PD521-167 Changing property category
OFFICIAL LETTERHEAD PD158-151 Various notifications

Key Acronyms (from The Key Lessons 21–22)

Acronym Full Term Context
FAS Firearms Analysis Section Section of the Police Lab that analyzes firearms evidence
JFTF Joint Firearms Task Force Contact for emergency firearm trace requests
SECK Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit DNA evidence category for lab delivery
PTR Property Transfer Report Report used when transferring property between locations
BATF Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives Contacted for routine or emergency firearm traces
DCWP Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection Receives LD6 complaints about tow operator violations
DSNY Dept. of Sanitation New York Tags vehicles for potential Ro-Tow (but UMOS makes final determination)
EPCS Evidence Property Control Specialist Obtains Ro Tow copy of PCI and updates FORMS-P

Ro-Tow Disqualifiers — CHASED FIT DOM

The mnemonic CHASED FIT DOM identifies what is NOT a Rotation Tow situation:

Letter Situation Notes
C Crime Dept. Tow
H Highway Authorized Tow or Dept. Tow
A Arrest made Except photo release
S Safeguard Aided/Collision
E Evidence Dept. Tow
D Determine True Owner —
F Forfeiture —
I Investigation —
T Truck (large) —
D Derelict Sanitation guidelines on inside back cover of Ro-Tow Log
O Owner on scene AND recovered prior to police —
M Moped (limited use motorcycle) —

VTL 1224 — Abandoned Vehicle Thresholds

Time Condition
More than 6 hours No plates
More than 24 hours Illegally parked
More than 48 hours Legally parked initially → becomes abandoned
More than 96 hours On private property without owner permission

Four Transparent Lab Bags (218-04)

Bag Contents
1 — GUNS Firearms and firearm-related evidence
2 — DRUGS Controlled substances / marijuana (PCI only — no Request for Lab Exam, no 61, no OLBS, no zFINEST)
3 — DNA DNA evidence including SECKs
4 — CRIMINALISTICS Trace evidence (hair, fiber, paint, GSR, etc.)

Additional Critical Timeframes (from Lessons 21–22)

Timeframe Rule Source
0030 Desk Officer must notify Patrol Boro that evidence is going to Lab 218-04
0100 Lab delivery must occur on 1st platoon prior to this time 218-04
0100 If boro not called by 0030, Borough Clerical Officer calls command 218-04
2 weeks Call BATF Regional Crime Gun Center for routine trace (after receipt by FAS) 218-23
Day 8–30 Ro-Tow operator may deliver vehicle to Property Clerk Auto Pound on weekdays 218-21
Day 30 Ro-Tow operator MUST deliver vehicle to Auto Pound 218-21
After 15 days at Auto Pound Unclaimed vehicles disposed of according to law 218-21

Key Contact Units

Unit When to Contact
Auto Crime Unit (0800–2300) Questions about vehicle immobilization
Stolen Property Inquiry Unit Cancel/transmit alarms; ONLY unit that cancels firearms alarms
Inter-City Correspondence Unit Vehicle stolen outside NYC recovered inside NYC
Computer Crimes Squad (Mon–Fri 0600–2000) Computer evidence seizure
Office of Chief of Detectives Computer evidence (after hours)
Asset Forfeiture Unit (24/7) Currency/instruments $1,000+ for forfeiture
Bomb Squad Range — Rodman’s Neck (Mon–Fri 0700–1500) Fireworks delivery (Mon–Fri 7x3)
Bomb Squad Office Fireworks delivery at all other times or if Range personnel unavailable
License Division Handgun license surrender issues
Legal Bureau Suspect evidence consent refusal; civil enforcement questions
Internal Affairs Bureau Command Center Property discrepancies; currency deposit discrepancies
Property Clerk Division Large seizures (2,000+ bills); large vendor property
Harbor Unit Boat removal to storage facility
Fleet Services Division, Department Tow Vehicles unable to be driven
Information Technology Bureau Service Desk FORMS Property outages
NYPD OCME Liaison Unit Unfounded sexual offense cases
FIU OCME Liaison Unit Immediate DNA delivery decisions
NYS Tax Office Lottery property recovery
Audits and Accounts Unit Fee receipts forwarded daily
Police Lab Evidence Control Supervisor (24/7) Unusual property before Lab delivery (large seizures, hypos, etc.)
Joint Firearms Task Force (JFTF) Emergency firearm trace requests
BATF Regional Crime Gun Center Routine firearm trace (2 weeks after receipt by FAS)
Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP) Tow operator violations/overcharges (LD6); tow company theft (61 dup.)

Section 218 — Review Questions

Instructions

Select the best answer for each question. Answers are provided after each question.

1. Under P.G. 218-12, which of the following is NOT an authorized method for immobilizing a vehicle in police custody?

  1. Removing rotor or coil wire and attaching to INVOICE printout
  2. Disconnecting the vehicle battery
  3. Utilizing wheel boots
  4. Removing starter relay/fuse or fuel pump relay/fuse
Answer

B) Disconnecting the vehicle battery

Members should NOT disconnect the battery due to the potential risk of damaging some vehicles. Contact the Auto Crime Unit (0800–2300) with questions. (P.G. 218-12)

2. A vehicle may be safeguarded at the command for how long when not required as evidence or forfeiture?

  1. 24 hours
  2. 48 hours
  3. 72 hours
  4. 30 days
Answer

B) 48 hours

After 48 hours, the vehicle must be removed to a Property Clerk storage facility. (P.G. 218-12)

3. During an inventory search of an automobile, a locked trunk cannot be forced open UNLESS one of several exceptions applies. If the owner does not consent and no exception exists, what is required?

  1. A supervisor’s approval
  2. A court order from the DA’s office
  3. A search warrant
  4. Notification to the Auto Crime Unit
Answer

C) A search warrant

No consent = no inventory of locked items (absent exigent circumstances). A search warrant is required. (P.G. 218-13)

4. Under the Rotation Tow program, when must the tow operator deliver a vehicle to the Property Clerk Auto Pound?

  1. Within 15 days
  2. Within 24 hours
  3. By day 30
  4. By day 60
Answer

C) By day 30

Vehicles remaining at the tow facility MUST BE DELIVERED to the Auto Pound by the tow operator by day 30. (P.G. 218-21)

5. When serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM on a defendant, the arresting officer must:

  1. Have a supervisor present as witness
  2. Activate the Body-Worn Camera
  3. Obtain written consent from the ADA
  4. Have the form notarized
Answer

B) Activate the Body-Worn Camera

The arresting officer must activate the BWC when serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM. (P.G. 218-48)

6. When marking firearm-related evidence, where should initials be scratched on a shell casing recovered from a crime scene?

  1. The exterior of the casing
  2. The bottom/base of the casing
  3. The inside of the casing
  4. Nowhere — shell casings are not individually marked
Answer

C) The inside of the casing

Shell casings are marked on the INSIDE. DO NOT mark the exterior or bottom. (P.G. 218-23)

7. Firearms requiring laboratory analysis must be delivered to the patrol borough office by what time daily?

  1. 2400 hours
  2. 0600 hours
  3. 0100 hours
  4. 1800 hours
Answer

C) 0100 hours

All invoiced firearms requiring Firearms Analysis Section examination must be delivered by 0100 hours daily. Delay requires exigent circumstances AND permission from the precinct CO/duty captain. (P.G. 218-23)
