8. A PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE being sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed how many line items?

  1. 10
  2. 15
  3. 20
  4. 25
Answer

C) 20

INVOICES sent or potentially sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed twenty line items. (P.G. 218-01)

9. Which unit is the ONLY unit authorized to cancel alarms for firearms?

  1. Auto Crime Unit
  2. Recovering command desk officer
  3. Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
  4. Property Clerk Division
Answer

C) Stolen Property Inquiry Unit

Cancellation of firearms alarms will be made ONLY by the Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. The recovering command will NOT cancel. (P.G. 218-23)

10. When seizing computer evidence, what should a member do if the computer needs to be powered off?

  1. Press the on/off switch
  2. Hold the power button for 10 seconds
  3. Unplug the computer from the wall outlet
  4. Close the laptop lid
Answer

C) Unplug the computer from the wall outlet

Do NOT use the on/off switch. Unplug from the wall outlet only. Prior to removing the plug, photograph the monitor screen. (P.G. 218-31)

11. Invoiced property categorized as Investigatory (non-vehicle) will be disposed of by the Property Clerk after what period if no retention request is submitted?

  1. 6 months
  2. 1 year
  3. 2 years
  4. 15 days
Answer

B) 1 year

The Property Clerk will dispose of investigatory property (non-vehicle) after one year from the date of invoice if no retention request is submitted. Vehicles/boats are disposed of after 15 days. (P.G. 218-55)

12. When processing non-evidence currency, the command messenger must be accompanied by whom when delivering the deposit bag to the bank?

  1. A detective
  2. The invoicing officer
  3. Another uniformed member of the service or Evidence/Property Control Specialist
  4. The desk officer
Answer

C) Another uniformed member of the service or Evidence/Property Control Specialist

Currency is delivered to the bank’s night deposit vault by the command messenger accompanied by another uniformed member or Evidence/Property Control Specialist. (P.G. 218-36)

13. Under P.G. 218-38, what is the minimum value of currency/negotiable instruments that requires notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit for forfeiture processing?

  1. $500
  2. $1,000
  3. $5,000
  4. $10,000
Answer

B) $1,000

Seized U.S. currency and/or negotiable instruments valued at $1,000 or more that are deemed forfeitable require notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit (24/7). (P.G. 218-38)

14. When processing a deceased person’s property, what must the patrol supervisor obtain before directing a body search?

  1. Permission from the commanding officer
  2. Conferral with the Medical Examiner
  3. A search warrant
  4. Written consent from next of kin
Answer

B) Conferral with the Medical Examiner

The patrol supervisor must confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner PRIOR to directing a member to search the body for valuables and identification documents. (P.G. 218-28)

15. What is the maximum number of fireworks bags that should be transported in an RMP?

  1. 2 bags
  2. 4 bags
  3. 6 bags
  4. 10 bags
Answer

B) 4 bags

As a general rule, no more than four bags of fireworks should be transported in an RMP, placed in the rear seat away from heat/spark sources. (P.G. 218-53)

16. A handgun license surrender investigation must be completed and forms forwarded to the License Division within how many days?

  1. 10 days
  2. 15 days
  3. 30 days
  4. 60 days
Answer

C) 30 days

The investigation must be completed and all related forms forwarded to the License Division, through channels, within thirty days of date the HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE was received at command. (P.G. 218-45)

17. Under the vendor enforcement procedures, what is the supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement?

  1. One supervisor for three officers
  2. One supervisor for five officers
  3. One supervisor for eight officers
  4. One supervisor for ten officers
Answer

B) One supervisor for five officers

Supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement shall not exceed one supervisor for five police officers. (P.G. 218-39)

18. When the desk officer receives a request for the return of investigatory evidence, the release must be authorized or denied within how many days?

  1. 5 days
  2. 10 days
  3. 15 days
  4. 30 days
Answer

B) 10 days

The desk officer must authorize release no more than ten days after receipt of the request, if property is no longer required as evidence. If denied, claimant may request CO/designee review within 5 days. (P.G. 218-02)

19. What is the removal fee when a licensed food vendor’s vehicle, cart, or stand is removed by a Department vehicle?

  1. $20.00
  2. $25.00
  3. $50.00
  4. $65.00
Answer

D) $65.00

The removal fee when property is removed by a Department vehicle is $65.00. If NOT removed by Department vehicle, the fee is $20.00. (P.G. 218-43)

20. When FORMS Property becomes inoperable for more than six hours and the problem is isolated to the invoicing command only, what should the desk officer instruct?

  1. Use pre-printed serialized INVOICES
  2. Contact the Property Clerk Division for manual processing
  3. Remove property to an adjoining command and invoice there
  4. Wait for the system to be restored
Answer

C) Remove property to an adjoining command and invoice there

When the outage exceeds six hours and is isolated to the command, the invoicing member removes property to an adjoining command and invoices in compliance with current procedures. The invoicing command (where the incident occurred) is selected as the invoicing command in FORMS. (P.G. 218-57)

21. Under P.G. 218-04, a found revolver with no connection to an arrest or criminal investigation would be:

  1. Delivered to the Police Laboratory for analysis
  2. Delivered to the Firearms Analysis Section (FAS) for tracing
  3. Delivered direct to the Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required)
  4. Held at the command until a detective supervisor directs otherwise
Answer

C) Delivered direct to the Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required)

Found revolvers not associated with an arrest or criminal investigation do NOT require laboratory analysis and go directly to the Property Clerk. By contrast, found firearms (semi-automatic handguns, etc.) DO go to the Lab. (P.G. 218-04)

22. Under P.G. 218-04, the Desk Officer must notify the Patrol Boro prior to what time that evidence is going to the Laboratory?

  1. 0100 hours
  2. 0030 hours
  3. 2400 hours
  4. 2200 hours
Answer

B) 0030 hours

The Desk Officer notifies the Patrol Boro PRIOR to 0030 that there is evidence going to the Lab for delivery. The actual delivery must then occur on the 1st platoon PRIOR to 0100. If the desk officer fails to call the boro by 0030, the Borough Member/Clerical Officer will call command at 0100. (P.G. 218-04)

23. Under P.G. 218-04, which of the following forms is NOT attached to the Drugs transparent bag going to the Police Laboratory?

  1. Property Clerk Invoice (PCI)
  2. Request for Laboratory Examination
  3. zFINEST printout
  4. All of the above are NOT attached to the Drugs bag
Answer

D) All of the above are NOT attached to the Drugs bag

The Drugs bag does NOT get a Request for Laboratory Examination, does NOT get a 61/Omni printout, and does NOT get a zFINEST printout. The ONLY paperwork attached to the Drugs bag is the PCI. (P.G. 218-04)

24. A member fires their service weapon during an officer-involved shooting. The recovered shell casings from the scene should be marked:

  1. On the exterior of the casing
  2. On the inside of the casing only
  3. On the bottom of the casing
  4. Not marked — shell casings from officer-involved shootings are never marked
Answer

B) On the inside of the casing only

Discharged shell casings are marked on the INSIDE only — never on the exterior or bottom. This applies to all shell casings regardless of the circumstances. (P.G. 218-23)

25. When a member wishes to have a firearm sent to the Lab but does NOT want it traced, what must they write and where?

  1. NO TRACE in the remarks section of the INVOICE only
  2. DO NOT TRACE in block letters in the remarks section of BOTH the PCI AND the Request for Lab Exam
  3. HOLD FOR TRACE in the arrest paperwork
  4. Nothing — tracing is automatic and cannot be declined
Answer

B) DO NOT TRACE in block letters in the remarks section of BOTH the PCI AND the Request for Lab Exam

The phrase DO NOT TRACE must be written in block letters in the remarks section of both documents. If just one document is marked, the firearm may still be traced. (P.G. 218-23)

26. Officers recover an abandoned vehicle with no plates on a highway. It has been unattended for 7 hours. Under VTL 1224, how should this vehicle be classified?

  1. Not abandoned — must be unattended 24 hours
  2. Abandoned — unattended more than 6 hours with no plates
  3. Derelict — subject to Sanitation disposal
  4. Evidence — subject to Dept. Tow only
Answer

B) Abandoned — unattended more than 6 hours with no plates

Under VTL 1224, a vehicle left unattended for more than 6 hours with no plates is deemed abandoned. This is the shortest of the four time thresholds. (P.G. 218-21)

27. A tow operator, after being told the type and condition of a vehicle, refuses to accept a Rotation Tow assignment. The Desk Officer should:

  1. Call the next tow operator without any documentation
  2. Prepare a Report of Violation (LD6) and forward to DCWP; then call next operator in sequence
  3. Notify the commanding officer and await further instructions
  4. Prepare a Complaint Report (61) and forward to DCWP
Answer

B) Prepare a Report of Violation (LD6) and forward to DCWP; then call next operator in sequence

When a tow operator refuses AFTER being informed of vehicle type and condition, the Desk Officer prepares a Report of Violation (LD6) and forwards it to the Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP). The next tow operator in sequence is then notified. (P.G. 218-21)

28. A complaint is received that a Rotation Tow company stole items from a towed vehicle. The correct form and routing is:

  1. LD6 (Report of Violation) forwarded to DCWP
  2. Complaint Report (61) forwarded to DCWP (duplicate, informational only)
  3. LD6 forwarded to Internal Affairs Bureau
  4. Complaint Report (61) forwarded directly to the tow company’s license bureau
Answer

B) Complaint Report (61) forwarded to DCWP (duplicate, informational only)

Theft and other unlawful acts by tow companies are recorded on the Complaint Report (61) and the duplicate is forwarded to DCWP for informational purposes only. Overcharges use LD6. Know the distinction. (P.G. 218-21)

29. A patrol supervisor responds to a scene where a person has died alone in their apartment. The supervisor wants to search the body. What must be done FIRST?

  1. Request Operations to notify the Public Administrator
  2. Confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner
  3. Obtain a search warrant from the DA
  4. Seal the door with a SEAL FOR DOOR OF DOA PREMISES
Answer

B) Confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner

The patrol supervisor must confer with the ME PRIOR to directing any member to search the body. This is a mandatory first step. (P.G. 218-28)

30. When securing fireworks for transport in an RMP, members should:

  1. Place bags in the trunk, sealed, with windows closed
  2. Place up to 4 bags in the rear seat with all windows opened minimum 2 inches
  3. Place bags in the front passenger seat with air conditioning running
  4. Call Bomb Squad for escort before any transport of fireworks
Answer

B) Place up to 4 bags in the rear seat with all windows opened minimum 2 inches

Fireworks are transported in the REAR SEAT of the RMP (NOT the trunk). All windows must be opened a minimum of 2 inches. Maximum 4 bags per RMP. For a significant amount, call FDNY through 311 for escort. (P.G. 218-53)

31. A Sanitation Department worker places a tag on an abandoned-looking vehicle and notifies NYPD. This automatically qualifies the vehicle for Rotation Tow.

  1. True — the DSNY notification triggers the Ro-Tow program
  2. False — the UMOS must make the final determination by visual inspection
  3. True — but only if the vehicle has been tagged for more than 24 hours
  4. False — only the Desk Officer can authorize Ro-Tow after DSNY notification
Answer

B) False — the UMOS must make the final determination by visual inspection

A DSNY sticker or notification does NOT automatically make a vehicle eligible for Ro-Tow. The Desk Officer directs a UMOS to inspect the vehicle and decide if it qualifies based on criteria found on the rear inside cover of the Ro-Tow Log. (P.G. 218-21)

Section 219 — Department Property

Source

  • Patrol Guide Section: 219
  • Source PDF: public-pguide4.pdf (OCR version)
  • Priority: Low

Learning Objectives

  • Know the vehicle inspection requirements (daily and weekly) and required equipment
  • Understand fuel dispensing card types and delivery supervision procedures
  • Know the reporting chain and notifications for lost/stolen Department property, firearms, shields, and ID cards
  • Identify rules for IT systems security, portable radios, LPR devices, and mobile digital devices
  • Understand the eligibility requirements and restrictions for Restricted Parking Permits
  • Know the rules governing OMNY Police Passes, E-Z Pass tags, and radiation detection pagers
  • Recognize emergency power outage procedures and fuel tank responsibilities

Chapter Files

File Procedures Topics
section-219-vehicles-fleet 219-01 through 219-10 Vehicle inspection, maintenance, car wash, tires, tire chains, Department tow, unmarked vehicles
section-219-fuel-facilities 219-11, 219-12, 219-13, 219-28 Fuel dispensing (card types), fuel/heating oil delivery, emergency power outages
section-219-it-communications 219-14, 219-15, 219-23, 219-24, 219-31, 219-32 Computer security, portable radios, callboxes, cell phones, LPR devices, mobile digital devices
section-219-equipment-supplies 219-17, 219-18, 219-19, 219-25, 219-26, 219-27, 219-29, 219-30 Patrol kits (PPE), supply inspection, lockers, E-Z Pass, OMNY passes, radiation pagers, parking permits
section-219-loss-theft 219-20, 219-21 Loss/theft of Department property, lost/stolen firearm/shield/ID card
key-terms — Definitions, timeframes, forms, contact units, fuel card colors
review-questions — 15 multiple-choice practice questions

Procedures

Procedure Title Status
P.G. 219-01 Department Vehicles — Inspection at Start of Tour completed
P.G. 219-02 Weekly Vehicle Inspections completed
P.G. 219-03 Vehicle Maintenance completed
P.G. 219-04 Car Wash completed
P.G. 219-05 Tire Repair completed
P.G. 219-06 Tire Replacement completed
P.G. 219-07 Tire Station Removal completed
P.G. 219-08 Tire Chains completed
P.G. 219-09 Department Tow completed
P.G. 219-10 Unmarked Vehicle Utilization Record completed
P.G. 219-11 Computerized Fuel Dispensing completed
P.G. 219-12 Fuel Delivery Supervision completed
P.G. 219-13 Heating Oil Delivery completed
P.G. 219-14 Computer Systems — Security and Integrity completed
P.G. 219-15 Portable Radios completed
P.G. 219-17 Patrol Kits (PPE) completed
P.G. 219-18 Inspection of Supplies completed
P.G. 219-19 Department Locker Inspections completed
P.G. 219-20 Loss or Theft of Department Property completed
P.G. 219-21 Lost/Stolen Firearm, Shield, or ID Card completed
P.G. 219-23 Solar Cellular Callboxes completed
P.G. 219-24 Cellular Telephones — Patrol Supervisor completed
P.G. 219-25 E-Z Pass Tags completed
P.G. 219-26 NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes completed
P.G. 219-27 Radiation Detection Pagers — Command-Assigned completed
P.G. 219-28 Emergency Power Outages completed
P.G. 219-29 NYPD Restricted Parking Permits completed
P.G. 219-30 Radiation Detection Pagers — Personally Assigned completed
P.G. 219-31 License Plate Reader Devices completed
P.G. 219-32 Department Mobile Digital Devices completed

Sergeant Focus Areas

  • Vehicle inspection accountability (daily tour checks + weekly Sunday inspections)
  • Notification chain for lost/stolen property: Desk Officer → CO/Ops Coordinator → IAB + Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
  • Disciplinary consultation with Department Advocate’s Office for lost/stolen firearms
  • ICO duties: monthly DAS audits (5 members), E-Z Pass monitoring, OMNY pass inspections, parking permit background checks
  • LPR alarm response: ALWAYS verify through NYSPIN before law enforcement action
  • Emergency power: disconnect non-essential equipment from red-plate receptacles; quarterly room surveys
  • Restricted Parking Permit: CO has final authority; 5+ red light or 15+ school speed violations = ineligible

Section 219 — Equipment, Supplies, and Assigned Property

P.G. 219-17: Patrol Kits (Personal Protective Equipment)

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To ensure patrol kits containing personal protective equipment (PPE) are maintained and available.

Patrol Kit Contents

Item
Disposable gowns
Eye/mouth shields
Heavy duty gloves
Shoe covers
Antiseptic wipes
Biohazard bag

Biohazard Waste

Item Detail
Receptacle 32-gallon biohazard receptacle at command
Pickup Medical Division messenger picks up regulated infectious waste

Exam Alert: Regulated infectious waste is picked up by the Medical Division messenger, not sanitation or other units. (P.G. 219-17)

See Also: P.G. 219-01 — Patrol Kit is required equipment in Marked RMPs only.

P.G. 219-18: Inspection of Supplies

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To ensure that supplies delivered to the command are properly inspected.

Key Points

  • Inspect deliveries for proper quantity and quality
  • Accept or refuse based on inspection findings
  • Report discrepancies to the supplying unit

P.G. 219-19: Department Locker Inspections

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To ensure the proper inspection of Department-assigned lockers.

Key Points

  • Member witnesses their own locker inspection when present
  • If member is not present, another member witnesses the inspection
  • Findings documented as appropriate

Exam Alert: A locker inspection must be witnessed — either by the assigned member or, if absent, by another member.

P.G. 219-25: E-Z Pass Tags

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To account for the safekeeping and usage of E-Z Pass tags assigned to each command.

Responsibilities

Role Duty
CO Ensure lieutenant/patrol supervisor accounts for all tags
CO Have ICO monitor E-Z Pass usage
Lt. Platoon Cmdr/Patrol Supervisor Inspect all vehicles each Sunday after second platoon; verify tags affixed to center top windshield; record serial numbers on VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT
ICO Maintain inventory; review usage for compliance; incorporate into monthly self-inspection

Mounting Rules

Vehicle Type Mounting Method
Standard Permanently affixed with double-sided tape (top center windshield)
Vehicles routinely leaving city (e.g., Category I) Secured with velcro strips (removable)

Transfer Restrictions

  • Commands are NOT authorized to transfer E-Z Pass tags between vehicles
  • All reassignments approved by CO, Fleet Services Division

Authorized Toll Crossings (Partial List)

Bronx-Whitestone Bridge, George Washington Bridge, Bayonne Bridge, Goethals Bridge, Henry Hudson Bridge, Holland Tunnel, Queens Midtown Tunnel, Lincoln Tunnel, Throgs Neck Bridge, Outerbridge Crossing, Cross Bay Veterans Memorial Bridge, Verrazano-Narrows Bridge, Hugh L. Carey Tunnel, Robert F. Kennedy Bridge, Marine Parkway-Gil Hodges Memorial Bridge

NOT Authorized Toll Crossings (Partial List)

New Jersey Turnpike, New York State Thruway, Garden State Parkway, New England Thruway, Governor Mario M. Cuomo (Tappan Zee) Bridge

Lost/Stolen E-Z Pass

Step Action
1 Notify desk officer
2 Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT
3 Notify IAB; enter log number on report
4 Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit of serial number
5 CO investigates; forwards report to CO, Fleet Services Division

Exam Alert: E-Z Pass tags are NOT authorized for the NJ Turnpike, NY State Thruway, Garden State Parkway, New England Thruway, or Tappan Zee Bridge. (P.G. 219-25)

Exam Alert: Commands may NOT transfer E-Z Pass tags between vehicles — Fleet Services Division must approve. (P.G. 219-25)

See Also: P.G. 219-10 — Category I vehicles (leave the city regularly) use velcro strips for E-Z Pass tags instead of permanent double-sided tape.

P.G. 219-26: NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes

Date Effective: 01/06/26 | Last Revision: R.O. 3 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To provide for the issuance, control, and strict accountability of NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes to all uniformed members.

Key Facts

Item Detail
Issued by Human Resources Division
Assigned to Individual uniformed member (NOT the command)
Unique serial number Each pass has its own number
Validity period Generally 7 years
Renewal Coordinated by Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit
Carry requirement At all times when shield and ID card required
Not valid on MTA express buses
Usage monitoring Each use electronically recorded by NYC Transit

CO Responsibilities

Duty
Maintain account of all passes
Ensure all uniformed members have a valid pass
Enter serial number on member’s FORCE RECORD
Have ICO monitor the program
Investigate damaged/defective passes
Investigate lost/stolen passes per P.G. 219-20

ICO Responsibilities

Duty
Enter serial number on FORCE RECORD
Inspect passes at roll calls and semi-annual uniform inspections
Investigate damaged/defective passes; prepare Typed Letterhead report
Hand deliver damaged/defective pass to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit

Long-Term Leave

  • Member must surrender OMNY pass immediately prior to long-term leave (military, child care)
  • Pass hand-delivered to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit
  • Upon return, report prepared requesting re-issuance

Lost/Stolen Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Member Notify desk officer
2 Desk Officer Notify CO and operations coordinator
3 Operations Coordinator Comply with P.G. 219-20; prepare COMPLAINT REPORT
4 Operations Coordinator Notify IAB Command Center; enter log number
5 Operations Coordinator Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
6 Operations Coordinator Notify Transit Bureau Wheel and Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit

Separation from Service

  • All passes surrendered upon separation (retirement, resignation, dismissal)
  • Notation on PROPERTY RECEIPT — DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013)
  • Pass hand-delivered to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit

Exam Alert: OMNY passes are NOT valid on MTA express buses. All other subway/bus use is permitted. (P.G. 219-26)

Exam Alert: Passes are assigned to the individual member, NOT the command. Commands may NOT assign or reassign passes. (P.G. 219-26)

Exam Alert: Unauthorized person using an OMNY pass can be charged with theft of service and impersonation of a Police Officer. (P.G. 219-26)

Prior Test (2017 Lt. Exam): A MetroCard is lost or stolen — the Ops Coordinator investigates (per 219-20). A MetroCard is damaged — the ICO investigates (per 219-26). These are different investigators for different situations. (P.G. 219-20, 219-26)

See Also: P.G. 219-20 — Loss or Theft of Department Property: use this procedure for lost/stolen OMNY passes, with additional notifications to Transit Bureau Wheel and Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit.

P.G. 219-27: Radiation Detection Pagers — Command-Assigned

Date Effective: 11/18/21 | Last Revision: 1.0.110 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To provide for control, accountability, and issuance of radiation detection pagers assigned to commands.

Key Points

Item Detail
Assigned by Counterterrorism Central Resource Division through Citywide Counterterrorism Coordinator
Training ONLY trained supervisors/members may use pagers
Battery type AAA batteries (obtained from Quartermaster Section)
Deployment Every tour — pager(s) assigned to trained patrol supervisor/member
Transfer Commands NOT authorized to transfer pagers to other commands
Inspection Desk officer inspects/accounts for all pagers at start of each tour
Command Log Serial numbers and operating status recorded
If alarm activates Comply with P.G. 212-102
Lost/stolen Process per P.G. 219-20; notify Citywide Counterterrorism Office immediately

Repair/Replacement

  • Desk officer investigates and prepares Typed Letterhead report
  • Contact Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator for return/replacement instructions

P.G. 219-29: NYPD Restricted Parking Permits

Date Effective: 05/25/22 | Last Revision: 1.0.64 | Pages: 7

Definition

NYPD Restricted Parking Permit (Misc. 23-N) — Cardboard, approximately 4” × 10”, with serial number, command code, vehicle license plate number, and expiration date.

Eligibility Requirements

Requirement
Valid NYS driver’s license
Valid NYS vehicle registration
Valid insurance policy
Vehicle registered to member, blood/marriage relative, or registered NYS domestic partner
Owner’s residence matches member’s Department address of record

Ineligible Members/Vehicles

Ineligible
Member assigned Category I vehicle
Members at One Police Plaza with garage access (except Category II)
Members on Military & Extended Leave Desk or vacation/terminal leave pending retirement
Members under suspension
Auxiliary Police members
Vehicle with 5+ red light camera violations or 15+ school speed camera violations within 12 months

Application Schedule (By Borough)

Borough Member Submission Deadline ICO Delivery Deadline
Manhattan Last business day, 1st week of September 2nd week of September
Bronx Last business day, 2nd week of September 3rd week of September
Staten Island/Brooklyn Last business day, 3rd week of September 4th week of September
Queens Last business day, 4th week of September 1st week of October

ICO Background Checks

System Purpose
ZFINEST RALL NYS Vehicle Registration by Plate Number
ZFINEST DCID NYS Driver Inquiry (suspensions, revocations, scofflaw)
CPR Employee Inquiry Report Department personnel records
DAS Summons History Search by license plate
NYC Dept of Finance website Outstanding parking/photo enforcement fines

Key Restrictions

  • Permit may NOT be photocopied or reproduced — subject to disciplinary action
  • Permit may NOT be laminated
  • Valid ONLY for the vehicle whose license plate appears on the face
  • Non-transferable — exclusive use of assigned member
  • If vehicle changes, notify CO within 30 days; may use substitute vehicle for max 30 days

Parking Still Prohibited (With or Without Permit)

Double parking, fire hydrant/zone, bus stop/lane, sidewalk, crosswalks/driveways, obstructing traffic, no standing zones, snow emergency, no parking/taxi stand, bicycle lane, no permit zone

Exam Alert: Vehicle with 5+ red light camera or 15+ school speed camera violations in 12 months = ineligible.

Exam Alert: Restricted Parking Permits may NOT be photocopied, reproduced, or laminated.

Sergeant Focus: CO has final authority to approve or deny all Restricted Parking Permit applications.

P.G. 219-30: Radiation Detection Pagers — Personally Assigned

Date Effective: 11/18/21 | Last Revision: 1.0.110 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To provide for control, accountability, and issuance of radiation detection pagers permanently assigned to individual uniformed members.

Key Differences from Command-Assigned (219-27)

Item Detail
Assigned by Counterterrorism Central Resource Division after training completion
Assignment Permanently to individual member (not the command)
Documentation PROPERTY TRANSFER RECEIPT (PD521-145) — copy to CO and ICO
Serial number Listed on member’s FORCE RECORD
Transfer Commands NOT authorized to transfer between members
Carry requirement At all times when performing duty
Separation Surrender to desk officer for return to Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator

Exam Alert: Personally assigned radiation pagers must be carried at all times while performing duty and are listed on the member’s FORCE RECORD.

Section 219 — Fuel, Heating, and Facility Operations

P.G. 219-11: Computerized Fuel Dispensing

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To provide for the proper use of the computerized fuel dispensing system.

Vehicle Identification Box (VIB)

The VIB is the in-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing. Fuel is dispensed using card-based authorization.

Card Types

Card Color Purpose
Master Green Administrative control
CRALO Yellow Command Radio Auto Liaison Officer
Non-Fleet Orange Non-fleet vehicles
Private Red Private vehicles authorized for official use
Operator White Individual operator cards

Electric Vehicles

  • ChargePoint cards issued for electric vehicle charging
  • Strictly prohibited to charge personal vehicles using Department ChargePoint cards or stations

Exam Alert: It is strictly prohibited to charge personal vehicles at Department charging stations.

Exam Alert: Know the fuel card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator.

P.G. 219-12: Fuel Delivery Supervision

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To ensure proper supervision of fuel deliveries to Department facilities.

Key Points

Item Detail
Tank fill level 90% ullage amount (maximum fill capacity)
Monitoring Electronic tank monitor system
Supervision Delivery must be supervised by designated member

Exam Alert: Fuel tanks are filled to 90% ullage — never completely full.

P.G. 219-13: Heating Oil Delivery

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To ensure proper procedures for receiving heating oil deliveries.

Key Points

  • DCAS inspector must sign the vendor delivery ticket before accepting delivery
  • Do not accept delivery without DCAS inspector signature

Exam Alert: A DCAS inspector must sign the vendor delivery ticket before heating oil delivery is accepted.

P.G. 219-28: Emergency Power Outages

Date Effective: 01/18/22 | Last Revision: 1.0.4 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To address emergency power outages and ensure non-essential equipment is removed from emergency electrical receptacles.

Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Desk Officer/Supervisory Officer Immediately notify Building Maintenance Section (24/7)
1a Same Record notification in Telephone Record
2 Same (if generator activated) Survey command — ensure only essential emergency equipment and critical computer systems operate on emergency power
3 Same Have all non-essential equipment disconnected from emergency electrical receptacles

Non-Essential Equipment (Disconnect Immediately)

  • Air conditioners
  • Vending machines
  • Refrigerators
  • Microwaves

Emergency Receptacle Identification

  • Red cover plate over the receptacle, OR
  • Raised lettering on the outlet receptacle

Fuel Tank Responsibilities

Configuration Responsibility
Single fuel tank (heating + generator) Command maintains fuel level above 50%
Two fuel tanks (separate) Command maintains heating tank; Building Maintenance Section maintains generator tank

CO Quarterly Survey

  • Commanding officers conduct quarterly survey to ensure generator rooms, mechanical rooms, and electrical rooms are used only for their intended purpose — not as locker rooms, storage rooms, etc.
  • Items in these rooms may create a fire hazard

Exam Alert: Commands with a single fuel tank must maintain the level above 50%.

Exam Alert: Only qualified Building Maintenance Section personnel should start or repair the emergency generator. No outside agencies without BMS supervisor approval (absent exigent circumstances).

Sergeant Focus: CO must conduct a quarterly survey of generator/mechanical/electrical rooms to ensure proper use only.

Section 219 — Information Technology and Communications Equipment

P.G. 219-14: Computer Systems — Security and Integrity

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To establish security and integrity procedures for Department computer systems.

Member of the Service — Responsibilities

  • Sign on using confidential password; always sign off even if leaving for brief time
  • Strictly responsible for entries made while signed on
  • If password compromised/misused: notify D.O./designated supervisor OR ICO (comply with 207-21 Corruption)
  • Make only official inquiries relating to Department business
  • Do not divulge official business EXCEPT for valid law enforcement purpose (comply with 304-15 Confidentiality)

Desk Officer/Designated Supervisor — Responsibilities

  • Inspect all computer areas of stationhouse at least once a tour
  • Record results of inspection in Command Log
  • If inoperable/malfunction: notify ITB Service Desk and make Telephone Record entry
  • If misuse or damage: take immediate action AND notify ICO AND C.O.

Patrol Supervisor — Responsibilities

  • Check RMP computers frequently
  • If misuse/damage: take immediate action AND notify ICO AND C.O.
  • Record notifications in digital Activity Log

Exam Alert: For computer misuse or damage, both the Desk Officer and Patrol Supervisor must notify ICO AND C.O. — not just one or the other. (P.G. 219-14)

Integrity Control Officer (ICO) Responsibilities

Duty Detail
Monthly audit Conduct monthly audit of Domain Awareness System by randomly picking 5 MOS with access
Password management Issue passwords for OLBS, BADS, and LAPS through Omniform; establish and maintain list of ALL passwords issued
Authorized users Establish number of necessary authorized users; ensure users understand security guidelines; maintain sign-on tables
Monitor/observe Frequently observe computer areas/RMPs for compliance; investigate indications of abuse
Document Prepare Report on Typed Letterhead of audit results; Report (49) filed at command

Key Rules

  • Department reserves the right to review, intercept, access, and disclose ALL material on and use of Department computer, including EMAIL, with or without notice to member concerned
  • Members have no expectation of privacy on Department computer systems
  • Unauthorized access or use subject to disciplinary action

Exam Alert: The ICO must conduct a monthly DAS audit of 5 random members. The audit report (49) is filed at command — it goes nowhere else. (P.G. 219-14)

Exam Alert: The Department may review, intercept, and disclose computer activity with or without notice — this includes email. (P.G. 219-14)

Sergeant Focus: Department reserves the right to review all computer activity — members have no privacy expectation on Department systems.

Focused Audit

  • ICO or C.O. can request a focused audit of computer activity performed during a particular time period
  • Request via 49 to Deputy Commissioner of IT
  • Audits may include: MOS password, Tax #, time signed on/off, license checks, etc.

P.G. 219-15: Portable Radios

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To provide for the issuance, use, and accountability of portable radios.

Key Points

  • Maintain RADIO ASSIGNMENT LOG at each command
  • Radio must be carried in an authorized carrying case
  • Lost radio = negligence finding if not in authorized carrying case at time of loss

Notifications for Lost/Stolen Radio

Notify
Operations Unit
Electronics Section
Internal Affairs Bureau (IAB)
Stolen Property Inquiry Unit

Exam Alert: If a portable radio is lost and was not in an authorized carrying case, the member is deemed negligent.

Exam Alert: Four notifications required for lost/stolen radio: Operations Unit, Electronics Section, IAB, and Stolen Property Inquiry Unit.

P.G. 219-23: Solar Cellular Callboxes

Date Effective: 02/04/15 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To ensure all components of a solar cellular callbox are in place and functioning properly.

Callbox Components

  1. Antenna
  2. Solar panel
  3. Pole
  4. Sign — Emergency Phone
  5. Callbox
  6. Handset and cable (cord)

Procedure

Step Action
1 Respond as directed by radio dispatcher
2 Verify all 6 components are in place
3 Make test call to Communications Section (verify operability, callbox number, location)
4 Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT for vandalism/damaged/missing components, or MV104AN for vehicle collision damage

Notifications

Time Contact
Mon–Fri, 0800–1600 Telecommunications Unit
All other times ITB Wheel

P.G. 219-24: Cellular Telephones Assigned to Patrol Supervisor

Date Effective: 08/01/13 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To ensure proper utilization of and accountability for cellular telephones assigned to patrol supervisors.

Speed Dial Numbers

Button Connects To
1 Telephone switchboard
2 Desk
3 Patrol borough
4 Operations Unit
5 911

Key Points

  • Phones receive all incoming calls but only allow the 5 outgoing calls listed above
  • Desk officer ensures phones are operational each tour (batteries charged, car adapters available)
  • Command Log entry: name, telephone number, and assignment of supervisor with phone
  • Phones not assigned accounted for each tour with Command Log entry

P.G. 219-31: License Plate Reader Devices

Date Effective: 11/16/22 | Last Revision: 1.0.112 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To provide for control, use, maintenance, and accountability of License Plate Reader (LPR) devices.

Definition

LICENSE PLATE READER (LPR) — A specialized camera and computer that captures license plate numbers, converts them to text, and compares against a hot list (stolen, wanted, suspended registration/insurance, etc.).

Critical Operational Rules

Rule Detail
NYSPIN verification Absolutely essential — verify through NYSPIN before any law enforcement action
Not connected to NYSPIN LPR is NOT automatically connected to NYSPIN
State of registration LPR does NOT scan state of registration
Partial matches LPR will activate on partial scan match
Hot list currency Do NOT use if hot list not updated within 24 hours

Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Member Inspect LPR for operability at beginning of tour; report to desk officer
2 Member Activate LPR; record in Activity Log last hot list update time
4 Desk Officer Account for each LPR device at beginning of each tour; record in Command Log
5 Desk Officer Enter vehicle LPR assignment on CPR Roll Call System
6 Desk Officer Contact ITB Service Desk for LPR repairs
7 Member (on alarm) Verify through NYSPIN before any law enforcement action
8 Member Take law enforcement action (arrest, summons, rotation tow)

Restrictions

  • LPR used only for stolen/wanted files and furtherance of criminal investigations
  • Any other use is strictly prohibited
  • Camera lens cleaned only with glass cleaner applied with soft cloth
  • LPR equipment must not be moved from mounting location
  • Vehicle does not go out of service for LPR repair

Exam Alert: ALWAYS verify through NYSPIN before any law enforcement action based on an LPR alarm — the device activates on partial matches and is NOT connected to NYSPIN. (P.G. 219-31)

Exam Alert: The NYSPIN verification requirement has TWO exceptions: (1) additional reasons for a lawful stop exist (e.g., observed VTL infraction), OR (2) circumstances provide sufficient reasonable suspicion to justify a Terry Stop/Level 3 encounter. In those cases, NYSPIN check may be bypassed. (P.G. 219-31)

Exam Alert: An RMP does NOT go out of service just because the LPR is down. The vehicle continues patrol. (P.G. 219-31)

See Also: P.G. 221-15 — Pursuits: be guided by this procedure when taking enforcement action based on LPR hit.

Real Time Crime Center: Can be contacted to conduct a search of past records of license plate numbers searched by an LPR device.

P.G. 219-32: Department Mobile Digital Devices

Date Effective: 03/26/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 15 | Pages: 4

Definition

MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE — Any smartphone, tablet, or portable computer issued by the Department.

Key Rules for Members

Rule Detail
Official use only Use Department devices for official business exclusively
Personal devices Do NOT use personal devices for official business
On-duty personal phone NOT permitted during tour (except authorized meal or emergency)
Carry requirement Carry Department smartphone while on duty
NYPD Phone Sticker Affix NYPD PHONE STICKER (PD589-090) to phone case
No app installation Do NOT install any applications unless approved by Deputy Commissioner, IT
No content deletion Do NOT delete any content (texts, photos, videos, notes, voicemail)
Foreign travel Request ITB Information Security Desk permission 5 business days prior

Lost/Stolen/Damaged

  • Notify supervisor and ITB Telecommunications Unit immediately
  • Process per P.G. 219-20, Loss or Theft of Department Property
  • Complete DEPARTMENT MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE REPAIR/REPLACEMENT/RETURN REQUEST (PD589-163)

Off-Duty Considerations

  • Members may secure Department smartphone at command when off-duty
  • Members may carry phone off-duty and remove phone case (to not identify as MOS)
  • C.O. may approve removal of phone case with sticker if it would interfere with or be inappropriate for specific duty/assignment

NYPD Phone Sticker

  • All MOS must affix sticker to Department-issued phone case where it is clearly visible and identifiable
  • May remove case with sticker with C.O. approval

Privacy Notice

  • Members have no right to privacy in any feature of Department mobile devices
  • Department may access and retrieve at any time with or without the user’s prior knowledge
  • All communications, photos, video may be accessed by Department at any time
  • Content may be Rosario material and/or discoverable in criminal prosecutions, civil lawsuits, CCRB/IAB investigations, Department’s internal disciplinary process, and legal administrative proceedings

CCRB Requests for Smartphone Information

Step Action
1 UMOS notifies ICO
2 ICO contacts Legal Bureau for guidance
3 IAB’s CCRB Liaison processes requests from CCRB per P.G. 211-14

Exam Alert: CCRB information requests about smartphones go UMOS → ICO → Legal Bureau → IAB responds to CCRB. (P.G. 219-32)

Rosario/Discovery Requests

  • Department is required by law to disclose certain information pursuant to FOIL, subpoenas, and court orders
  • Legal Bureau’s Subpoena Litigation Unit responds to subpoenas per P.G. 211-17

Operational Considerations

  • Members are permitted but not required to photograph/video record encounters with public
  • Shall NOT record situations where BWC use would be prohibited
  • Shall NOT document demonstrations or political activity (unless per P.G. 212-71, 212-72)
  • Crime Scene Unit (CSU and ECT) remains solely responsible for documenting crime scenes unless authorized by competent authority
  • Do NOT save personal contacts, phone numbers, true names of MOS performing sensitive duties (undercovers, CIs), or any other information that could pose a safety risk

Information Relating to Arrests, Prosecutions, and Civil Matters

If MOS has knowledge of info, image, text, photo, video, note, calendar entry, or voicemail on any device relating to an arrest, prosecution, or civil matter, MOS MUST notify:

Matter Type Notify
Criminal (police incident or crime investigation) Assigned detective/investigator AND ADA
Civil Legal Bureau for potential or actual civil litigation
Family Court Prosecution Appropriate Assistant Corporation Counsel

Exam Alert: Do NOT document arrest/prosecution/civil matter information on a personal device. All such info must be shared — withholding is subject to discipline. (P.G. 219-32)

Exam Alert: Members must not delete any content from Department mobile devices. All content is potentially discoverable.

Exam Alert: Permission from ITB Information Security Desk required 5 business days before taking a Department phone to a foreign country. The request must list every foreign country you intend to visit.

Sergeant Focus: CO/designated supervisor must return Department smartphone to ITB when member has change in duty status (retired, suspended, modified).

Situational Awareness Note: Always maintain situational awareness and limit phone use in absence of another UMOS for tactical cover.

Section 219 — Loss or Theft of Department Property

P.G. 219-20: Loss or Theft of Department Property

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To properly report and investigate the loss or theft of Department property.

Procedure

Step Role Action
1 Member (UMOS) Report facts to Desk Officer
2 Desk Officer Notify Ops Coordinator AND Commanding Officer (does NOT say Duty Captain or ICO)
3 Operations Coordinator Conduct investigation
4 Operations Coordinator Make Command Log entry with facts and results
5 Operations Coordinator Prepare Report on Typed Letterhead (49) addressed to C.O.
6 Operations Coordinator Prepare Complaint Report; duplicate copies for IAB, Patrol/Transit/Housing Boro, Detective Borough, or bureau concerned
7 Operations Coordinator Notify IAB; put log # in details of 61
8 Operations Coordinator Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit — transmit alarm

Exam Alert: The Desk Officer notifies Ops Coordinator AND Commanding Officer — NOT the Duty Captain and NOT the ICO. (P.G. 219-20)

Prior Test (2017 Lt. Exam): Loss/theft of a MetroCard — the Ops Coordinator investigates. A damaged MetroCard — the ICO investigates (per P.G. 219-26). (P.G. 219-20, 219-26)

Property with Identifying Marks

If the property has identifying marks: - Notify SPIU to transmit alarm - If ID Plate lost/stolen: give plate number including letter P if appropriate, AND expiration of plate

Loss of Tire, Tool, etc. from Department Auto

  • Send 3 copies of 49 to C.O., Fleet Services Division

NYCT Police Pass (MetroCard) — Additional Notifications

Notify
Transit Bureau Wheel
Employee Resources Section (ERS)
  • Forward copy of 49 direct to C.O., Transit Bureau (but NOT the 61)
  • Forward copy of 49 AND Complaint Report to C.O., Employee Resources Section

LIRR or Metro North Pass — Lost/Stolen

  • Send 49 and 61 to C.O., Employee Resources Section (ERS)

Loss Occurring OUTSIDE the City

No Complaint Report (61) prepared when loss of NYCT, LIRR, or Metro North pass occurs outside the city.

Step Role Action
1 Member Notify Local PD AND Desk Officer at command of assignment
2 Desk Officer Make Command Log entry; notify Operations Coordinator
3 Ops Coordinator Investigate; notify IAB; prepare 49 addressed to C.O. (include IAB log #); forward as indicated
4 Ops Coordinator Take disciplinary action as appropriate

Exam Alert: If property loss occurs outside the city, there is no 61 (Complaint Report) prepared. (P.G. 219-20)

Precinct Squad Commander

  • Examines all 61s, including those NOT referred to the squad, including: police frequency radios, vehicles, uniforms, shields or ID cards, bulletproof vests, police-type emergency lights or sirens
  • Notifies C.O. Major Case by phone AND forwards a duplicate of the 61/Complaint Follow-Up with 49

Exam Alert: The Precinct Squad Commander reviews all 61s related to Department property — even those not referred to the squad. (P.G. 219-20)

NYCT OMNY Police Pass Procedures

When an OMNY Police Pass is lost or stolen, additional notifications are required: - Transit Bureau Wheel - Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit

Exam Alert: Lost/stolen Department property always requires IAB notification and a Stolen Property Inquiry Unit alarm. (P.G. 219-20)

Sergeant Focus: The Operations Coordinator conducts the investigation into loss or theft of Department property.

P.G. 219-21: Lost or Stolen Firearm, Shield, or Identification Card

Date Effective: 02/16/22 | Last Revision: 1.0.13 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To establish reporting and investigative procedures when a member’s firearm, shield, or identification card is lost or stolen.

UMOS — Immediate Actions

  • Notify Desk Officer, Pct of occurrence, immediately
  • Special county rules:
    • Loss in Nassau or Suffolk (Long Island): notify Desk Officer 105 Pct
    • Loss in Westchester, Putnam, Rockland, or Orange counties: notify Desk Officer 50 Pct
    • Loss outside residence counties (further away): notify Operations Unit DIRECT; local PD notified immediately and requested to send a FINEST notification to our Department
  • Prepare Part A of Loss Form (if out of city, D/O of 105 or 50 Pct or Operations Unit member does Part A)
  • Report loss to C.O. at first opportunity

Prior Test (recent exam): UMOS lost ID Card in resident county. Answer: Do NOT notify ICO, do NOT make 61, Desk Officer makes entry in Telephone Record. (P.G. 219-21)

Exam Alert: If loss occurs in Nassau/Suffolk, notify Desk Officer 105 Pct. If in Westchester/Putnam/Rockland/Orange, notify Desk Officer 50 Pct. Outside those, notify Operations Unit directly. (P.G. 219-21)

Desk Officer — Actions

Action Detail
Notify C.O./Duty Captain Immediately
Notify Operations Unit Immediately
Notify IAB Obtain log #; enter in details of 61 OR if no 61 (loss outside city), enter IAB log # in TELEPHONE RECORD
Document Make Command Log entry
Document Prepare Part B of Loss Form (and possibly Part A depending on circumstances)
Alarm (if NYC) Ensure alarm is transmitted if loss occurred within NYC
Complaint Report If occurred in NYC, ensure the Complaint Report is finalized

Exam Alert: If loss occurred outside the city, do NOT prepare a 61 AND do NOT transmit alarm. Instead, make Telephone Record and/or Command Log entry. (P.G. 219-21)

If member is from another command: Prepare and send Transcript of Command Log entry to member’s C.O.

Immediate Notification Chain

Step Role Action
1 Member Immediately report loss/theft to desk officer of pct of occurrence
2 Desk Officer Make Command Log entry
3 Desk Officer Notify C.O./Duty Captain (immediately) AND Operations Unit (immediately) AND IAB

LOST/STOLEN REPORT (PD520-150) — Parts

Part Prepared by Content
Part A MOS reporting loss (or D/O 105/50 or Ops Unit if out of city) Initial report of loss/theft
Part B D/O Pct of occurrence (or D/O 105/50 or Ops Unit member) Details of item lost/stolen
Part C C.O. / Duty Captain Investigation details; distributed per BOTTOM of form
Part D C.O. of MOS reporting loss Results of investigation, actions taken or contemplated; distributed per REAR of form

Exam Alert: C.O./Duty Captain distributes per the BOTTOM of the form. C.O. of MOS concerned distributes per the REAR of the form. These are different distribution instructions. (P.G. 219-21)

Exam Alert: If it’s the Duty Captain who completes Part C, a copy of the Command Discipline gets filed in the Duty Captain’s command. (P.G. 219-21)

Loss Outside the City — Summary

Loss Location Part A & B Prepared By
In NYC MOS reporting loss (Part A); D/O Pct of occurrence (Part B)
In resident counties (Nassau, Suffolk, Westchester, etc.) D/O 105 or 50 Pct does Parts A and B
Outside resident counties Operations Unit member does Parts A and B

Commanding Officer/Duty Captain — Investigation

  • Conduct immediate investigation
  • If out of city: call and speak to PD receiving report AND UMOS reporting loss
  • Prepare Part C with recommendation re: negligence; distribute per BOTTOM of form

Commanding Officer Actions (C.O. of MOS concerned)

Step Action
1 Determine if disciplinary action should be taken
2 IF FIREARM, MUST consult with Department Advocate first
3 Initiate CD or Charges if warranted; note disposition; file CD; copy to Advocate if B CD
4 Prepare Part D of Lost/Stolen Report; distribute per REAR of form
5 If FIREARM: have entry made on Force Record (10 card) with date/time of Command Log Entry (NOT date and time of loss)

Exam Alert: The 10 card Force Record entry uses the date/time of Command Log Entry — NOT the date and time of the actual loss. (P.G. 219-21)

Negligence Determination

  • CO must determine if loss resulted from negligence
  • If disciplinary action warranted, consultation with Department Advocate’s Office required to determine appropriate disciplinary action
  • IF FIREARM: Must consult with Advocate before initiating CD or charges

Member — After Investigation

  • Report to CO, Employee Resources Section to obtain new shield or ID card
  • Return to permanent command; request clerical member to enter new shield number on FORCE RECORD

Special Locations

Location Report To Who Investigates
Police Headquarters (5th Precinct confines) Headquarters Security Unit (5th records the 61) Captain or above from reporting member’s command
Police Academy (13th Precinct confines) Police Academy Integrity Unit (13th records the 61) Captain or above from reporting member’s command

Exam Alert: The PG says 13th Precinct for Police Academy — The Key notes the Department has still not corrected this even though the actual precinct is the 109th. Know the PG says 13th. (P.G. 219-21)

  • In both cases, a supervisor from HQ/PA makes notifications; a captain or above from HQ/PA investigates and prepares reports
  • COMPLAINT REPORT serial numbers obtained from the 5th or 13th Precinct, as appropriate

Shield Replacement — Timing Issue

  • If shield lost when Employee Resources Section is closed and member scheduled for uniform duty: member assigned to duty in uniform without a shield until ERS opens the next business day

Exam Alert: If loss occurred while ERS is closed and member is scheduled to work, they MAY work in uniform without a shield. (P.G. 219-21)

Auxiliary Police Shields

  • This procedure (219-21) does NOT apply — use P.G. 207-12 (Lost or Stolen Property/Identity Theft)
  • Additional copies to Auxiliary Police Section (Command Clerk puts Auxiliary Police Section in Additional Copies For caption)
  • Auxiliary shields recovered (not evidence) forwarded to CO, Auxiliary Police Section with a 49 — no PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE required

HQ/PA Assignment Special Rules

Assignment Action
Loser assigned to HQ AND lost in 5th Precinct confines Report to HQ Security Unit (5th records the 61)
Loser assigned to PA AND lost in 13th Precinct confines Report to PA Integrity Unit (13th records the 61)
Supervisor from HQ/PA Makes notifications
Captain or above from HQ/PA Investigates and prepares reports

Forms and Reports

Form Number
COMPLAINT REPORT PD313-152
FORCE RECORD PD406-143
IDENTIFICATION CARD PD416-091
LOST/STOLEN FIREARM/SHIELD/I.D. CARD REPORT PD520-150
PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE PD521-141
SUPERVISOR’S COMPLAINT REPORT/COMMAND DISCIPLINE ELECTION REPORT PD468-123

Exam Alert: When disciplinary action is warranted for loss of a firearm, consultation with the Department Advocate’s Office is required.

Exam Alert: At Police Headquarters or Police Academy, a captain or above from the member’s command conducts the investigation — not the local precinct.

Sergeant Focus: CO must determine negligence and consult Department Advocate before initiating discipline for lost/stolen firearms.

Section 219 — Vehicles and Fleet Operations

P.G. 219-01: Department Vehicles — Inspection at Start of Tour

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To inspect Department vehicles at the start of each tour to ensure safe and proper operation.

Vehicle Inspection Checklist

Item Check
Gasoline Adequate level
Brakes Functioning properly
Tires Proper inflation, condition
E-Z Pass tag Present, affixed to windshield
Mobility tablet Present, operational
License Plate Reader (LPR) Operational (if equipped)
Lights/siren Functioning
Radio Operational

Fluids inspected by operator: Gas, oil, engine coolant reservoir level, water in battery (if applicable).

Vehicle Equipment (all vehicles): Brakes, lights, wipers, warning devices, tire and spare are inflated, lug wrench/jack in trunk, seatbelts (in proper working order), seatbelt extenders.

Seatbelt Extenders: Operator checks that 2 seatbelt extenders have RMP # engraved AND are stored in glove box when not in use.

E-Z Pass Tag: Ensure affixed to windshield — top center. Enter serial number on INSPECTION AND EQUIPMENT RECORD.

Technology Equipment (if equipped): Vehicle printer, LPR, and Mobility Tablet (labeled and assigned to vehicle; CANNOT transfer to another vehicle; tech support from ITB Service Desk 24/7).

Registration and Inspection: NYS Inspection (all cars — marked and unmarked) AND NYS Registration (UNMARKED cars ONLY).

Records — Inspect the following 4 Records: - Inspection and Equipment Record - Radio Repair Record - Radio Code Signal Card - Deaf or Hard of Hearing Cards (Marked RMPs only)

Exam Alert: The weekly inspection (219-02) checks the INSPECTION AND EQUIPMENT RECORD but does NOT mention Radio Repair Record or Radio Code Signal Card — those are operator daily checks only. (P.G. 219-01, 219-02)

See Also: P.G. 202-02 — RMP Operator Step 3a: Inspect Ballistic Window Inserts and Window Insert Wedges and immediately report deficiencies to Patrol Supervisor.

Body of Water — Command versus Sector Distinction: - Command bordering OR containing a body of water: Life Ring with Rope attached (RMPs in command) - Sector with body of water: Two (2) vest type life jackets (if RMP assigned to sector with body of water)

Exam Alert: The life safety equipment requirement depends on whether the command borders water (life ring with rope) versus the sector having water (2 vest-type life jackets). These are different items for different situations. (P.G. 219-01)

Required Vehicle Equipment (Patrol)

Equipment
3-foot polycarbonate shield (with door bag — door rope/wedge affixed)
Ballistic window inserts and wedges (if so equipped)
2 heavy duty ballistic vests (if assigned to vehicle)
Patrol kit (PPE) — Marked RMPs only
Life ring with rope OR vest type life jackets (waterfront — see above)
Barrier tapes (Crime Scene and Police Line)
4 traffic cones
Safety flares (if assigned to vehicle)
Box of disposable gloves, First Aid Kit (both marked and unmarked)
2 disposable emergency blankets (marked/unmarked RMPs/vans)
Rechargeable flashlight in trunk
Life Hammer
Fire Extinguisher

Marked RMPs ONLY also carry: - Patrol Kit (PG 219-17) - Deaf or hard of hearing visor card - Deaf or hard of hearing instruction card

Patrol Supervisor Vehicle — Additional Equipment

Equipment
Rapid Mobilization Plan Dispatch Schedule
Command Disorder Control Plan
Detail Roster/Assignment Sheet
Crowbar
Sledgehammer
Restraining Straps
(5) Crime Scene Evidence Cones
(4) Waterproof Body Covers
Crime scene signs

Exam Alert: Crime Scene Evidence Cones (per P.G. 212-04) are orange, 5 inches tall, with black lettering NYPD. (P.G. 212-04)

Sergeant Focus: Patrol supervisor vehicles carry additional emergency/tactical equipment. Ensure these items are present and serviceable each tour.

Vehicle Operator — Post-Inspection Documentation

  • Enter results in Digital ACTIVITY LOG (COG): condition, odometer, and gas gauge reading
  • Report to Patrol Supervisor: condition of vehicle, missing equipment, and/or damaged equipment
  • Seatbelt issues to report: missing, damaged, defective seatbelts AND improper use (buckled behind seat, etc.)

Patrol Supervisor — Inspection Verification

  • Verify proper inspection and required entries made in digital A/L
  • Minor Deficiencies: Direct correction; follow up for compliance; enter results in digital A/L
  • Serious Deficiencies: Report to Lt. platoon commander OR, in the absence of Platoon Commander, report directly to C.O.
  • Vandalism: Report all vandalism to vehicle/equipment to IAB

Exam Alert: In the absence of the Platoon Commander, the Patrol Supervisor reports serious vehicle deficiencies directly to the C.O. — not to the Desk Officer. (P.G. 219-01)

See Also: P.G. 217-06 (Add Data) — Vandalism to Department Vehicle with no actor ID: report on Typed Letterhead to Fleet Services Division; deliver report to borough service station with vehicle.

Equipment Borrowed by Another Command: Desk Officer must verify identity of that member and make appropriate Command Log entries.

Commanding Officer — Equipment Accountability

  • Engrave Department Equipment: Use Department-issued engraving tool to mark crowbar, sledgehammer, and polycarbonate shield with command’s designation and identifying serial number (e.g., 104 Pct – 001)
  • Record/Document: Maintain a record of the equipment and the vehicle it is assigned to; refer to record during weekly vehicle inspections; ensure E-Z Pass serial number is included in maintained record

P.G. 219-02: Weekly Vehicle Inspections

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To ensure weekly accountability and condition reporting for all Department vehicles assigned to the command.

Key Points

  • Inspections conducted each Sunday after the second platoon has been posted (OR at other times as C.O. directs)
  • Lieutenant Platoon Commander performs the inspection; if unavailable, Patrol Supervisor performs it
  • Prepare VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT (PD571-153)
  • Record E-Z Pass tag serial numbers on the report

What the Weekly Inspection Checks (beyond daily): - Visual Message Signs (NOT mentioned in operator’s daily inspection) - Seatbelt extenders (with RMP # engraved; in glove box when not in use) - INSPECTION AND EQUIPMENT RECORD (does NOT include Radio Repair Record or Radio Code Signal) - NYS Inspection (ALL vehicles) and NYS Registration (UNMARKED only) - Ballistic Window Inserts and Window Insert Wedges - Mobility tablet, dock, and related equipment

Exam Alert: Weekly inspections occur on Sunday after the second platoon is posted. If the Platoon Commander is unavailable, the Patrol Supervisor conducts the inspection. (P.G. 219-02)

Exam Alert: Visual Message Signs are checked during the weekly inspection but are NOT part of the operator’s daily (start of tour) inspection. (P.G. 219-02)

Weekly Inspection — Role Comparison

Role Weekly 219-02 Duty
Lt. Platoon Commander / Patrol Supervisor Inspect each vehicle; prepare Vehicle Inspection Report; submit to Plt. Com or D/O
Lt. Platoon Commander / Desk Officer Schedule preventive maintenance (direct MOS to log on to Support Service Bureau’s online app); report serious deficiencies to C.O.; deliver Vehicle Inspection Report to C.O. for signature and file

Exam Alert: The Vehicle Inspection Report goes to the C.O. for signature — not simply filed by the platoon commander. (P.G. 219-02)

Daily vs. Weekly Inspection — Role Comparison

Action Daily (219-01) Weekly (219-02)
Who inspects vehicles Operator (UMOS) Lt. Platoon Commander (or Patrol Supervisor)
Serious deficiency goes to Patrol Supervisor → Platoon Commander (or C.O.) C.O. directly
Radio records checked Yes (Repair Record, Code Signal Card) No
Visual Message Signs No Yes
Report prepared Digital A/L entry Vehicle Inspection Report (PD571-153)

P.G. 219-03: Vehicle Maintenance

Date Effective: 12/31/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 78 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To ensure Department vehicles receive timely maintenance and repairs.

Key Points

  • Use Fleet Services Division online application to request maintenance
  • Roadside repair service available for breakdowns
  • Mobility tablet support provided by Information Technology Bureau (ITB)

P.G. 219-04: Car Wash

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To provide guidelines for the washing of Department vehicles.

Key Points

  • Competitive bidding required for car wash services
  • Prepare CAR WASH AUTHORIZATION (PD171-051)
  • EXPENSE REPORTS forwarded to Audits and Accounts by the 10th of each month

Exam Alert: Car wash expense reports are due to Audits and Accounts by the 10th of each month.

P.G. 219-05: Tire Repair

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To provide for the repair of tires on Department vehicles.

P.G. 219-06: Tire Replacement

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To provide for the replacement of tires on Department vehicles.

P.G. 219-07: Tire Station Removal

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To provide for the removal of tires at designated tire stations.

P.G. 219-08: Tire Chains

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To provide for the installation and use of tire chains on Department vehicles.

Key Rules

Rule Detail
Availability period November 1 through March 15
Installation threshold NOT installed until more than 4 inches of snow
Front-wheel drive restriction Tire chains are NOT installed on front-wheel drive vehicles

Exam Alert: Tire chains are NOT installed until there is more than 4 inches of snow and are NOT used on front-wheel drive vehicles.

P.G. 219-09: Department Tow

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To provide for the proper use of Department tow vehicles.

Authorized Uses for Department Tow

Use
Tow inoperable Department vehicle
Tow evidence vehicle
Clear path for parade/emergency
Assist Emergency Service Unit (ESU)
Lift heavy articles

Exam Alert: Department Tow has limited authorized uses — it is not a general-purpose tow service.

P.G. 219-10: Unmarked Vehicle Utilization Record

Date Effective: 05/20/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 31 | Pages: 2

Purpose

To account for the utilization of unmarked Department vehicles.

Vehicle Categories

Category Description
General Purpose Standard unmarked vehicles for routine operational use
Special Purpose Vehicles assigned for specific operational needs
Category I Vehicles not required to be stored at Department facility; permitted to be removed from the City on a regular basis; specifically designated by the Police Commissioner

Exam Alert: Category I vehicles are specifically designated by the Police Commissioner and may leave the city regularly. Members with Category I vehicles are ineligible for Restricted Parking Permits. (P.G. 219-10)

Exam Alert: Category I vehicles use velcro strips (removable) to secure E-Z Pass tags, since they routinely leave the city. Standard vehicles use permanently affixed double-sided tape. (P.G. 219-25)

Section 219 — Key Terms, Timeframes, and Forms

Key Definitions

Term Definition
Category I Vehicle Unmarked vehicle not required to be stored at Department facility; permitted to be removed from the City regularly; designated by the Police Commissioner
Category II Vehicle Unmarked vehicle with specific designations (exempt from OPP garage parking ineligibility)
General Purpose Vehicle Standard unmarked vehicle for routine operational use
Special Purpose Vehicle Unmarked vehicle assigned for specific operational needs
License Plate Reader (LPR) Specialized camera/computer that captures license plate numbers, converts to text, compares against hot list; NOT connected to NYSPIN; activates on partial matches
Mobile Digital Device Any smartphone, tablet, or portable computer issued by the Department
Vehicle Identification Box (VIB) In-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing
NYPD Restricted Parking Permit (Misc. 23-N) Cardboard parking credential (~4”×10”) with serial number, command code, license plate number, and expiration date
NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Pass Transit pass for subway/bus access (not express bus); valid ~7 years; assigned to individual member by unique serial number
Radiation Detection Pager Device assigned to commands or individual members for detecting radiological threats; requires specific training
Ullage Remaining capacity in a fuel tank; tanks filled to 90% ullage
Regulated Infectious Waste Biohazard materials picked up by Medical Division messenger
OLBS Online Booking System — one of the systems ICO issues passwords for through Omniform
BADS Booking and Arraignment Desk System — ICO issues passwords through Omniform
LAPS LAPS system — ICO issues passwords through Omniform
VIB Vehicle Identification Box — in-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing
CRALO Command Radio Auto Liaison Officer — holder of yellow fuel card
ERS Employee Resources Section — where member reports for replacement shield/ID after loss
SPIU / Stolen Property Inquiry Unit Unit notified to transmit alarm for lost/stolen Department property with identifying marks
ITB Information Technology Bureau — provides Mobility Tablet tech support 24/7; handles LPR repairs; telecommunications unit handles lost/stolen mobile devices
COG Change of Gear — the Digital Activity Log entry documenting vehicle inspection results (condition, odometer, gas gauge)
DAS Domain Awareness System — ICO audits 5 random members monthly for compliance
Focused Audit An audit of computer activity during a specific time period, requested by ICO or C.O. via 49 to Deputy Commissioner of IT

Fuel Card Colors

Card Color
Master Green
CRALO Yellow
Non-Fleet Orange
Private Red
Operator White

Critical Timeframes

Timeframe Rule Source
Each tour Inspect Department vehicle; account for radiation detection pagers; account for LPR devices 219-01, 219-27, 219-31
Each Sunday Weekly vehicle inspection after second platoon posted 219-02
10th of month Car wash expense reports due to Audits and Accounts 219-04
Nov 1 – Mar 15 Tire chains available 219-08
>4 inches snow Threshold for installing tire chains 219-08
24 hours LPR hot list must be updated within this period to use device 219-31
5 business days Request ITB permission before taking mobile device to foreign country 219-32
30 days Notify CO of vehicle change for Restricted Parking Permit; max time to use substitute vehicle 219-29
7 years General validity period for NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Pass 219-26
Monthly ICO audits DAS usage for 5 random members 219-14
Quarterly CO surveys generator/mechanical/electrical rooms 219-28
50% Minimum fuel tank level for commands with single tank (heating + generator) 219-28
90% Maximum fill level (ullage) for fuel deliveries 219-12
12 months Period for counting red light (5+) or school speed (15+) camera violations for permit ineligibility 219-29

Restricted Parking Permit Ineligibility Triggers

Trigger
Assigned Category I vehicle
OPP garage access (except Cat II)
Military & Extended Leave / vacation-terminal leave pending retirement
Under suspension
Auxiliary Police
5+ red light camera violations in 12 months
15+ school speed camera violations in 12 months
Vehicle in judgment for outstanding fines
Invalid license, registration, or insurance

Parking Always Prohibited (Even With Permit)

Violation
Double parking
Fire hydrant/zone
Bus stop/lane
Sidewalk
Crosswalks/driveways
Obstructing traffic
No standing zones
Snow emergency
Taxi stand
Bicycle lane
No Permit Zone

E-Z Pass — Authorized vs. Unauthorized Toll Crossings

Authorized Unauthorized
Bronx-Whitestone Bridge NJ Turnpike
George Washington Bridge NY State Thruway
Bayonne Bridge Garden State Parkway
Henry Hudson Bridge New England Thruway
Holland/Lincoln Tunnels Tappan Zee Bridge
Queens Midtown Tunnel (list not all-inclusive)
Throgs Neck Bridge
Verrazano-Narrows Bridge
Hugh L. Carey Tunnel
RFK Bridge

Key Forms

Form Number Used For
VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT PD571-153 Weekly vehicle inspection
CAR WASH AUTHORIZATION PD171-051 Car wash services
RADIO ASSIGNMENT LOG — Tracking portable radio assignments
LOST/STOLEN FIREARM/SHIELD/I.D. CARD REPORT PD520-150 Parts A–D for reporting loss/theft
SUPERVISOR’S COMPLAINT REPORT/COMMAND DISCIPLINE ELECTION REPORT PD468-123 Disciplinary findings
COMPLAINT REPORT PD313-152 Lost/stolen/damaged property
FORCE RECORD PD406-143 Member records (shield number, serial numbers)
PROPERTY TRANSFER RECEIPT PD521-145 Personally assigned radiation pagers
RESTRICTED PARKING PERMIT APPLICATION PD671-041 Parking permit request
NYPD PHONE STICKER PD589-090 Affixed to Department phone case
DEPARTMENT MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE REPAIR/REPLACEMENT/RETURN REQUEST PD589-163 Phone repair/replacement
PROPERTY RECEIPT — DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE PD520-013 Separation from service property return

Lost/Stolen Firearm — County Notification Rules

Location of Loss Who to Notify First
Within NYC Desk Officer, Pct of occurrence
Nassau or Suffolk County Desk Officer 105 Pct
Westchester, Putnam, Rockland, or Orange Desk Officer 50 Pct
Outside residence counties Operations Unit DIRECT

Lost/Stolen Firearm/Shield/ID — Report Parts

Part Prepared By Distribution
Part A MOS reporting loss —
Part B D/O Pct of occurrence —
Part C C.O. / Duty Captain Per BOTTOM of form
Part D C.O. of MOS concerned Per REAR of form

Key Contact Units

Unit When to Contact
Fleet Services Division Vehicle maintenance, E-Z Pass reassignment, defective tags; loss of tire/tool from dept auto (3 copies of 49)
Building Maintenance Section (24/7) Emergency power outages — immediately
ITB Service Desk (24/7) LPR repairs, Mobility Tablet support
ITB Telecommunications Unit Lost/stolen/damaged mobile devices; callbox issues (Mon–Fri 0800–1600)
ITB Information Security Desk Foreign travel with Department phone (5 business days prior; list every country)
ITB Wheel Callbox issues after hours
Operations Unit Lost/stolen portable radio; also notified immediately for lost/stolen firearm/shield/ID card
Electronics Section Lost/stolen portable radio
Internal Affairs Bureau Lost/stolen Department property; lost/stolen firearm/shield/ID
Stolen Property Inquiry Unit (SPIU) Lost/stolen Department property alarms; property with identifying marks
Transit Bureau Wheel Lost/stolen OMNY pass; also for lost/stolen MetroCard (NYCT Police Pass)
Employee Resources Section (ERS) Lost/stolen MetroCard (NYCT Police Pass) — forward 49 and Complaint Report; replacement shield/ID card
Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit OMNY pass issues; damaged/defective/lost/stolen passes
Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator Radiation pager repair/replacement instructions
Citywide Counterterrorism Office Lost/stolen radiation pager — notify immediately
Chief of Department’s Vehicle Identification Unit Restricted Parking Permit applications/returns
Department Advocate’s Office Consultation required before disciplinary action for lost/stolen FIREARM
Employee Resources Section Replacement shield/ID card after loss; also for LIRR/Metro North pass issues
Medical Division Regulated infectious waste pickup
Quartermaster Section AAA batteries for radiation pagers
DCAS Inspector Must sign heating oil delivery ticket
HQ Security Unit Loss occurs in 5th Precinct confines — reports loss here (5th records the 61)
PA Integrity Unit Loss occurs in 13th Precinct confines — reports loss here (13th records the 61)
Legal Bureau ICO contacts for CCRB smartphone requests; also handles Rosario/Discovery requests
Real Time Crime Center Can search past records of license plates queried by LPR
Telecommunications Unit Callbox notification Mon–Fri 0800–1600

Section 219 — Review Questions

Instructions

Select the best answer for each question. Answers are provided after each question.

1. Under P.G. 219-08, tire chains should NOT be installed on Department vehicles until there is more than how many inches of snow?

  1. 2 inches
  2. 3 inches
  3. 4 inches
  4. 6 inches
Answer

C) 4 inches

Tire chains are NOT installed until there is more than 4 inches of snow. Additionally, tire chains are NOT installed on front-wheel drive vehicles. (P.G. 219-08)

2. Which color fuel card is designated for the CRALO?

  1. Green
  2. White
  3. Yellow
  4. Orange
Answer

C) Yellow

Card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator. (P.G. 219-11)

3. How often must the Integrity Control Officer audit the Domain Awareness System (DAS) and how many members must be reviewed?

  1. Weekly, 3 random members
  2. Monthly, 5 random members
  3. Quarterly, 10 random members
  4. Semi-annually, 5 random members
Answer

B) Monthly, 5 random members

The ICO conducts a monthly DAS audit reviewing 5 random members. (P.G. 219-14)

4. If a portable radio is lost and the member was NOT carrying it in an authorized carrying case, the finding will be:

  1. No fault
  2. Accidental loss
  3. Negligence
  4. Misconduct
Answer

C) Negligence

If a portable radio is lost and was not in an authorized carrying case at the time, the member is deemed negligent. (P.G. 219-15)

5. Which of the following is NOT a required notification when a portable radio is lost or stolen?

  1. Operations Unit
  2. Electronics Section
  3. Fleet Services Division
  4. Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
Answer

C) Fleet Services Division

The four required notifications are: Operations Unit, Electronics Section, Internal Affairs Bureau, and Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. Fleet Services Division is not notified for radio losses. (P.G. 219-15)

6. Weekly vehicle inspections are conducted on what day and after which platoon has been posted?

  1. Saturday, after the first platoon
  2. Sunday, after the second platoon
  3. Monday, after the first platoon
  4. Friday, after the third platoon
Answer

B) Sunday, after the second platoon

The lieutenant platoon commander/patrol supervisor inspects all Department vehicles each Sunday after the second platoon has been posted. (P.G. 219-02)

7. Regulated infectious waste from patrol kits is picked up by:

  1. Department of Sanitation
  2. Medical Division messenger
  3. FDNY Hazmat
  4. Quartermaster Section
Answer

B) Medical Division messenger

The Medical Division messenger picks up regulated infectious waste from the 32-gallon biohazard receptacle at the command. (P.G. 219-17)

8. Before a law enforcement action is initiated based on a License Plate Reader (LPR) alarm, the operator must:

  1. Notify the patrol supervisor
  2. Contact the Real Time Crime Center
  3. Verify the vehicle status through NYSPIN
  4. Photograph the license plate
Answer

C) Verify the vehicle status through NYSPIN

It is absolutely essential that the LPR operator verify the current status of a vehicle through NYSPIN prior to initiating any law enforcement action. The LPR is NOT connected to NYSPIN and activates on partial scan matches. (P.G. 219-31)

9. NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes are NOT valid for use on which type of transit?

  1. Subway
  2. Local bus
  3. MTA express bus
  4. Staten Island Railway
Answer

C) MTA express bus

OMNY Police Passes are not valid for use on MTA express buses. Besides this exception, they have no electronic restrictions and may be used on subways and public buses. (P.G. 219-26)

10. A vehicle with how many red light camera violations within a twelve-month period will be ineligible for a Restricted Parking Permit?

  1. 3 or more
  2. 5 or more
  3. 10 or more
  4. 15 or more
Answer

B) 5 or more

Vehicles with 5 or more red light camera violations OR 15 or more school speed camera violations within a twelve-month period are ineligible. (P.G. 219-29)

11. Commands with a single fuel tank (heating and generator) must maintain the fuel level above what percentage?

  1. 25%
  2. 50%
  3. 75%
  4. 90%
Answer

B) 50%

Commands with a single fuel tank must maintain the level above 50%. Commands with two separate tanks are responsible only for the heating tank; Building Maintenance Section maintains the generator tank. (P.G. 219-28)

12. When a member’s firearm is lost and disciplinary action is warranted, the commanding officer must consult with:

  1. Internal Affairs Bureau
  2. Legal Bureau
  3. Department Advocate’s Office
  4. Chief of Department
Answer

C) Department Advocate’s Office

When disciplinary action is warranted for loss of or failure to safeguard a firearm, a consultation with the Department Advocate’s Office is required to determine appropriate disciplinary action. (P.G. 219-21)

13. A member’s firearm, shield, or ID card is lost within Police Headquarters (5th Precinct). The investigation will be conducted by:

  1. The 5th Precinct commanding officer
  2. A captain or above from the reporting member’s command
  3. The Headquarters Security Unit
  4. Internal Affairs Bureau
Answer

B) A captain or above from the reporting member’s command
