Resignation bars reinstatement EXCEPT if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year of date of resignation.

CO Duties

Step Action
2 Instruct member that resignation bars reinstatement (except Police Commissioner approval within 1 year)
3 Request member complete RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW (PD452-151)
4 Interview member and record responses
5 Sign and distribute RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within 5 business days
6 Have command clerk prepare PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013)
7 Sign PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE

Desk Officer Duties

Step Action
8 Make Command Log entry of resignation
9 Obtain NYC Transit OMNY Police Pass, LIRR/Metro-North passes
10 Notify Operations Unit of resignation — provide 8 items of information
11 Notify Occupational Safety and Health Section if member indicated feeling unsafe

Operations Unit Notifications (Upon Learning of Resignation)

Notified
Communications Section (FINEST Message)
Internal Affairs Bureau
Payroll Section
Police Pension Fund
Human Resources Division

Last Working Day

Rule
Assign member to 2nd platoon on last working day
If last day falls on Saturday/Sunday → report to PPF on Friday; perform clerical duty in command on Saturday/Sunday
Report to PPF on last working day in civilian clothes
Deliver firearms to borough Property Clerk’s Office ONLY

If Member Resigns Other Than In Person

Step Action
43 Notify Operations Unit
44 Notify permanent command
45 C.O., Personnel Orders Division: mail certified letter acknowledging receipt and acceptance
46 Attempt personal communication to determine location of property/firearms
49 Assign sergeant and clerical member to open locker and obtain Department property

Vacation Accrual Note

Rule
Members in rank of PO through deputy chief receive entire vacation allotment on January 1
Vacation time only accrued while in full pay status
Members who resign, are terminated, or take leave without pay — vacation accruals are prorated
Using vacation in excess of accrual rates prior to resigning → required to forfeit pay and/or reimburse Department
Resigning in good standing after 1 year of service → compensated for remaining accrued time

Exam Alert — Resignation Bars Reinstatement: Resignation bars reinstatement EXCEPT if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year. This is a critical exam distinction — the one-year window is the only exception, and only the Police Commissioner (not the Chief, not the First Deputy) can grant it.

Exam Alert — Sergeant + Clerical for Absent Resignee: When a member resigns other than in person, the desk officer assigns a sergeant and clerical member (both required) to open the locker and obtain Department property. This is frequently tested as a which rank question — the answer is always sergeant AND clerical.

Exam Alert — Last Working Day = 2nd Platoon, Civilian Clothes at PPF: On the last working day, assign the resigning member to the 2nd platoon. The member reports to PPF in civilian clothes (not uniform). Firearms go to borough Property Clerk’s Office only. These three details (2nd platoon, civilian clothes, borough PC) are frequently tested.

Section 329 — Key Terms and Definitions

Retirement Definitions

Term Definition
Terminal Leave Leave with pay computed at PPF; 1 calendar month per 10 years + 3 days per year beyond 20 + 1 day per 4 months
LEOSA Certification Card Wallet-sized card exempting retired LEOs from local/state concealed carry prohibitions; must accompany retired ID card
Vested Interest Discontinuance of police service with pension benefit eligibility but not full retirement
Resignation Voluntary separation other than retirement/vested interest
Dismissal Probation Period included in negotiated penalties for alcohol-related motor vehicle incidents
NICS Check National Instant Criminal Background Check — required to retain post-1/15/13 Department-issued firearms

Key Forms

Form Purpose
PROPERTY RECEIPT - DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013) Property return documentation for retiring/resigning members
PENSION APPLICATION (PD440-045) Grants terminal leave
RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW (PD452-151) Completed and signed by resigning member
PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE (PD521-141) Lists firearms safeguarded
ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS (PD424-150) Report to NY State Police for each firearm
HANDGUN LICENSE APPLICATION (PD643-041) Typed and notarized; for NYC residents applying for handgun license upon retirement

Key Timeframes

Item Timeframe
Tier 2 reporting to PPF At least 30 calendar days in advance
Tier 3 reporting to PPF May report without notice
Terminal leave PPF reporting Requested 10 days prior (not required)
LEOSA card expiration 12 months from last range requalification
NICS check Within 30 days prior to separation
RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW distribution Within 5 business days
Reinstatement after resignation Only by Police Commissioner within 1 year
Tier 2 mandatory retirement age 63
Tier 3 mandatory retirement age 62
Tier 3 retired ID/LEOSA issuance 30 days after discontinuance
Terminal leave discontinuance — vacation eligibility Not for 30 days after

Terminal Leave Restrictions

Restriction
No leaves of any kind after terminal leave begins
No sick report (unless LOD action while on terminal leave)
Excluded from excusal limitations
Available only for emergency situations
Cash overtime only if ordered to work
No programmatic overtime, fill details, or non-emergency duties (without bureau chief/DC approval)

Resignation Key Rules

Rule
Bars reinstatement except by Police Commissioner within 1 year
Benefits/pension contributions NOT returned until ALL property accounted for
Police Commissioner decides if resignation is without permission
Firearms delivered to borough Property Clerk’s Office ONLY
Report to PPF on last working day in civilian clothes
Sergeant and clerical member assigned to open locker of absent resignee

Memory Aids

Memory Aid — TERMINAL LEAVE MATH: 1 month per 10 years + 3 days per year beyond 20 + 1 day per 4 months partial year. Example: 23 years = 2 months (20 years) + 9 days (3 × 3 years) = 2 months + 9 days terminal leave.

Memory Aid — TIER 2 vs TIER 3 RETIREMENT AGE: Tier 2 = 63rd birthday, Tier 3 = 62nd birthday. Think: 2 has 3 digits (63), 3 has 2 digits (62) — or: Tier 2 gets the higher age (63), Tier 3 gets the lower age (62).

Memory Aid — TIER 2 vs TIER 3 PPF NOTICE: Tier 2 = 30 days advance notice (Tier Two = Takes Time, needs 30 days). Tier 3 = No notice required (Three = Free, no notice).

Memory Aid — RESIGNATION REINSTATEMENT: Only Police Commissioner can reinstate, and only within 1 year. Think: PC = Police Commissioner = Person who has Complete authority.

Memory Aid — FIREARMS UPON RESIGNATION: Deliver to borough Property Clerk’s Office ONLY (Resigning = Borough Property Clerk, not Manhattan — actually both Manhattan AND borough PC deliver, but the key exam point is borough PC is where resignations go, not the member’s choice). LEOSA holders don’t deliver firearms.

Section 329 — Review Questions

Multiple Choice

1. Terminal leave for a uniformed member with 20 years of service is computed as:

    1. 30 calendar days
    1. One calendar month per 10 years of service
    1. Two calendar months
    1. 60 calendar days
Answer

B) One calendar month per 10 years of service

One calendar month (28, 30, or 31 days depending on the month) of leave is granted for each 10 years of service. Beyond 20 years, 3 days per completed year and 1 day per completed 4 months. (A.G. 329-06)

2. A Tier 2 uniformed member must report to the Police Pension Fund:

    1. At least 10 calendar days in advance
    1. At least 30 calendar days in advance
    1. Without notice
    1. At least 60 calendar days in advance
Answer

B) At least 30 calendar days in advance

Tier 2 members must report at least 30 calendar days in advance of discontinuance of service. Tier 3 members may report without notice. (A.G. 329-06)

3. A LEOSA Certification Card expires:

    1. 6 months from date of last range requalification
    1. 12 months from date of last range requalification
    1. 24 months from date of retirement
    1. It does not expire
Answer

B) 12 months from date of last range requalification

The LEOSA Certification Card will expire 12 months from the date of the member’s last range requalification. The member must have qualified during a regular Department range cycle within the past 12 months. (A.G. 329-06)

4. After commencement of terminal leave, which of the following may occur?

    1. Member may be granted vacation
    1. Member may be placed on sick report for any illness
    1. No leaves of any kind may be granted
    1. Member may request comp time
Answer

C) No leaves of any kind may be granted

No leaves of any kind may be granted after commencement of terminal leave, nor may the member be placed on sick report, unless such sick report results from action taken in the line of duty while on terminal leave. (A.G. 329-06)

5. Resignation bars reinstatement EXCEPT if approved by:

    1. The Chief of Department within 6 months
    1. The Police Commissioner within one year
    1. The First Deputy Commissioner within 2 years
    1. The Chief of Personnel within 90 days
Answer

B) The Police Commissioner within one year

Resignation bars reinstatement except if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year of date of resignation. (A.G. 329-07)

6. When a UMOS resigns, the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW form must be distributed within:

    1. 24 hours
    1. 3 business days
    1. 5 business days
    1. 10 business days
Answer

C) 5 business days

The commanding officer signs and distributes the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within five business days. (A.G. 329-07)

7. A resigning member’s firearms must be delivered to:

    1. Manhattan Property Clerk
    1. Firearms and Tactics Section
    1. Borough Property Clerk’s Office only
    1. The member’s permanent command
Answer

C) Borough Property Clerk’s Office only

The resigning member must deliver firearms with PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE and ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS report to the borough Property Clerk’s Office ONLY. (A.G. 329-07)

8. Tier 2 uniformed members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their:

    1. 60th birthday
    1. 62nd birthday
    1. 63rd birthday
    1. 65th birthday
Answer

C) 63rd birthday

Tier 2 members are required by law to retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 63rd birthday. Tier 3 members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 62nd birthday. (A.G. 329-06)

9. Members on terminal leave are available for duty only during:

    1. Regular scheduled tours
    1. Emergency situations
    1. Programmatic overtime
    1. Detail assignments
Answer

B) Emergency situations

Members on terminal leave, or using accrued time attached to terminal leave, are excluded from excusal limitations and are only available for duty during emergency situations (natural disaster, etc.) or court must-appear notifications. (A.G. 329-06)

10. A UMOS wishing to retain a Department-issued service firearm (issued after 1/15/13) must obtain a NICS check within how many days prior to separation?

    1. 15 days
    1. 30 days
    1. 60 days
    1. 90 days
Answer

B) 30 days

Members must obtain a NICS check through a Federal Firearms Licensee (FFL) no more than 30 days prior to separation from the Department. (A.G. 329-06)

11. If a UMOS resigns other than in person, who is assigned to open the member’s locker?

    1. The desk officer
    1. A sergeant and clerical member of the service
    1. The commanding officer personally
    1. Internal Affairs Bureau
Answer

B) A sergeant and clerical member of the service

The desk officer assigns a sergeant and clerical member of the service to open the member’s locker and obtain Department property. (A.G. 329-07)

12. Upon learning of a member’s resignation, the Operations Unit notifies all of the following EXCEPT:

    1. Communications Section
    1. Internal Affairs Bureau
    1. Payroll Section
    1. The member’s union representative
Answer

D) The member’s union representative

Operations Unit notifies: Communications Section (FINEST Message), Internal Affairs Bureau, Payroll Section, Police Pension Fund, and Human Resources Division. The union representative is not on this notification list. (A.G. 329-07)

13. A Departmental Recognition Committee must be composed of:

    1. 3 ranking officers and 2 nonsupervisory officers
    1. 2 ranking officers (one as chairperson) and 3 nonsupervisory officers
    1. 5 ranking officers
    1. 4 nonsupervisory officers and 1 ranking officer
Answer

B) 2 ranking officers (one as chairperson) and 3 nonsupervisory officers

Departmental Recognition Committees are composed of five uniformed members: two ranking officers (one designated as chairperson) and three nonsupervisory officers (which may include a precinct detective squad member). (A.G. 329-03)

14. When multiple members are involved in the same incident, how many DEPARTMENTAL RECOGNITION REQUEST forms are required?

    1. One form listing all members
    1. One form per member
    1. One form per rank
    1. One form for up to 3 members
Answer

B) One form per member

A separate DEPARTMENTAL RECOGNITION REQUEST (PD439-162) must be prepared for each member of the service involved, even if they were part of the same incident. (A.G. 329-03)

15. When multiple members are involved in the same incident, the precinct/command serial number assigned should be:

    1. Different for each member
    1. The same for all members
    1. Sequential numbers for each member
    1. Assigned only to the ranking officer
Answer

B) The same for all members

If more than one member of the service is involved in the same incident, the same precinct/command serial number will be used for all requests. (A.G. 329-03)

16. Who is responsible for conducting the investigation of a Departmental Recognition Request?

    1. The precinct commander
    1. The operations coordinator
    1. A ranking officer assigned by the operations coordinator
    1. The Recognition Committee chairperson
Answer

C) A ranking officer assigned by the operations coordinator

The operations coordinator assigns the request to a ranking officer for investigation. The ranking officer conducts the investigation including checking records, interviewing members and witnesses, and obtaining signed statements. (A.G. 329-03)

17. Which of the following is NOT part of the ranking officer’s investigation of a recognition request?

    1. Checking Department records
    1. Interviewing the patrol supervisor
    1. Obtaining signed statements from witnesses
    1. Making the final approval decision
Answer

D) Making the final approval decision

The ranking officer conducts the investigation but does NOT make the final approval. The investigation includes: checking records, interviewing members (including patrol supervisor), interviewing witnesses, obtaining signed statements, and attaching all forms. The commanding officer makes the final approval. (A.G. 329-03)

18. To be eligible for Integrity Review Board recognition, the uniformed member must:

    1. Have effected any felony arrest
    1. Have been offered/accepted a bribe AND effected the arrest or charged the defendant with bribery
    1. Have witnessed another officer accepting a bribe
    1. Have made 10 or more integrity-related arrests in a year
Answer

B) Have been offered/accepted a bribe AND effected the arrest or charged the defendant with bribery

To receive recognition from the Integrity Review Board, the uniformed member must be the individual who was offered/accepted the bribe AND either effected the arrest for bribery or charged the defendant with the additional crime of bribery. (A.G. 329-03)

19. When the arresting officer is NOT the member offered the bribe, what is required?

    1. A separate recognition form
    1. An ARREST REPORT SUPPLEMENT delineating the circumstances
    1. Approval from the Chief of Department
    1. The arresting officer cannot receive recognition
Answer

B) An ARREST REPORT SUPPLEMENT delineating the circumstances

When the arresting officer is not the uniformed member offered the bribe, an ARREST REPORT SUPPLEMENT must be prepared delineating the circumstances surrounding the additional charge of bribery. (A.G. 329-03)

20. A retiring member must complete the pistol license application:

    1. Within 30 days after retirement
    1. Prior to their last tour of duty
    1. On their last tour of duty
    1. After obtaining the Certificate of Service
Answer

B) Prior to their last tour of duty

A uniformed member desiring a pistol license must complete the application prior to their last tour of duty. This includes preparing PD643-041, obtaining photographs, and getting fingerprints processed. (A.G. 329-08)

21. How many photographs are required for a NYC resident retiring member’s pistol license application?

    1. 2
    1. 3
    1. 4
    1. 6
Answer

B) 3

NYC residents require 3 head-type photographs (1½ inch square). Non-residents require 4 photographs. Photos must be taken within 30 days of application. (A.G. 329-08)

22. Where must a retiring member report for fingerprint processing?

    1. The borough Property Clerk
    1. The Payroll/Pension Section
    1. Identification Unit, Police Headquarters
    1. The precinct of occurrence
Answer

C) Identification Unit, Police Headquarters

The retiring member must report to the Identification Unit, Police Headquarters with fingerprints for processing. Forms used include PD423-144, NYS O13 OOIN card, and FBI FD258. (A.G. 329-08)

23. The Certificate of Service with medical endorsement is obtained from:

    1. The Identification Unit
    1. The Payroll/Pension Section
    1. The Police Pension Fund
    1. The Operations Coordinator
Answer

B) The Payroll/Pension Section

The retiring member reports to the Payroll/Pension Section to obtain the Certificate of Service with medical endorsement. Non-residents need an additional copy for their local police authority. (A.G. 329-08)

24. When requesting personnel transfers, the commanding officer must confer with:

    1. The Chief of Personnel
    1. Personnel Orders Division
    1. The borough commander
    1. The Civilian Employee Services Unit
Answer

B) Personnel Orders Division

The commanding officer must confer with Personnel Orders Division and document the results under Results of Conferral on the REQUEST FOR PERSONNEL (PD406-040) form. (A.G. 329-09)

25. Which form is used for Central Personnel Index Background Requests?

    1. PD406-040
    1. PD439-162
    1. PD449-160
    1. PD643-041
Answer

C) PD449-160

CENTRAL PERSONNEL INDEX BACKGROUND REQUEST (PD449-160) is prepared and distributed as indicated on the form to obtain background information on members being considered for specialized assignments. (A.G. 329-09)

26. What must be attached to the REQUEST FOR PERSONNEL (PD406-040)?

    1. The member’s training records
    1. The member’s last performance evaluation
    1. A copy of the member’s precinct assignment history
    1. The Recognition Committee recommendation
Answer

B) The member’s last performance evaluation

The commanding officer must obtain a copy of the member’s last performance evaluation and attach it to the REQUEST FOR PERSONNEL form. (A.G. 329-09)

Section 330 — Medical, Health and Wellness

Source

  • Administrative Guide Section: 330
  • Source PDFs: A.G. 330-03, A.G. 330-07, A.G. 330-09; P.G. 330-01, P.G. 330-02, P.G. 330-04, P.G. 330-16
  • Priority: Medium

Learning Objectives

  • Know the complete line of duty injury investigation and reporting process
  • Understand Sick Desk notification triggers for suspicious LOD claims
  • Master the hospital privacy rules for injured members
  • Know the Trauma Counseling Program scope, timeline, and confidentiality rules
  • Understand infectious disease exposure procedures, universal precautions, and decontamination
  • Know needle handling procedures and bleach solution protocols
  • Understand Regular Sick reporting (Sick Desk call, sick serial number, confinement rules)
  • Know Administrative Sick eligibility, 2-day limit, and LIMB LSS MD exclusions
  • Know court notification procedures when a member reports sick (Command Diary, Roll Call Clerk)
  • Understand Counseling Unit confidentiality exceptions, non-disciplinary referral protections, and EAU conferral requirements

Procedures

Procedure Title Status
A.G. 330-03 Line of Duty Injury or Death completed
A.G. 330-07 Trauma Counseling Program completed
A.G. 330-09 Exposure to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials completed
P.G. 330-01 Regular Sick completed
P.G. 330-02 Administrative Sick completed
P.G. 330-04 Notifying Court When Reporting Sick completed
P.G. 330-16 Counseling Unit completed

Chapter Files

File Content
A.G. 330-03: LOD injury investigation, IS rank requirements, 3 reports (PAR/AIDED/LODI), Sick Desk triggers, hospital privacy, MOS DOA/SILD permitted visitors (5-2-2-2), FECA
A.G. 330-07: Referral triggers, Trauma Team response, 48-hour follow-up, confidentiality, CO duties
A.G. 330-09: Universal precautions, exposure procedures, bleach solution ratio, needle handling, Ryan White Act
P.G. 330-01: Sick Desk call, 3 items received/1 given to D.O., 5 District Surgeon exceptions, confinement rules, travel restrictions, routine visit exemptions, malingering
P.G. 330-02: UMOS only, 2-day max, LIMB LSS MD exclusions, Admin vs Regular first-call distinction
P.G. 330-04: Command Diary entries, Roll Call Clerk duties, Operations Coordinator/PRAA duties, condition changes
P.G. 330-16: Confidentiality exceptions, non-disciplinary referral, misconduct not exempt, EAU conferral for alcohol/DV cases
LOD, trauma, infectious disease, regular sick, admin sick, court notification, counseling unit definitions; key forms; timeframes; bleach/needle rules
25 multiple-choice questions covering all seven procedures

Sergeant Focus Areas

  • LOD Injury Report: Sign off electronically before end of tour; include BWC/video findings
  • IS and 3 Reports: IS always prepares PAR, never prepares AIDED, sometimes prepares LODI
  • Out-of-City LOD: Investigating supervisor must be captain or above
  • Sick Desk triggers: Not witnessed, in/near police facility, first 2 hours of tour, off-duty request
  • Hospital privacy: Only Medical Division address in hospital records — never member’s personal info
  • MOS DOA/SILD visitors: 5-2-2-2 (5 supervisors, 2 investigators, 2 fraternal/religious, 2 conditional)
  • Trauma Counseling: 48-hour follow-up, strictly confidential, captain+ can request response
  • Infectious disease: Assign officer who already had contact; patrol supervisor coordinates for multiple exposures
  • Decontamination: Bleach ratio 1:10, freshly mixed, sink disposal only; one-handed needle scoop technique
  • Regular Sick: UMOS gets 3 items from Sick Desk but gives D.O. only the sick serial number
  • Admin Sick: UMOS only (no CMOS); max 2 days; call command first (not Sick Desk); LIMB LSS MD exclusions
  • Court notification: Command Diary under appearance date AND 2 days prior (excl. weekends/holidays)
  • Counseling Unit: Supervisors are vital link in detection/referral; non-disciplinary referral = no report, no promo impact; EAU conferral required before adjudication of alcohol/DV misconduct

Section 330 — Administrative Sick

P.G. 330-02: Administrative Sick

Applicability

  • UMOS only — Civilian members of the service (CMOS) cannot go Administrative Sick

Definition — Three-Day Progression

Day Status Requirements
1st Sick Day Admin Sick One (1) tour; call D.O./Supervisor at least 2 hours prior to start of tour; no visit to District Surgeon
2nd Sick Day Admin Sick If still sick next day, call D.O./Supervisor 2 hours prior to tour; no District Surgeon; counts as one (1) sick event with Day 1
3rd Sick Day Regular Sick Report Regular Sick; guided by Sick Desk regarding Medical Division visit to District Surgeon; consolidates into 1 sick event

Exam Alert: Admin Sick lasts a maximum of 2 days. On the 3rd day, it automatically converts to Regular Sick. All three days consolidate into one sick event.

When Admin Sick Is NOT Authorized

Memory Aid — LIMB LSS MD (A Limbless Doctor):

LIMB: - Line of Duty - Injury - Mutual (exchange) - Chronic B

LSS: - Late (less than 2 hours pre-tour) - Stress/psychological - Supervisor deems inappropriate

MD: - Major Illness - Dental

Exam Alert: The mnemonic LIMB LSS MD covers all situations where Admin Sick is not authorized. If any of these apply, the member must report Regular Sick instead.

UMOS Duties

Step Action
1 Notify D.O./Supervisor at assigned command by phone or in person at least 2 hours pre-tour
2 Inform D.O./Supervisor of any pending arraignment, court, trial room, or any government appearance
3 If command has no available supervisor or is otherwise closed: (a) call Sick Desk Supervisor to request Admin Sick, AND (b) notify command when it reopens

Exam Alert: For Admin Sick, the UMOS calls their command (D.O./Supervisor) — NOT the Sick Desk. For Regular Sick, the UMOS calls the Sick Desk first, then the command. This is a key distinction.

Key Recap: Admin vs. Regular Sick First Call

Admin Sick Regular Sick
Call Command (D.O./Supervisor) — not the Sick Desk Call Sick Desk first, then the command

Desk Officer / Supervisor / Sick Desk Supervisor Duties

Step Action
1 Query Medical Division Sick Reporting System to determine if UMOS is eligible for Admin
2 If eligible: place UMOS on Admin via Med Div Sick Reporting System and provide sick serial number
3 If not eligible: direct member to report Regular Sick
4 Comply with P.G. 330-04, Notify Court when MOS sick if appropriate

After Admin Sick Is Granted

  • Desk Officer and members follow all Regular Sick reporting, recording, notifications, and restrictions
  • This includes out-of-residence, out-of-city, out-of-resident-counties, out-of-state/country, malingering, etc.

See Also: P.G. 330-01 — Regular Sick (the full sick reporting procedure applies after Admin is placed).

See Also: P.G. 330-04 — Notifying Court When Reporting Sick.

Section 330 — Counseling Unit

P.G. 330-16: Counseling Unit

Confidentiality

  • Counseling Unit records are confidential
  • BUT may be disclosed with:
    • Written consent
    • Valid subpoena
    • U.S. District Court Order
    • Suspected child abuse, OR
    • If member is a danger to self or others

Exam Alert: Counseling Unit confidentiality has 4 exceptions beyond written consent: subpoena, U.S. District Court Order, suspected child abuse, and danger to self/others. Know all four.

Primary Function

  • Assist in recovery and return to full duty those MOS experiencing difficulties with:
    • Alcohol and prescription drugs
    • Gambling or finances

Non-Disciplinary Referrals

  • Denial is an inherent part of alcoholism, so supervisors are a vital link in detection and referral
  • Non-Disciplinary Referral:
    • No report is made
    • Promotion opportunities are NOT jeopardized
    • Current assignment NOT ordinarily changed (but may be if in best interest of all)

Exam Alert: A non-disciplinary referral to the Counseling Unit produces NO report, does NOT affect promotions, and does NOT ordinarily change the member’s assignment. Supervisors play a critical detection/referral role because denial is inherent in alcoholism.

Misconduct

  • Counseling Unit is not a sanctuary for misconduct
  • Suspected illegal drug use will be reported to IAB
  • Participation will NOT exempt MOS from discipline for acts of misconduct

Adjudication of Discipline Involving Alcohol or Domestic Violence

  • Department Advocate will ensure:
    • Prior to final adjudication of all misconduct cases involving alcohol or domestic violence, a conferral with EAU must be made, AND
    • Assessment/referral by Counseling Unit must be done

Exam Alert: For ANY misconduct case involving alcohol or domestic violence, two things must happen before final adjudication: (1) conferral with EAU and (2) assessment/referral by Counseling Unit. The Dept. Advocate ensures compliance.

See Also: A.G. 330-07 — Trauma Counseling Program (separate program for incident-related trauma; does not prevent voluntary use of Counseling Unit).

Section 330 — Exposure to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials

A.G. 330-09: Exposure of Members of the Service to Infectious Diseases or Hazardous Materials

Date Effective: 11/18/21 | Last Revision: I.O. 99 | Pages: 4

Purpose

To provide instructions and minimize risk to members of the service (uniformed and civilian) who have contact with, or handle, an animal or person who may have an infectious disease, or who have contact with or handle hazardous materials.

Definitions

Term Definition
Infectious Disease Diseases transmitted by contact with infected animal’s saliva/CNS tissue, or infected individual’s blood or body fluids
Hazardous Material Any chemical, biological, or radiological substance which is a health hazard (carcinogens, toxic agents, irritants, corrosives, flammable/combustible liquids, compressed gas, explosives)
Exposure to Infectious Disease Airborne transmission or direct contact with individual suspected of having disease; contact with blood/body fluids; human/animal bite; hypodermic needle puncture; contaminated sharp instrument
Exposure to Hazardous Material Exposure through inhalation, ingestion, skin contact, or parenteral contact
Universal Precautions Approach to infection control: treat all human blood and certain body fluids as if infectious for HIV, Hepatitis B, or other bloodborne pathogens; treat all animal saliva and CNS tissue as if infectious for rabies
Designated Officer Individual designated under 42 U.S.C. 300ff-86 to execute provisions of Ryan White Act regarding emergency response employees requesting exposure determination

Scene Management Note

To avoid unnecessary exposure, control of the aided/collision victim or prisoner will be assigned to an officer at the scene who has already had contact with the person suspected of having an infectious disease. If numerous individuals involved, the patrol supervisor coordinates information and documents names of involved MOS.

Procedure — Upon Exposure

Role Step Action
Member 1 Comply with guidelines in Additional Data
Member 2 Notify desk officer, precinct of occurrence
Desk Officer 3 Obtain exposure report number via Line of Duty Injury Reporting System
Desk Officer 4 Make Command Log entry (include exposure report number)
Desk Officer 5 Notify designated Department surgeon of facts
Desk Officer 6 Contact member and advise of necessary treatment
Member 7 Comply with directions of Department surgeon
Member 8 Make digital Activity Log entry (include exposure report number)

When Exposure Involves Actual Injury

  • Member transported to hospital for treatment
  • Comply with A.G. 330-03 (Line of Duty Injury or Death)

Tuberculosis Exposure (No Actual Injury)

  • Department offers free Mantoux (PPD) skin testing
  • If results positive → notify Unit Safety Officer to initiate entry on DOSH 900 log
  • Code (e.g., maiden name pseudonym) may substitute for description in column F

Infectious Disease Guidelines

Guideline
Confer with EMS, family, friends, neighbors, doctor, or agency about source individual’s medical conditions
Wear disposable gloves if contact with blood/body fluids possible
Heavy-duty rubber gloves for routine capture/seizure of suspected rabid animals
Wash hands with soap and water (disinfectant soap if possible) after removing gloves
Immediately wash unprotected body parts if contact with blood/body fluids or animal bite/scratch
Machine wash (hot cycle) or dry clean uniform/civilian clothes that contact blood/body fluids
Use extraordinary care to avoid accidental needle puncture wounds
Affix Biohazard Label to any property, evidence, or waste that may be contaminated

Accidental Blood/Body Fluid Spills

Rule
Supply of household bleach maintained at all precinct/PSA/transit district commands, ESU, borough court section facilities
Clean spills with FRESHLY MIXED solution: 1 part bleach : 10 parts water
Bleach must NOT be mixed with any solution other than water
Must be freshly mixed for each use
Do NOT use bleach solution to clean uniforms (will cause damage) — use routine laundering or dry cleaning
Do NOT use bleach to cleanse hands
Dispose of bleach dilutions in sink only (not urinal or toilet — may contain chemical deodorizers causing toxic gas)

Hypodermic Needle Handling

Rule
Place in hypodermic needle/syringe container using one-handed scoop technique
Move needle away from yourself (hold syringe, move toward container)
Do NOT hold container and move hand toward needle
Cap container if possible; if syringe extends beyond container, secure with tape
NEVER attempt to remove, dislodge, or bend a needle from a hypodermic syringe

Ryan White Act — Designated Officer

Rule
Emergency response employees may request determination of exposure through Department’s designated officer at Medical Division, Hazmat Unit
If exposure determined → signed written request sent to medical facility where victim was transported
Designated officer must immediately notify member(s) upon receiving notification from medical facility
HIV-related information disclosed to member → redisclosure prohibited when not in conformance with state law

Exam Alert: Bleach solution ratio: 1 part bleach : 10 parts water, freshly mixed. Hypodermic needles handled with one-handed scoop technique — NEVER remove, dislodge, or bend. Assign the officer who already had contact to continue handling the person.

Sergeant Focus: Coordinate documentation of all involved MOS names when numerous individuals are exposed. Ensure exposure report number obtained via LOD Injury Reporting System. If actual injury → comply with A.G. 330-03. Know the bleach ratio and needle handling rules for roll call training.

Section 330 — Line of Duty Injury or Death

A.G. 330-03: Line of Duty Injury or Death

Date Effective: 02/03/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 8 | Pages: 5

Purpose

To report and record line of duty injuries and deaths occurring within the City or outside of the City.

Definition

INVESTIGATING SUPERVISOR — A supervisory UMOS in the rank of sergeant or above (patrol supervisor, platoon commander, duty captain, etc.) assigned to the injured UMOS’s command. If unavailable, a supervisory UMOS within the precinct of occurrence.

Injured Member Duties

Step Action
1 Request supervisor to respond
2 Notify desk officer of: (a) circumstances of injury, (b) names/addresses of witnesses
3 Request witnesses to await arrival of supervisor
4 Remain at scene unless: (a) hospitalization/medical attention required, (b) further police action necessary
5 Notify local police authorities and Operations Unit immediately if reporting LOD injury from outside of City

Investigating Supervisor (IS) — Who Qualifies

Scenario Minimum Rank
Normal LOD (within city) Sergeant or above from member’s command
If unavailable Supervisor from precinct of occurrence
Out of city Captain or above
Force against us (P.G. 221-06) Supervisor uninvolved, AND 1 rank higher than all involved members

Exam Alert: The investigating supervisor hierarchy is a frequent test topic. Within the city = Sgt+. Out of city = Capt+. Force incidents = uninvolved AND 1 rank higher.

Investigating Supervisor Duties

Step Action
6 Respond to scene and request medical attention if necessary
7 Interview injured member and investigate circumstances
8 Interview witnesses and ensure preparation of: (a) WITNESS STATEMENT - INJURY TO MEMBER OF THE DEPARTMENT (PD429-065) for non-Department witnesses, (b) Line of Duty Injury Reporting System witness statement for MOS witnesses
9 Prepare Police Accident Report (MV104AN) if vehicle collision involved
10 Direct injured member to prepare AIDED REPORT (PD304-152) if no vehicle involved
11 Comply with P.G. 221-06 if any force used by UMOS or civilian and UMOS sustains injury
12 Prepare Line of Duty Injury Report if injured member is incapacitated

Investigating Supervisor and the 3 Reports

Memory Aid — IS and the 3 Reports: - IS always prepares the PAR (Police Accident Report) - IS never prepares the AIDED (directs injured MOS to do it; if incapacitated, assigns another member) - IS sometimes prepares the LODI (only if injured member is incapacitated)

Out of City LOD Injuries

Investigating supervisor must be in the rank of captain or above.

Line of Duty Injury Report

Rule
Prepared using Line of Duty Injury Reporting System on Department intranet or smartphone
Include: witness information, documents related to injury (photographs, hospital discharge paperwork)
Submitted electronically to investigating supervisor
Investigating supervisor reviews for completeness/accuracy and signs off electronically prior to completion of tour (absent exceptional circumstances)
Include recommendation for approval/disapproval of LOD designation
Include findings of review of any available BWC or other video surveillance

Desk Officer Notifications

Memory Aid — D.O. Notification Tiers:

Condition Notify
MOS not from precinct of occurrence MOS’s own command
MOS is DOA or likely Boro C.O. (not XO), Pct CO/XO/Duty Capt, Operations, Medical Examiner or Assistant
MOS requires hospital treatment Operations
Not witnessed AND in/around stationhouse, 1st 2 hours, off-duty Sick Desk Boss or Sick Desk
Notify When
Injured member’s command (if not assigned to precinct of occurrence)
Borough commander, CO/XO/duty captain, Operations Unit, Medical Examiner — if member dies or likely to die
Operations Unit — if member requires hospital treatment
Sick Desk supervisor when any of these conditions exist:

Sick Desk Notification Triggers

Condition
Incident causing injury was NOT specifically witnessed
Injury occurred within any police facility (building, office, etc.)
Injury occurred in immediate vicinity of police facility (parking lot, perimeter sidewalk)
Injury occurred during first two hours of a tour
Any request for LOD designation by an off-duty UMOS

Exam Alert: Think about it this way: if none of the Sick Desk trigger conditions exist, the officer is from the precinct, the incident was witnessed, the MOS was on-duty, and it did not happen in/near the stationhouse — the Desk Officer has no Sick Desk notification duty.

CO Duties

Step Action
17 Review all LOD Injury Reports via Reporting System application
18 Prepare Typed Letterhead to CO, Medical Division when: (a) injured member is PPO, (b) injury occurred while off-duty, (c) injury reported after end of tour
19 Prepare Typed Letterhead for LOD death or serious injury/likely to die → forward per P.G. 212-09 Unusual Occurrence Reports
20 Comply with A.G. 330-07, Trauma Counseling Program
21 Forward copies to PPF if LOD death or serious injury/likely to die

Hospital Privacy Rule

When treated at hospital for LOD injury, ONLY the following address/phone may be entered in hospital records: - Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza, 59-17 Junction Boulevard, Corona, NY 11368 - Member’s command, home address, and telephone number will NOT be disclosed unless authorized supervisory officer determines requesting person is entitled

When Member Is Hospitalized or Dies

Patrol supervisor ensures: - Firearms, Department property (shield, ID, nameplate), and personal effects are removed and safeguarded - Member struck by bullet on protective vest and apparently uninjured → must be examined by doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury

Exam Alert: If MOS is DOA, also follow P.G. 216-04, Dead Body. For Department vehicle collisions (P.G. 217-06), the patrol supervisor enters the command address as operator’s address on the PAR.

Hospital Response — Serious Injury/Death in Line of Duty

Operations Unit notifies the following to respond to hospital:

Respond Also Respond
Police Commissioner Commanding Officer, Family Assistance Section
First Deputy Commissioner (if PC unavailable) Lt, Family Assistance Section (borough concerned)
Chief of Department Patrol borough CO
Chief of Detectives CO/duty captain (prepares UOR)
Bureau chief of injured/deceased Detective borough commander
Deputy Commissioner, Public Information Detective assigned to investigate
Supervising Chief Surgeon Crime Scene Unit detective/technician
Department Chaplain Line organization representative
Duty inspector (control access to ER) EAU representative
Patrol borough operations commander (if DI unavailable) POPPA representative

Permitted to Speak to Injured/View Deceased (MOS DOA/SILD)

Applies in residence counties too.

Supervisors (5): - PC, 1st Deputy, Chief of Department, Bureau Chief, Patrol Borough C.O. or representative

Investigators (2): - Detective assigned to the case, CSU detective/technician

Fraternal and Religious (2): - Union representative, Department Chaplain

Under Certain Circumstances — Qualified (2): - ESU member (if transported by Department ambulance) - Policewomen’s Endowment Association representative (if member identifies as female)

Memory Aid — Who Can Speak/View: 5-2-2-2: 5 Supervisors, 2 Investigators, 2 Fraternal/Religious, 2 Conditional

FECA (Federal Employees Compensation Act)

Rule
Members may be entitled to medical and financial benefits under FECA if a Federal statute was violated and injury occurred while apprehending
Investigating supervisor must indicate on LOD Injury Report whether FECA applies
If FECA applies → CO forwards items to Medical Division, Federal Compensation Liaison Officer, within 90 days

Delayed LOD Designation

Rule
If designation cannot be made expeditiously → Typed Letterhead with reasons forwarded to Medical Division within 60 days
Subsequent reports every 60 days thereafter if necessary

Exam Alert: Investigating supervisor for out-of-city LOD injuries must be captain or above. Sick Desk must be notified when injury is during first 2 hours of tour, in/near police facility, not witnessed, or off-duty request. Hospital records receive Medical Division address only — not member’s personal info.

Sergeant Focus: Sign off LOD Injury Report electronically before end of tour. Include BWC/video review findings. If member dies or likely to die, ensure firearms and Department property are safeguarded. Comply with P.G. 221-06 if any force was used. FECA indicator required on LOD report.

Section 330 — Notifying Court When Reporting Sick

P.G. 330-04: Notifying Court, Etc., When Reporting Sick

UMOS Duties

  • Court, Trial Room, or other governmental appearances:
    • Inform Desk Officer/Supervisor of ANY pending appearance

Desk Officer / Supervisor Duties

Step Action
1 Make entry in Command Diary under: (a) date of appearance, AND (b) two (2) days prior (not counting Sat/Sun/Holidays)
2 If pending arraignment, notify Borough Court Section concerned

Exam Alert: The Command Diary entry goes under BOTH the appearance date AND 2 days prior (excluding weekends and holidays). This ensures the command has advance notice.

Roll Call Clerk Duties

Step Action
1 Immediately notify court of member’s inability to attend due to being on sick report
2 Two (2) days before date of appearance: if member unable to appear, notify Court and request adjournment
3 Date of scheduled appearance: notify court on the morning of the appearance (except Criminal Court)
4 Enter ALL notifications in Telephone Record

Operations Coordinator / PRAA Duties

Step Action
1 Two (2) days prior to appearance: call District Surgeon to determine if member can appear
2 If Surgeon approves appearance: notify member to appear AND notify Sick Desk

Condition Changes After Directed to Appear

  • If condition changes after member was directed to appear and member cannot make appearance:
    • Member must notify Roll Call Clerk or D.O. who makes required notifications

Exam Alert: If a member was cleared by the Surgeon to appear in court but their condition worsens, the member contacts the Roll Call Clerk or D.O. — not the Surgeon or Sick Desk.

See Also: P.G. 330-01 — Regular Sick (court/trial room compliance when MOS goes sick).

See Also: P.G. 330-02 — Administrative Sick (UMOS must inform D.O. of pending appearances).

Section 330 — Regular Sick

P.G. 330-01: Regular Sick

Definition

  • Regular Sick — Member is not eligible for, or declines, Administrative Sick
  • Requires a visit to the Department Surgeon

District Surgeon Reporting

Report to your assigned District Surgeon at next office hours, EXCEPT in 5 situations:

Memory Aid — 5 Times You Do NOT Visit Your Assigned District Surgeon:

Problem Report Instead To
Chronic B Special/Confidential Medical District at next regular office hours
Go sick on Friday/Saturday Weekend Surgeon at 1000 hours the following day
Unable to travel Remain at place of confinement and call Sick Desk Supervisor
Dental problems Be guided by Sick Desk Supervisor/member
Medical Division Supervisor directs otherwise As directed

Paid Detail Restriction

  • NO Paid Detail while out sick — even while outside assigned tour or on RDO
  • Notify Paid Detail Unit as soon as member knows they cannot make a scheduled assignment

Exam Alert: A member out sick cannot work paid detail at ANY time — not just during their scheduled tour. This includes RDOs. The Paid Detail Unit must be notified immediately.

UMOS Reporting Procedure

Step Action
1 Notify Medical Division Sick Desk
2 Obtain from Sick Desk: (a) sick serial number, (b) Sick Desk name, (c) time notified
3 Give Sick Desk: details and any change of address/location or phone number
4 Notify D.O./Supervisor at your command
5 Give D.O./Supervisor the sick serial number only

Prior Test: Which of the following is correct? A UMOS going out on regular sick should provide the desk officer at the permanent command with: A) Name of Sick Desk member notified B) Time Sick Desk was notified C) Sick serial number D) Change of address/location — Correct answer is C. UMOS gets 3 pieces of information from the Sick Desk but only passes the sick serial number to the D.O.

Exam Alert: The UMOS receives 3 items from the Sick Desk (serial number, name, time) but only gives 1 item to the D.O./Supervisor — the sick serial number. This distinction is a frequent exam trap.

Confinement Rules

  • Remain at residence/other authorized location while out sick EXCEPT when:
    • Outside assigned tour of duty
    • RDO, OR
    • Permission to leave is granted by Department Surgeon or Sick Desk Supervisor

Chronic (A or B), Modified, Suspended, Dismissal Probation, OR Surgeon Deems Confinement Necessary

  • Must remain at residence/other authorized location entire sick time — even when NOT scheduled to be on-duty, including RDOs
  • Unless permission granted by Surgeon or Sick Desk Supervisor

Tours Vary

  • If member’s tours vary, the tour scheduled for the day they went sick is the tour for the duration of the sick leave

Travel Restrictions

  • Do NOT leave New York City or resident counties without approval of Chief of Personnel
  • Request approval through District Surgeon

Prior Test: To leave the city and resident counties while out sick, request permission through: A) Chief of Personnel B) Sick Desk Supervisor C) Desk Officer, permanent command D) District Surgeon — Correct answer is D (District Surgeon). The Chief of Personnel grants the approval, but the request goes through the District Surgeon.

UMOS Out Sick from Outside Residence Counties

  • Contacted weekly by their District Surgeon

Report to District Surgeon

  • Next office hours as directed by Medical Division
  • If unable to visit Department Surgeon, notify Sick Desk Supervisor and be guided by them

Visited While Sick

  • Comply with lawful order/instruction of supervisor, regardless of rank, dispatched by competent authority to visit member on sick report

Desk Officer/Supervisor Duties

Step Action
1 Record sick in: Sick Log, Telephone Record, and CPR System Roll Call
2 Query Sick Reporting System to ensure UMOS is listed as being sick
3 If member scheduled for either of the next two platoons, notify relieving D.O./Supervisor
4 If sick out of state/country, notify Sick Desk Supervisor
5 If suspect malingering, notify Medical Division’s Absence Control and Investigations Unit
6 Court/Trial Room: comply with P.G. 330-04, notify court when MOS goes sick

Prior Test: Which D.O. notification is correct? A) If MOS working the next three platoons, notify relieving D.O./Supervisor B) If MOS suspected of malingering, notify Sick Desk Supervisor C) If special or unusual circumstances, notify Medical Division’s Absence Control and Investigations Unit D) If MOS scheduled for either of the next two platoons, notify relieving D.O./Supervisor — Correct answer is D. It is the next two platoons, not three. Malingering goes to Absence Control, not Sick Desk.

Routine Visits (Members NOT Subject to Visits/Calls)

Members are not subject to routine visits or calls if sick for: - Heart condition - Broken limb - Post-surgical - Pregnant - Illness diagnosed by Department Surgeon as likely to exceed 6 months (with Supervising Chief Surgeon conferral)

Exam Alert: The C.O. of Medical Division or designee (non-doctors) may revoke routine visit exemptions upon notice to MOS and District Surgeon.

Operations Coordinator Duties

  • Access Sick Reporting System periodically throughout tour
  • Ensure Department records are updated as necessary

Request Limited Capacity from Department Surgeon

  • Do NOT have to go out sick, but must visit Surgeon
  • Exception: Pregnant and requesting position limited status — no visit necessary

See Also: P.G. 330-02 — Administrative Sick (alternative to Regular Sick for eligible UMOS).

See Also: P.G. 330-04 — Notifying Court When Reporting Sick.

Section 330 — Trauma Counseling Program

A.G. 330-07: Trauma Counseling Program

Date Effective: 03/24/25 | Last Revision: R.O. 14 | Pages: 3

Purpose

To provide Trauma Counseling Services for members of the service (uniformed and civilian) involved in shooting incidents, disasters, or other violent occurrences resulting in death or injury.

Scope — Referral Triggers

A member will be referred to the Trauma Counseling Program when involved in:

Trigger
Member is shot or otherwise seriously injured
Member discharges a weapon causing injury or death
Member causes, accidentally or otherwise, serious physical injury or death to another
Member directly involved where their partner was killed or seriously injured
Member directly involved in incidents or serious disasters where multiple serious injuries and deaths occurred

Trauma Counseling Team Response — Mandatory

The Trauma Counseling Team will respond to all incidents involving: - Member of the service is shot, killed, or seriously injured in performance of duty - Any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above

Procedure

Role Step Action
Desk Officer 1 Notify Operations Unit and provide details
Operations Unit Supervisor 2 Notify Sick Desk supervisor, provide details, request Trauma Counseling Team response
Sick Desk Supervisor 3 Ascertain location for team response and name of contact supervisor at scene
Sick Desk Supervisor 4 Arrange transportation of Trauma Team
Ranking Supervisor at Scene 5 Ensure members needing counseling are available for interview upon team arrival
Ranking Supervisor at Scene 6 Provide suitable place for trauma interview

Trauma Counseling Team Duties

Step Action
7 Respond to location and report to designated contact person
8 Respond to stationhouse of precinct of occurrence or other location to make contact with involved member(s)
9 Visit place of occurrence if necessary
10 Notify member they will be contacted for follow-up meeting

Follow-Up Schedule

Timeline Action
48 hours after event or initial contact Follow-up meeting
1 to 6 weeks after incident Additional counseling/interview sessions

Member Duties

Step Action
11 Cooperate with Trauma Counseling Team and keep appointments
12 Report to Behavioral Care and Support Section or other location 48 hours after incident or initial contact
12a Report for additional follow-up meetings as scheduled

CO/Duty Captain Duties

Step Action
13 Cooperate with Trauma Counseling Team and permit access to involved members
14 Verify that involved members have been referred and contacted in person
15 Have UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT (PD370-152) prepared listing involved members contacted by Trauma Team
16 Forward copies of UOR, T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORT (if prepared), and other reports to CO, Medical Division

Confidentiality

ALL conversation with the Trauma Counseling Team by members of the service (uniformed and civilian) will be STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL.

Additional Data

Fact
Trauma Counseling Team staffed by Chief Surgeon, Director of Behavioral Care and Support Section, and licensed certified professionals of Medical Division
Team members will NOT interfere with ongoing investigations
Team members will NOT be assigned duties outside their Trauma Counselor functions when responding
Services also available for personal or family tragedies (violent criminal incidents, death in family, etc.)
Does NOT prevent voluntary use of or referral to any Employee Assistance Programs
Team members wear authorized nylon windbreaker jackets with NYPD logo on front and N.Y.C. POLICE on back

Exam Alert: Follow-up meeting at 48 hours after event. Additional sessions within 1 to 6 weeks. All conversations are strictly confidential. Trauma Team will NOT interfere with investigations.

Sergeant Focus: Ensure involved members are available for Trauma Team interview. Verify members have been contacted in person. Prepare UOR listing contacted members. Captain or above can request Trauma Team response to any incident.

Section 330 — Key Terms and Definitions

Line of Duty Injury

Term Definition
Investigating Supervisor Sergeant or above from injured member’s command; if unavailable, from precinct of occurrence
Out of City LOD Investigating supervisor must be captain or above
Line of Duty Injury Report Prepared via LOD Injury Reporting System (intranet or smartphone)
FECA Federal Employees Compensation Act — benefits when Federal statute violated during apprehension

Trauma Counseling

Term Definition
Trauma Counseling Team Staffed by Chief Surgeon, Director of Behavioral Care and Support Section, and licensed professionals of Medical Division
Behavioral Care and Support Section Location for 48-hour follow-up meeting
POPPA Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance — separate from Trauma Counseling
Employee Assistance Programs Multiple programs available; Trauma Counseling does not prevent voluntary use of any

Infectious Disease/Hazmat

Term Definition
Infectious Disease Diseases transmitted by contact with blood, body fluids, animal saliva, or CNS tissue
Hazardous Material Any chemical, biological, or radiological health hazard
Universal Precautions Treat all blood and body fluids as infectious for HIV, HBV, and other bloodborne pathogens
Designated Officer Individual designated under Ryan White Act (42 U.S.C. 300ff-86) for exposure determinations

Key Forms

Form Purpose
WITNESS STATEMENT - INJURY TO MEMBER (PD429-065) Non-Department witness statement for LOD injury
Police Accident Report (MV104AN) If vehicle collision involved in LOD injury
AIDED REPORT (PD304-152) If no vehicle involved in LOD injury
UNUSUAL OCCURRENCE REPORT (PD370-152) For LOD death/serious injury; also for Trauma Counseling incidents
SCOC M187 Report of Inmate Death to NYS Commission of Correction
Biohazard Label Affixed to contaminated property, evidence, or waste

Key Timeframes

Item Timeframe
LOD Injury Report sign-off by investigating supervisor Before end of tour
Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting 48 hours after event
Additional trauma counseling sessions 1 to 6 weeks after incident
FECA documentation forwarding Within 90 days of occurrence
Delayed LOD designation report Within 60 days (then every 60 days)
Exposure report number Obtained via LOD Injury Reporting System

Sick Desk Notification Triggers (LOD Injury)

Trigger
Incident NOT specifically witnessed
Injury in or near any police facility
Injury during first 2 hours of tour
Off-duty LOD designation request

Bleach Solution for Blood/Body Fluid Spills

Rule Detail
Ratio 1 part bleach : 10 parts water
Preparation FRESHLY MIXED for each use
Do NOT mix with Any solution other than water
Do NOT use on Uniforms (causes damage) or hands
Disposal In sink only (not urinal/toilet — chemical deodorizers cause toxic gas)

Needle Handling

Rule
One-handed scoop technique
Move needle AWAY from yourself
NEVER remove, dislodge, or bend a needle
Cap container if possible; tape if syringe extends

Regular Sick (P.G. 330-01)

Term Definition
Regular Sick Member not eligible for or declines Admin Sick; requires visit to Department Surgeon
District Surgeon Report at next office hours; 5 exceptions (Chronic B, Fri/Sat, unable to travel, dental, Medical Division directs)
Sick Serial Number Obtained from Sick Desk; the ONLY item UMOS passes to D.O.
Absence Control and Investigations Unit Medical Division unit notified by D.O. when malingering is suspected
Sick Reporting System Queried by D.O. and Operations Coordinator to verify sick status
Limited Capacity Must visit Surgeon to request; exception: pregnant members requesting position limited — no visit required

Administrative Sick (P.G. 330-02)

Term Definition
Admin Sick UMOS-only (no CMOS); max 2 days; call D.O. at least 2 hours pre-tour; no Surgeon visit
LIMB LSS MD Mnemonic for when Admin Sick is NOT authorized: Line of duty, Injury, Mutual, Chronic B, Late, Stress, Supervisor deems inappropriate, Major illness, Dental
One Sick Event Days 1-3 (Admin + Regular) consolidate into a single sick event

Notifying Court When Sick (P.G. 330-04)

Term Definition
Command Diary Entry made under appearance date AND 2 days prior (excluding Sat/Sun/Holidays)
Roll Call Clerk Notifies court of inability to attend; requests adjournment 2 days before; notifies on morning of appearance (except Criminal Court)
Operations Coordinator / PRAA Calls District Surgeon 2 days prior to determine if member can appear

Counseling Unit (P.G. 330-16)

Term Definition
Counseling Unit Assists MOS with alcohol, prescription drug, gambling, or financial difficulties; return to full duty
Non-Disciplinary Referral No report made; promotions NOT jeopardized; assignment NOT ordinarily changed
Confidentiality Exceptions Written consent, valid subpoena, U.S. District Court Order, suspected child abuse, danger to self/others
EAU Conferral Required before final adjudication of ALL misconduct involving alcohol or domestic violence

Key Timeframes (Updated)

Item Timeframe
LOD Injury Report sign-off by investigating supervisor Before end of tour
Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting 48 hours after event
Additional trauma counseling sessions 1 to 6 weeks after incident
FECA documentation forwarding Within 90 days of occurrence
Delayed LOD designation report Within 60 days (then every 60 days)
Admin Sick notification to D.O. At least 2 hours before start of tour
Admin Sick maximum duration 2 days (3rd day becomes Regular Sick)
Court notification — Command Diary Appearance date AND 2 days prior (excl. weekends/holidays)
UMOS out sick outside resident counties Contacted weekly by District Surgeon

Section 330 — Review Questions

Multiple Choice

1. The investigating supervisor for a line of duty injury must sign off on the LOD Injury Report:

    1. Within 24 hours
    1. Within 48 hours
    1. Prior to completion of tour
    1. By end of the following tour
Answer

C) Prior to completion of tour

The investigating supervisor reviews for completeness and accuracy, and signs off electronically prior to completion of tour, absent exceptional circumstances. (A.G. 330-03)

2. For a line of duty injury occurring outside of New York City, the investigating supervisor must be in the rank of:

    1. Sergeant or above
    1. Lieutenant or above
    1. Captain or above
    1. Inspector or above
Answer

C) Captain or above

The investigating supervisor for out-of-City line of duty injuries must be in the rank of captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)

3. The desk officer must notify the Sick Desk supervisor for a LOD injury when:

    1. The injury occurred during any part of the member’s tour
    1. The injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour
    1. The member was treated and released from the hospital
    1. The injury was witnessed by multiple members
Answer

B) The injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour

Sick Desk notification is required when: incident was not specifically witnessed, injury in/near police facility, injury during first 2 hours of tour, or off-duty LOD designation request. (A.G. 330-03)

4. When a UMOS is treated at a hospital for a LOD injury, which address should be entered in hospital records?

    1. The member’s home address
    1. The precinct of occurrence address
    1. The Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza address
    1. The member’s permanent command address
Answer

C) The Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza address

Only the Medical Division address (1 Lefrak City Plaza, 59-17 Junction Boulevard, Corona, NY 11368) may be entered in hospital records. Member’s command, home address, and telephone number will not be disclosed. (A.G. 330-03)

5. When a UMOS is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured:

    1. No further action is required
    1. The member may continue duty without medical evaluation
    1. The member must be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury
    1. The member is placed on sick report for 24 hours
Answer

C) The member must be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury

When an injured member is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured, the member must be examined by a doctor to ascertain if a blunt body trauma or internal injury has occurred. (A.G. 330-03)

6. The Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting should occur:

    1. 24 hours after the event
    1. 48 hours after the event or initial contact
    1. One week after the event
    1. 30 days after the event
Answer

B) 48 hours after the event or initial contact

The member will be contacted for a follow-up meeting 48 hours after the event or initial contact. Additional counseling/interview sessions are scheduled within 1 to 6 weeks after the incident. (A.G. 330-07)

7. All conversations with the Trauma Counseling Team are:

    1. Reported to the commanding officer
    1. Recorded in the Command Log
    1. Strictly confidential
    1. Shared with Internal Affairs Bureau
Answer

C) Strictly confidential

ALL conversation with the Trauma Counseling Team by members of the service (uniformed and civilian) will be STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL. (A.G. 330-07)

8. The Trauma Counseling Team responds to all incidents involving a member being shot, killed, or seriously injured in the line of duty, AND:

    1. Any incident at the discretion of the desk officer
    1. Any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above
    1. Only when directed by Internal Affairs Bureau
    1. Only firearms discharge incidents
Answer

B) Any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above

The Trauma Counseling Team will respond to all incidents involving MOS shot/killed/seriously injured in LOD, and any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above. (A.G. 330-07)

9. The correct ratio for the bleach solution used to clean blood or body fluid spills is:

    1. 1 part bleach to 5 parts water
    1. 1 part bleach to 10 parts water
    1. 1 part bleach to 20 parts water
    1. Undiluted bleach
Answer

B) 1 part bleach to 10 parts water

Accidental spills may be cleaned by applying a FRESHLY MIXED solution of 1 part household bleach to 10 parts water. The solution must be freshly mixed for each use and must not be mixed with any solution other than water. (A.G. 330-09)

10. When handling hypodermic needles, members should use the:

    1. Two-handed grip technique
    1. One-handed scoop technique
    1. Pliers and container method
    1. Gloved removal technique
Answer

B) One-handed scoop technique

Members use a one-handed scoop technique, using the needle itself to pick up the container, moving the needle away from themselves. Members should NOT hold the container and move their hand toward the needle. Under no circumstances should a member attempt to remove, dislodge, or bend a needle. (A.G. 330-09)

11. When multiple individuals are exposed to an infectious disease at a scene, who coordinates documentation?

    1. The desk officer
    1. The patrol supervisor at the scene
    1. The first member to arrive
    1. Emergency Medical Services
Answer

B) The patrol supervisor at the scene

If there are numerous individuals involved, the patrol supervisor at the scene will be responsible for the coordination of information and documenting the names of the members of the service involved. (A.G. 330-09)

12. Under A.G. 330-09, to avoid unnecessary exposure at a scene, control of an aided victim suspected of having an infectious disease should be assigned to:

    1. The patrol supervisor
    1. EMS personnel only
    1. An officer who has already had contact with the person
    1. The next available officer
Answer

C) An officer who has already had contact with the person

To avoid unnecessary exposure, control of the aided/collision victim or prisoner will be assigned to an officer at the scene who has already had contact with a person suspected of having an infectious disease. (A.G. 330-09)

13. If a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, the ranking officer must forward a Typed Letterhead to Medical Division within:

    1. 30 days
    1. 60 days
    1. 90 days
    1. 120 days
Answer

B) 60 days

When a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, a report on Typed Letterhead indicating reasons for the delay must be forwarded to Medical Division within 60 days. Subsequent reports are forwarded for each additional 60-day period. (A.G. 330-03)

14. If an injury comes under the Federal Employees Compensation Act (FECA), the CO must forward documentation to Medical Division within:

    1. 30 days
    1. 60 days
    1. 90 days
    1. 180 days
Answer

C) 90 days

If the injury comes under FECA, the member’s CO will forward items (LOD Injury Report, witness statements, arrest history, criminal court complaint, firearms trace, completed DOL form BEC-721a) to Medical Division, Federal Compensation Liaison Officer, within 90 days of occurrence. (A.G. 330-03)

15. The bleach solution for cleaning blood spills should be disposed of:

    1. In any available drain
    1. In a urinal or toilet
    1. In a sink only
    1. In a hazardous waste container
Answer

C) In a sink only

Disposal of bleach or bleach dilutions should be performed only in a sink (not a urinal or toilet, since they sometimes contain chemical deodorizers that may cause toxic gas when mixed with bleach). (A.G. 330-09)

16. A UMOS going out on regular sick obtains three items from the Sick Desk. Which of the following should the UMOS provide to the desk officer at the permanent command?

    1. Name of Sick Desk member notified
    1. Time Sick Desk was notified
    1. Sick serial number
    1. Change of address/location or phone number
Answer

C) Sick serial number

The UMOS receives 3 items from the Sick Desk (sick serial number, Sick Desk name, and time notified), but only provides the sick serial number to the D.O./Supervisor. (P.G. 330-01)

17. Administrative Sick is available to:

    1. All members of the service
    1. Uniformed members of the service only — CMOS cannot go Admin Sick
    1. Civilian members of the service only
    1. Members with more than 5 years of service
Answer

B) Uniformed members of the service only — CMOS cannot go Admin Sick

Administrative Sick is for UMOS only. Civilian members of the service cannot go Administrative Sick. (P.G. 330-02)

18. On the 3rd consecutive day of Administrative Sick, the member must:

    1. Continue on Admin Sick with no changes
    1. Report Regular Sick and be guided by Sick Desk regarding District Surgeon visit
    1. Return to full duty
    1. Report directly to the District Surgeon without calling anyone
Answer

B) Report Regular Sick and be guided by Sick Desk regarding District Surgeon visit

Admin Sick lasts a maximum of 2 days. On the 3rd day, it converts to Regular Sick. The member is guided by the Sick Desk regarding the Medical Division visit to the District Surgeon. All three days consolidate into 1 sick event. (P.G. 330-02)

19. For Administrative Sick, the UMOS first calls:

    1. The Sick Desk, then the command
    1. The D.O./Supervisor at their command — not the Sick Desk
    1. The District Surgeon
    1. Operations Unit
Answer

B) The D.O./Supervisor at their command — not the Sick Desk

For Admin Sick, the UMOS calls the command (D.O./Supervisor) at least 2 hours pre-tour. For Regular Sick, the UMOS calls the Sick Desk first, then the command. This is a key distinction. (P.G. 330-02)

20. Which of the following is NOT a reason Admin Sick would be denied (per the LIMB LSS MD mnemonic)?

    1. Line of Duty injury
    1. Headache
    1. Stress/psychological
    1. Dental problems
Answer

B) Headache

The LIMB LSS MD mnemonic lists: Line of duty, Injury, Mutual, Chronic B, Late (less than 2 hrs pre-tour), Stress/psychological, Supervisor deems inappropriate, Major illness, Dental. A simple headache is not on the exclusion list. (P.G. 330-02)

21. When a member is out sick and has a pending court appearance, the desk officer makes a Command Diary entry under:

    1. The date of appearance only
    1. The date of appearance and 1 day prior
    1. The date of appearance and 2 days prior (not counting Sat/Sun/Holidays)
    1. The date of appearance and 3 days prior
Answer

C) The date of appearance and 2 days prior (not counting Sat/Sun/Holidays)

The D.O. makes a Command Diary entry under both the date of appearance AND 2 days prior, not counting Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays. (P.G. 330-04)

22. The Counseling Unit provides assistance to MOS experiencing difficulties with all of the following EXCEPT:

    1. Alcohol
    1. Prescription drugs
    1. Gambling
    1. Marital counseling
Answer

D) Marital counseling

The Counseling Unit’s primary function is assisting MOS with difficulties involving alcohol, prescription drugs, gambling, or finances. Marital counseling is not listed as a primary function. (P.G. 330-16)

23. A non-disciplinary referral to the Counseling Unit results in:

    1. A report being filed with IAB
    1. Jeopardized promotion opportunities
    1. No report, no jeopardized promotions, and assignment not ordinarily changed
    1. Automatic transfer to a different command
Answer

C) No report, no jeopardized promotions, and assignment not ordinarily changed

A non-disciplinary referral produces no report, does not jeopardize promotions, and the member’s current assignment is not ordinarily changed (though it may be if in best interest of all). (P.G. 330-16)

24. The investigating supervisor for a LOD injury within the city must be:

    1. A detective or above
    1. A sergeant or above from the injured member’s command
    1. A captain or above
    1. Any available supervisor regardless of command
Answer

B) A sergeant or above from the injured member’s command

Within the city, the investigating supervisor is a sergeant or above from the injured member’s command. If unavailable, a supervisor from the precinct of occurrence. Out of city requires captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)

25. Regarding the investigating supervisor and the three LOD reports (PAR, AIDED, LODI), which statement is correct?

    1. IS always prepares all three reports
    1. IS always prepares the PAR, never prepares the AIDED, and sometimes prepares the LODI
    1. IS never prepares any of the three reports
    1. IS always prepares the AIDED and the LODI
Answer

B) IS always prepares the PAR, never prepares the AIDED, and sometimes prepares the LODI

The Investigating Supervisor always prepares the Police Accident Report. The IS never prepares the AIDED (directs injured MOS to prepare it, or assigns another member if incapacitated). The IS sometimes prepares the LODI (only when the injured member is incapacitated). (A.G. 330-03)

Section 331 — Evaluations

Source

  • Admin Guide Section: 331
  • Source PDF: Public Admin Guide2.pdf (pages 213-229)
  • Priority: Medium

Overview

Section 331 governs the performance evaluation process for all uniformed and civilian members of the NYPD. It covers six procedures addressing evaluations by rank — from probationary police officers through deputy chiefs — including the annual evaluation cycle, probationary evaluation schedules, the PERF system for non-investigative personnel, and the Management By Objectives (MBO) system for captains and above. Critical for the Sergeant exam: understanding probationary evaluation timelines, Chronic Absent impact on ratings, reviewer rank requirements, and the PERF scoring scale.

Procedures

Procedure Title Pages Status
A.G. 331-01 Evaluations — General — Members of the Service 6 Complete
A.G. 331-02 Evaluation of Probationary Officers 2 Complete
A.G. 331-03 Evaluation of POs/Detectives — Investigative Duties 1 Complete
A.G. 331-04 Evaluations of POs/Detectives — Non-Investigative Duties 4 Complete
A.G. 331-05 Evaluations of Sergeants/Lieutenants 2 Complete
A.G. 331-06 Evaluation of Captain Through Deputy Chief 2 Complete

Priority Study Topics

  1. Probationary evaluation schedules — PO (10/16/22), Sgt/Lt (4/10), Captain (4/10/16), Investigative (8/15)
  2. PERF scoring scale — 90-100 Exceptional, 77-89 Exceeds Expectations, 68-76 Meets Standards, 60-67 Needs Improvement, 59 or below Unsatisfactory
  3. Chronic Absent impact — Category A (consider attendance), Category B (consider Very Low/Unsatisfactory)
  4. Reviewer rank requirements — below competent or extremely competent: captain or above; Performance Monitoring Program: commanding officer
  5. 22nd month recommendation — Permanent Appointment, Extension of Probation, or Termination of Services
  6. MBO system — captains through deputy chiefs, five Key Result Areas (KRAs)
  7. Military absence rule — average of 3 prior evals, minimum satisfactory (3.0)

Section 331 — Evaluation of Probationary Officers

A.G. 331-02

Effective: 05/03/24 | Last Revision: R.O. 24 | Pages: 1-2

Purpose

To ensure proper evaluation of probationary police officers.

Procedure

Before a probationary police officer is permanently assigned to a command:

Commanding Officer, Police Academy

  1. Ensure preparation of two copies of RECRUIT EVALUATION (PD439-1412), prior to permanent assignment.
  2. Forward original to permanent command.
  3. Forward duplicate to Human Resources Division upon assignment to permanent command.

Commanding Officer/Supervisor Concerned (Permanent Command)

  1. Evaluate continuously, the conduct and performance of probationary police officers.
  2. Evaluate probationary police officers via the Online Evaluation System available on the Department’s Intranet, three times during their probationary period: the tenth, sixteenth and twenty-second month.

Exam Alert: Probationary police officers receive three evaluations during their probationary period: at the 10th, 16th, and 22nd month. This differs from probationary sergeants/lieutenants (4th and 10th month) and probationary captains (4th, 10th, and 16th month). Know the distinction for each rank.

  1. Ensure that twenty-second month evaluation specifically recommends Permanent Appointment, Extension of Probation or Termination of Services in reviewer’s comments area.
      1. Notify the Performance Analysis Unit whenever a probationary police officer receives an overall evaluation less than 3.0.

Exam Alert: The 22nd month evaluation MUST include one of three specific recommendations. Also, any evaluation below 3.0 must be reported to the Performance Analysis Unit.

  1. Forward completed PERFORMANCE EVALUATIONS via Department Intranet by finalizing the PERFORMANCE EVALUATION online.
      1. Ensure a signed copy of the PERFORMANCE EVALUATION is printed out and forwarded to Personnel Services Unit.

When recommending Termination of Services or Extension of Probation, a detailed report to Commanding Officer, Performance Analysis Unit, including all pertinent facts related to the probationary police officer’s performance, will be forwarded with a copy of the PERFORMANCE EVALUATION. If warranted, these recommendations may also be forwarded at any time during the probationary period, prior to the twenty-second month anniversary.

Exam Alert: Termination of Services or Extension of Probation recommendations require a detailed report to the Commanding Officer, Performance Analysis Unit — but can be submitted at ANY time during the probationary period, not just the 22nd month.

Additional Data

The probationary period should be utilized to closely scrutinize the member’s performance to assess their abilities as a police officer. The entry level probationary period may be extended an additional six months, if necessary, to allow the Department more time to further review and evaluate a probationer’s suitability to remain a police officer.

When a probationary officer is transferred to a command, their performance from the previous command must be reviewed. Interim evaluations should be prepared when it is apparent there is a problem with an officer’s performance. The rater will discuss the probationary evaluation with the ratee. This will make the probationary officer aware of their performance and also place those probationary officers with a below standards evaluation on notice that their performance must show an improvement.

When a commanding officer becomes aware that a probationary officer’s performance may be below standards, supervisors should be instructed to carefully document the member’s performance by utilizing the SUPERVISOR FEEDBACK FORM, command discipline, and when appropriate, charges and specifications. The probationer will be notified of the observed performance deficiencies and instructed on proper Department procedure. Written documentation that the probationer was instructed and counseled will be maintained in the officer’s personnel folder.

In addition to evaluation reports, commanding officers will submit the following reports during the probationary period as they occur, on Typed Letterhead: - a. Request for Charges and Specifications to First Deputy Commissioner. - b. Report of any matter that may bear upon the fitness of probationer to Chief of Personnel in a sealed envelope.

Exam Alert: The probationary period can be extended by six months. Charges and Specifications go to the First Deputy Commissioner; fitness reports go to the Chief of Personnel in a sealed envelope.

> Sergeant Focus: The first sixteen months of a probationary police officer’s field assignment is a critical phase. You must conscientiously evaluate probationary officers and use the SUPERVISOR FEEDBACK FORM, command discipline, and charges to document below-standards performance. Don’t wait for scheduled evaluation periods if problems arise — submit interim evaluations immediately.

Section 331 — Evaluation of Police Officers and Detectives Assigned to Investigative Duties

A.G. 331-03

Effective: 11/18/21 | Last Revision: I.O. 110 | Pages: 1

Purpose

To ensure that each uniformed member is evaluated each year while assigned to investigative duties.

Procedure — Rater

Prior to the scheduled evaluation of uniformed members of the service:
